unstructured_notes: QA sprint: do not modify game logic, only write tests and flag discrepancies. The Red Gyarados shiny check is the most commonly miscimplemented feature — BATTLETYPE_FORCESHINY must set the shiny DVs directly, not go through the standard shiny probability check. The toll gate direction detection is critical: the script reads the player's facing direction (VAR_FACING) to determine whether they approach from north or south, because both directions trigger the same shakedown. A direction-agnostic implementation would fail. For the Electrode puzzle: Lance takes Electrodes 4-6 (x=22 positions) so the player only needs to faint 3, but all 6 must ultimately be fainted — if Lance's AI fails, the puzzle could deadlock. Verify Lance's electrode-clearing happens automatically as a scripted event, not via actual battle.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

unstructured_notes: Tyrogue evolution: evolves at Lv20 into Hitmonlee (Atk>Def), Hitmonchan (Def>Atk), Hitmontop (Atk=Def). The only Tyrogue in the base game without trading. Kiyo is implemented as an OBJECTTYPE_SCRIPT NPC (not OBJECTTYPE_TRAINER) so he requires direct interaction rather than triggering on line-of-sight. Route 44 Psychic Phil uses SPRITE_YOUNGSTER in the object_const_def despite being trainer class PSYCHIC_T — the sprite and class are different. Fisher Wilton has a rematch sequence giving different ball types (Ultra Ball / Great Ball / Poke Ball) based on ENGINE_WILTON_HAS_ITEM flag. Ice sliding on ice_path_1f: the tile physics cause player to slide until hitting a wall or non-ice tile. This is tile-level physics that must be implemented at the map system level, not just as a map data property. The FISHGROUP_DRATINI fishing group allows Dratini to be caught via fishing in Ice Path waters (when water tiles are accessible on lower floors) but 1F has no water tiles.

unstructured_notes: Ice Path is the only multi-floor dungeon in Johto with NO trainers — all floors have only items and wild encounters. The boulder-hole system on B1F is directional: boulders fall down through holes to B2F Mahogany Side, and once there they are permanently frozen (cannot be pushed back up). The ice physics must be implemented at the tile/map level: a player stepping onto any ice tile slides in the current direction until hitting a non-ice tile or wall. This requires special tile behavior flags in the map engine. Blackthorn City's two-level geography is unique among Johto cities — the gym and Dragon's Den are on a raised cliff separated from the main plaza by a water channel, connected only by a bridge. Santos of Saturday uses SPRITE_YOUNGSTER (purple) and the callback pattern identical to Wesley of Wednesday. Clair's Kingdra is the most challenging opponent in the Johto gym circuit — Water/Dragon typing means only Dragon-type moves are super-effective in Gen 2 (Ice is neutral against Dragon in Gen 2); only Dragon-type attacks deal 2x damage. The Move Deleter in Blackthorn is the only character in the entire game who can delete HM moves — critical for players who taught Cut or Flash to Pokemon they want to keep.

critical_checks: Ice sliding physics must be implemented at tile behavior level — not just a visual effect
Boulder fall mechanism: must trigger earthquake animation + SFX_STRENGTH + text 'The boulder fell through.' — purely visual/audio feedback with warp result
Kiyo is OBJECTTYPE_SCRIPT not OBJECTTYPE_TRAINER — a player walking past him should NOT trigger a battle
Tyrogue gift blocked when party is full — must check PARTY_LENGTH == 6 condition
Clair refuses badge post-battle — this is NOT a bug, it is the intended story progression requiring Dragon's Den
Santos Spell Tag: one-time gift checked by EVENT_GOT_SPELL_TAG_FROM_SANTOS event flag
Kingdra is only weak to Dragon in Gen 2 — Ice-type moves deal neutral (1x) damage due to Gen 2 type chart
source_data.tyrogue_evolution_note: Tyrogue at Lv20: Atk>Def=Hitmonlee, Def>Atk=Hitmonchan, Atk=Def=Hitmontop
source_data.clair_refusal_note: Clair refusing the badge after losing is canonical to Crystal/Gold/Silver. She sends the player to Dragon's Den. Only after passing the Elder's quiz does she give the Rising Badge and TM24 DragonBreath.

unstructured_notes: QA sprint: do not modify game logic, only write tests and flag discrepancies. The ice-sliding physics are the most likely source of engine bugs in this batch — the tile behavior system may not yet support directional momentum physics. If ice sliding is not yet implemented at the engine level, flag it as a blocker for this entire group. The Strength boulder puzzle on B1F has a subtle state management issue: if the player pushes a wrong boulder (onto a non-hole tile), it is now blocking the path and cannot be pushed back. In the original game the boulders can be pushed into non-hole positions permanently — the player must leave the floor and re-enter to reset boulder positions. Verify this reset mechanic (boulder positions reset on map exit/re-entry unless they fell through a hole). Clair's three Dragonair have different coverage moves but all share Thunder Wave as the opener — verify the moveset ordering is correct in the battle data.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

unstructured_notes: The Blackthorn Gym 2F uses the stonetable/cmdqueue mechanism unique to Gen 2 — boulder fall is evaluated on map load, not during push. Both 2F trainers use Water-types despite being in a Dragon gym (lampshaded in dialogue). Dragon's Den B1F is a flooded underground lake requiring both Surf and Whirlpool. The Dragon Fang item uses a hand-written GS event script rather than the standard itemball macro — replicate this faithfully. The quiz cannot be failed outright — wrong answers set a flag and continue. The ExtremeSpeed Dratini is counterintuitively obtained by answering questions WRONG. Clair's TM24 DragonBreath delivery can glitch to give TM twice (known pokecrystal bug — document but replicate the behavior).

unstructured_notes: Route 45 is the longest route in Johto at 90 tiles tall — the engine tile system must handle maps of this height correctly. The central pond has Dratini fishing (Good Rod 10%, Super Rod 30%) and Dragonair (Super Rod 10%). Route 46 Pokemon are all low-level (Lv2-5) because the map was originally early-game only; players descending from Route 45 find it anachronistically easy. Dark Cave has two distinct halves as separate maps connected by internal warps requiring Surf and Waterfall. The Blackthorn side contains Wobbuffet (night-only) and Ursaring/Teddiursa as important encounter differentiators. Dunsparce on the Violet side is one of only two reliable wild Dunsparce locations in the game. Teddiursa is morning-only on the Violet side. The Flash darkness mechanic is visual-only — a dark overlay with a small visibility radius around the player sprite that resets on map re-entry.

unstructured_notes: QA sprints must not modify game logic — only write test cases and flag discrepancies for the next work sprint. Critical failure modes to check: (1) stonetable callback not firing on map reload — boulders must fall on entry to 2F if pushed to hole tiles on a prior visit; (2) Dragon Shrine quiz wrong-answer tolerance incorrectly blocking progress — Elder must proceed regardless of answer; (3) ExtremeSpeed Dratini condition inverted — wrong answers should give ExtremeSpeed, correct answers give standard; (4) Wobbuffet appearing in daytime slots in Blackthorn cave — must be night-only; (5) ledge tiles on Routes 45/46 allowing northward movement; (6) Flash darkness persisting between map entries — effect must reset on re-entry; (7) Rising Badge not being the 8th badge enabling Waterfall HM outside battle.

unstructured_notes: The Whirl Islands are accessed from Route 41 by using Whirlpool (HM06, requires Glacier Badge from Pryce). All four entrances share identical encounter tables with the only variation being night time (Seel slots replaced by Golbat and Krabby). SW is the most strategically important entrance — it has a direct passage to B2F at (17,15) allowing players to skip most of B1F when going to Lugia. NW connects to both SW (via lower passage) and to the interior WhirlIslandCave junction (also via lower passage). NE has two separate staircases to B1F at different entry coordinates. SE is the simplest cave with one staircase and no cross-cave connections. SW uniquely has a surfable water pool with Tentacool/Horsea/Tentacruel. The WhirlIslandCave map (pokecrystal map ID 69) is a small connector between NW and B1F — include it as part of this sprint even though it is listed separately in the area spec.

unstructured_notes: B1F is the largest map in the complex at 40x36 tiles, functioning as the central hub connecting all 4 entrance caves plus both B2F staircases. The Strength Boulder at (23,26) blocks a shortcut path in the lower section. B2F's tileset attribute $2e differs from B1F ($09), giving it a visually distinct appearance within the dark cave tileset. The waterfall at B2F (7,25) requires HM07 Waterfall AND the Rising Badge — the 8th and final badge — making this the last area gated by a badge requirement in the game. Lugia's encounter level is 60 in Crystal (vs 70 in Gold/Silver) — this is version-accurate. BATTLETYPE_FORCEITEM allows item use even in normally restricted contexts. The Silver Wing is obtained from an elderly man in Pewter City (Kanto) after arriving by Magnet Train. EVENT_FOUGHT_LUGIA is permanent — catching or KOing Lugia both set the flag. Seadra on the Lugia Chamber surf table is a unique differentiator from all other Whirl Islands water encounters and must be present.

unstructured_notes: QA sprints must not modify game logic — only write test cases and flag discrepancies. Critical failure modes: (1) Lugia spawning without Silver Wing — MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS must check bag contents; (2) EVENT_FOUGHT_LUGIA not persisting after KO — must prevent re-encounter even after save reload; (3) SW-to-B2F shortcut warp firing incorrectly — passage at (17,15) in SW must target B2F warp 4, not any other warp; (4) B1F Strength Boulder not blocking correctly — verify correct collision tile type; (5) NE cave having two staircases — verify warp count is 3 not 2; (6) Seadra absent from Lugia Chamber surf encounters — must be present at 10% since it is unique to this map; (7) B2F waterfall descent accessible without Rising Badge — must verify badge check is active for HM07 outside battle; (8) Night encounter tables failing to replace Seel with Golbat/Krabby in entrance caves and B1F — time-of-day table swap must be correctly implemented.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

unstructured_notes: Route 27 is one of the widest maps in the game (40x9 blocks). It requires Surf to access the Tohjo Falls cave entrances at tiles (26,5) and (36,5) — the western half of the route is accessible on foot but the waterway crossing requires Surf. The Sandstorm House happiness check uses GetFirstPokemonHappiness with threshold > 150 (strictly greater, so >= 151 in implementation). The Fisher cutscene at tiles (18,10)/(19,10) fires only once (SCENE_ROUTE27_FIRST_STEP_INTO_KANTO). Route 26 is extremely long north-south (10x54 blocks, 108 tiles tall) — the map system must handle this vertical extent. The heal house on Route 26 at (15,57) uses FadeOutToBlack sequence identical to a PokemonCenter heal but without the center UI. Both routes require all 8 Johto badges to proceed north through the victory_road_gate.

goal: Build Victory Road — single large cave map (10x36 blocks, 20x72 tiles) with internal warp maze, Rival Battle 5, and high-level wild encounters
technical_debt_notes: This area does not exist in the current engine and is being built fresh. Verify that the map system supports the tile dimensions and features of this new area.
abstract_tasks: Implement victory_road map (10x36 blocks, 20x72 tiles, tileset: cave) with no external connections; southern exit warp at (9,67) to victory_road_gate target_warp_id 5 (enters from Route 26 direction); northern exit warp at (13,5) to route_23 target_warp_id 3 (leads to Indigo Plateau)

Implement all 9 internal passage warps that create the maze-like multi-level cave illusion within a single map: warp_id 2 at (1,49) <-> warp_id 3 at (1,35); warp_id 4 at (13,31) <-> warp_id 5 at (13,17); warp_id 6 at (17,33) <-> warp_id 7 at (17,19); warp_id 8 at (0,11) <-> warp_id 9 at (0,27); each pair links bidirectionally within the same map

Place 5 visible item balls: TM26 Earthquake at (3,28) with flag EVENT_VICTORY_ROAD_TM_EARTHQUAKE, Max Revive at (12,48) with flag EVENT_VICTORY_ROAD_MAX_REVIVE, Full Restore at (18,29) with flag EVENT_VICTORY_ROAD_FULL_RESTORE, Full Heal at (15,48) with flag EVENT_VICTORY_ROAD_FULL_HEAL, HP Up at (7,38) with flag EVENT_VICTORY_ROAD_HP_UP

Place 2 hidden items: Max Potion at (3,29) hidden bg_event with flag EVENT_VICTORY_ROAD_HIDDEN_MAX_POTION; Full Heal at (3,65) hidden bg_event with flag EVENT_VICTORY_ROAD_HIDDEN_FULL_HEAL (near south entrance)

Implement Rival (Silver) encounter: Rival NPC stands at (18,13) facing up, does NOT activate on line-of-sight; coord_events at (12,8) and (13,8) trigger SCENE_VICTORYROAD_RIVAL_BATTLE if not yet cleared; Rival walks toward player via scripted movement (approach from right on tile 12,8; approach from below on tile 13,8); after battle SCENE_VICTORYROAD_NOOP is set to prevent retriggering

Rival team selection based on player's starting Pokemon: if player chose Cyndaquil, Rival has Typhlosion Lv38/Sneasel Lv34/Golbat Lv36/Magneton Lv35/Haunter Lv35/Kadabra Lv35; if Chikorita, Rival has Meganium Lv38/same supporting team; if Totodile, Rival has Feraligatr Lv38 and Magneton is Lv34; loadtrainer uses RIVAL1_13/RIVAL1_14/RIVAL1_15 constants

Implement wild encounters (cave walking, encounter_rate 6): morning/day slots: Graveler Lv34, Rhyhorn Lv32, Onix Lv33, Golbat Lv34, Sandslash Lv35, Rhydon Lv35 (x2); night slots: Golbat Lv34, Graveler Lv34, Onix Lv32, Graveler Lv36, Graveler Lv38, Graveler Lv40 (x2); no water encounters (no Surf tiles in Victory Road)

Implement Rival pre-battle dialogue: 'Hold it. ...Are you going to take the #MON LEAGUE challenge? ...Don't make me laugh. You are so much weaker than I am. I am not like I was before. I now have the best and strongest #MON with me. I am invincible! <PLAYER>! I challenge you!'; post-loss dialogue about beginning to understand what the dragon master said; post-win dialogue about continuing to train and challenging player again when stronger

Tile the cave map row-by-row (20 wide x 72 tall) as a winding vertical corridor: the path enters at the south bottom (rows 67-71), winds through the cave via C passage warps, and exits at the top (rows 0-5); no water tiles anywhere in the map; Rival stands at (18,13) which is near the top section of the cave
Ensure no additional trainers besides the Rival appear in Victory Road; no NPCs; no signs; this is a pure dungeon navigation and climactic Rival encounter area

unstructured_notes: Victory Road is the climactic pre-League dungeon. The internal warp system (9 warps linking within the same map) simulates different elevation levels and winding tunnels without a multi-floor structure — the entire cave is a single map. All 9 internal warps are bidirectional pairs within victory_road. The Rival encounter at coord tiles (12,8) and (13,8) is the fifth and final Rival battle of the main story. His team at level 38 (fully evolved starter) is the strongest seen so far. TM26 Earthquake is the highest-value item — a 100-power Ground-type move ideal for the E4. Rhydon (Lv35) appears only during morning/day — night encounters replace Rock/Ground types with Graveler waves. No Strength boulders appear in Crystal's Victory Road (unlike Gen 1). No water exists anywhere in this cave.

goal: QA audit of sprints 49-50 — verify Victory Road Approach and Victory Road traversal, all-8-badge gate, Rival Battle 5, items, encounters
abstract_tasks: Verify all-8-badges gate check on the north end of Route 26: confirm the victory_road_gate NPC blocks passage until all 8 Johto badges are set in the player's badge flags; test with 7 badges (should block) and 8 badges (should pass)

Verify Route 27 traversal from New Bark Town (west) to Route 26 (east): all six trainers battleable, Rare Candy and TM37 Sandstorm items pickable, Fisher NPC cutscene fires once at tiles (18,10)/(19,10) and does not fire again on return visit

Verify Sandstorm House happiness gate: enter with lead Pokemon happiness <= 150 (should refuse TM with trust dialogue), then enter with happiness >= 151 (should give TM37 Sandstorm, flag EVENT_GOT_TM37_SANDSTORM set, second entry gives post-receipt description dialogue)

Verify Tohjo Falls: Surf required to enter water pool (attempt without Surf should be blocked), Waterfall required to ascend (attempt without Waterfall HM should be blocked), Moon Stone item accessible, wild Zubat/Slowpoke/Golbat encounters fire, two exit warps return to correct Route 27 tiles

Verify Route 26 full north-south traversal: all six trainers battleable, Max Elixir item pickable, Fruit Tree accessible, Heal House at (15,57) provides free full party heal, heal jingle plays, Teacher NPC dialogue matches expected text

Verify Victory Road all 9 internal warp pairs link correctly: walk to each warp tile and confirm the bidirectional connection is correct; test full traversal from south entrance (9,67) through the cave to north exit (13,5)

Verify Rival Battle 5 in Victory Road: stepping on tile (12,8) triggers Rival approach from right; stepping on (13,8) triggers approach from below; battle fires with correct team for player's starter choice (Typhlosion/Meganium/Feraligatr path); after battle SCENE_VICTORYROAD_NOOP prevents retriggering

Verify all 5 visible items in Victory Road (TM26 Earthquake, Max Revive, Full Restore, Full Heal, HP Up) are collectible; verify both hidden items (Max Potion at 3,29 and Full Heal at 3,65) trigger from bg_event facing-tile interactions

Verify wild encounters in Victory Road: Graveler/Rhyhorn/Onix/Golbat/Sandslash/Rhydon appear during morning and day; Golbat/Graveler/Onix appear at night with no Rhydon or Sandslash; confirm no water encounters exist anywhere in this map

Test end-to-end journey from New Bark Town (Route 27 west entrance) through Tohjo Falls, along Route 26, through Victory Road to Route 23 north exit — verify all connections and warp targets resolve without error

source_data.qa_focus_group_49_50: Group 49 areas: route_27 (80x18 tiles, 6 trainers, 2 items, coord events, 3 warps), route_27_sandstorm_house (8x8 tiles, Granny NPC happiness gate TM37), tohjo_falls (30x18 tiles, Moon Stone, Surf+Waterfall required, cave encounters), route_26 (20x108 tiles, 6 trainers, Max Elixir, Fruit Tree, 3 warps), route_26_heal_house (8x8 tiles, Teacher healer NPC). Group 50 areas: victory_road (20x72 tiles, 9 internal warps + 2 external warps, Rival Battle 5 with 3 starter-dependent teams, 5 visible items + 2 hidden items, cave encounters with Rhydon/Graveler/Rhyhorn/Onix/Golbat/Sandslash).
source_data.badge_gate_check: All 8 Johto badges required: Zephyr (Falkner), Hive (Bugsy), Plain (Whitney), Fog (Morty), Storm (Chuck), Mineral (Jasmine), Glacier (Pryce), Rising (Clair). Gate guard at Route 26 north end / victory_road_gate blocks if any badge flag is missing.
source_data.rival_teams: RIVAL1_13 (player chose Chikorita, Rival has Meganium Lv38): Sneasel34/Golbat36/Magneton35/Haunter35/Kadabra35/Meganium38. RIVAL1_14 (player chose Totodile, Rival has Feraligatr): Sneasel34/Golbat36/Magneton34/Haunter35/Kadabra35/Feraligatr38. RIVAL1_15 (player chose Cyndaquil, Rival has Typhlosion): Sneasel34/Golbat36/Magneton35/Haunter35/Kadabra35/Typhlosion38.

unstructured_notes: QA focus: the 8-badge gate is the most critical check in this group — a bug here would allow premature access to the Pokemon League. The Sandstorm House happiness check uses strict greater-than (> 150, i.e., >= 151) not >=150 — verify the boundary case at exactly 150 is rejected and 151 is accepted. The internal warp maze in Victory Road is complex — each of the 9 warp pairs must be tested individually to confirm they do not cross-link incorrectly. The Rival team selection depends on a constant index (RIVAL1_13/14/15) tied to the player's starter choice event flag set at game start — verify all three paths. Rhydon at level 35 is one of the highest-level wild Pokemon available before the E4 — the QA should confirm it appears during day but not at night.
technical_debt_notes: QA sprint — verify that migration/creation changes from the previous two content sprints correctly reconcile old map data with new canonical area specifications. Test that warps, connections, NPCs, and collision data match the new JSON files. Flag any remaining discrepancies for the next content sprint.

goal: Build Indigo Plateau Pokemon Center and Elite Four rooms 1-3 — indigo_plateau_pokecenter, wills_room, kogas_room, brunos_room
technical_debt_notes: This area does not exist in the current engine and is being built fresh. Verify that the map system supports the tile dimensions and features of this new area.
abstract_tasks: Implement indigo_plateau_pokecenter map (9x7 blocks, 18x14 tiles, tileset: pokecenter, music: MUSIC_POKEMON_CENTER) with four warps: left exit at (5,13) to route_23 warp_id 1, right exit at (6,13) to route_23 warp_id 2, Cable Club warp at (0,13) to pokecenter_2f warp_id 1, E4 entrance door at (14,3) to wills_room warp_id 1

Place all NPCs in indigo_plateau_pokecenter: Nurse Joy at (3,7) counter facing down with PokecenterNurseScript heal; Pokemart Clerk at (11,7) with MART_INDIGO_PLATEAU inventory (Ultra Ball 1200, Max Repel 700, Hyper Potion 1200, Max Potion 2500, Full Restore 3000, Revive 1500, Full Heal 600); CooltrainerM at (11,11) walk_around radius 2 (explains E4 consecutive rule); Gramps at (1,9) facing down with Abra at (0,9) — EVENT_TELEPORT_GUY flag controls both (disappear after Hall of Fame); Rival (Silver) at (16,9) appears Monday/Wednesday only if EVENT_BEAT_RIVAL_IN_MT_MOON set and ENGINE_INDIGO_PLATEAU_RIVAL_FIGHT not set

Implement IndigoPlateauPokecenter1FPrepareElite4Callback: fires every map entry, resets all E4 room scene flags to LOCK_DOOR, clears all EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_4_* flags, clears all EXIT_OPEN and ENTRANCE_CLOSED events, sets EVENT_LANCES_ROOM_OAK_AND_MARY; this ensures a clean E4 run on every attempt

Implement coord events at (16,4) and (17,4) for Rival battle: day-of-week check (Monday/Wednesday only), condition EVENT_BEAT_RIVAL_IN_MT_MOON set and ENGINE_INDIGO_PLATEAU_RIVAL_FIGHT not set; after battle sets ENGINE_INDIGO_PLATEAU_RIVAL_FIGHT permanently; Rival uses RIVAL2 party variant (RIVAL2_2_TOTODILE/CHIKORITA/CYNDAQUIL) at prize_money 2500

Implement wills_room map (5x9 blocks, 10x18 tiles, tileset: elite_four_room, music: MUSIC_GYM) with three warps: south entrance at (5,17) back to indigo_plateau_pokecenter warp_id 4; north exit left at (4,2) to kogas_room warp_id 1; north exit right at (5,2) to kogas_room warp_id 2; north exits start as wall tile ($2a block), open after Will defeated

Implement wills_room door mechanics: on map entry SCENE_WILLSROOM_LOCK_DOOR fires — player walks UP 4 steps automatically, earthquake sound plays, south entrance tile at (4,14) changes to wall block ($2a), EVENT_WILLS_ROOM_ENTRANCE_CLOSED set, scene changes to SCENE_WILLSROOM_NOOP; mapcallback_tiles enforces door states on every tile refresh

Place Will trainer at (5,7) facing down, talk-triggered, SPRITE_WILL PAL_NPC_RED; team: Xatu Lv40 (Quick Attack/Future Sight/Confuse Ray/Psychic), Jynx Lv41 (DoubleSlap/Lovely Kiss/Ice Punch/Psychic), Exeggutor Lv41 (Reflect/Leech Seed/Egg Bomb/Psychic), Slowbro Lv41 (Curse/Amnesia/Body Slam/Psychic), Xatu Lv42 (Quick Attack/Future Sight/Confuse Ray/Psychic); prize_money 4200; ai_items [Max Potion]; one_time_flag EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_4_WILL; after defeat north door opens (EVENT_WILLS_ROOM_EXIT_OPEN)

Implement kogas_room map (5x9 blocks, 10x18 tiles, tileset: elite_four_room, music: MUSIC_GYM) with four warps: south entrance left at (4,17) to wills_room warp_id 2; south entrance right at (5,17) to wills_room warp_id 3; north exit left at (4,2) to brunos_room warp_id 1; north exit right at (5,2) to brunos_room warp_id 2; same door locking mechanics as wills_room (SCENE_KOGASROOM_LOCK_DOOR, SCENE_KOGASROOM_NOOP, KogasRoomDoorsCallback)

Place Koga trainer at (5,7) facing down, SPRITE_KOGA PAL_NPC_BLUE; team: Ariados Lv40 (Double Team/Spider Web/Baton Pass/Giga Drain), Venomoth Lv41 (Supersonic/Gust/Psychic/Toxic), Forretress Lv43 (Protect/Swift/Explosion/Spikes), Muk Lv42 (Minimize/Acid Armor/Sludge Bomb/Toxic), Crobat Lv44 (Double Team/Quick Attack/Wing Attack/Toxic); prize_money 4400; ai_items [Full Heal, Full Restore]; one_time_flag EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_4_KOGA; Koga was Fuchsia Gym Leader in Gen 1 — daughter Janine replaced him

Implement brunos_room map (5x9 blocks, 10x18 tiles, tileset: elite_four_room, music: MUSIC_GYM) with four warps: south entrance left at (4,17) to kogas_room warp_id 3; south entrance right at (5,17) to kogas_room warp_id 4; north exit left at (4,2) to karens_room warp_id 1; north exit right at (5,2) to karens_room warp_id 2; same door locking mechanics (SCENE_BRUNOSROOM_LOCK_DOOR, SCENE_BRUNOSROOM_NOOP, BrunosRoomDoorsCallback)

Place Bruno trainer at (5,7) facing down, SPRITE_BRUNO PAL_NPC_BROWN; team: Hitmontop Lv42 (Pursuit/Quick Attack/Dig/Detect), Hitmonlee Lv42 (Swagger/Double Kick/Hi Jump Kick/Foresight), Hitmonchan Lv42 (ThunderPunch/Ice Punch/Fire Punch/Mach Punch), Onix Lv43 (Bind/Earthquake/Sandstorm/Rock Slide), Machamp Lv46 (Rock Slide/Foresight/Vital Throw/Cross Chop); prize_money 4600; ai_items [Max Potion]; one_time_flag EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_4_BRUNO; Bruno returning from Gen 1 Elite Four

unstructured_notes: The Indigo Plateau Pokecenter's PrepareElite4Callback is the most critical implementation detail: every time the player re-enters, all E4 flags reset so the gauntlet must be run fresh from Will. This means players cannot defeat Will, heal, then fight Koga — they must defeat all four consecutively. The E4 room locking mechanic (south door seals behind player on entry, north door opens after trainer defeat) is shared across all four E4 rooms. Each room's door state is persisted via mapcallback_tiles which enforces the block changes on every tile redraw. Will's two Xatu (one at Lv40, one at Lv42) represent the same species appearing twice in a trainer's party — the battle system must handle this correctly. Koga's Ariados uses Spider Web to trap the player's Pokemon and Baton Pass to pass Double Team boosts — this is a Gen 2-era stall strategy. Bruno's Hitmonchan has elemental punches covering three types (Thunder/Ice/Fire) — the broadest single-Pokemon type coverage in the E4.

unstructured_notes: Lance's room is the only E4 room with a different map size (5x12 blocks vs 5x9 for the other three) and different music (MUSIC_BEAUTY_ENCOUNTER ambient, not MUSIC_GYM). His team has three Dragonite — all technically underleveled (Dragonite normally evolves at Lv55 from Dragonair) which is a canonical detail to preserve. His Gyarados leads to set up Rain Dance for Thunder's 100% accuracy in rain. The post-Lance victory cutscene involves four distinct NPC movements (Mary rush-in, Oak slow walk-in) and a FadeOutToWhite warpfacing sequence — this is the most complex cutscene in the game. The Hall of Fame machine uses the HEALMACHINE animation but records data to SRAM rather than healing. EVENT_RED_IN_MT_SILVER being CLEARED (not set) enables Red — the flag name is inverted (it blocks Red when set). The SS Ticket phone call from Elm is the post-game gate for Kanto. Karen's Gengar (Lv45) with Destiny Bond is the most dangerous threat in the E4 — if the player KOs it, they also lose one of their own Pokemon.

goal: Final QA sprint for world-building — verify complete game traversal from New Bark Town through Elite Four. All 152 areas should be fully implemented with correct tile layouts, collision, warps, connections, NPCs, trainers, and encounter tables matching canonical area specifications. Test end-to-end playability.

Verify E4 reset callback: enter Indigo Plateau Pokecenter after partial E4 run (e.g. defeated Will and Koga), confirm all E4 scene flags reset to LOCK_DOOR and EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_4_WILL + EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_4_KOGA are cleared — the gauntlet must restart from Will

Weak Pokemon. That is only the selfish perception of people.'

Verify Lance's Room: room is taller (10x24 tiles vs 10x18); door locks, player auto-walks UP 4 steps; SCENE_LANCESROOM_APPROACH_LANCE triggers Lance approach coord event at (4,5)/(5,5); music fades before Lance speaks; Lance battle fires with 6-Pokemon team (Gyarados44/Dragonite47/Dragonite47/Aerodactyl46/Charizard46/Dragonite50); Lance ai uses Full Heal and Full Restore; verify Rain Dance + Thunder accuracy interaction (Thunder should have 100% accuracy in rain); verify Outrage on Lv50 Dragonite is the signature finishing move

Verify E4 room-to-room transitions have no skipping: confirm it is impossible to reach Koga's Room without defeating Will, Bruno's Room without Koga, Karen's Room without Bruno, Lance's Room without Karen — each room's mapcallback_tiles must enforce the closed south door blocking re-entry and the locked north door blocking advancement before trainer defeat

Run complete end-to-end traversal simulation: start from New Bark Town, travel Route 29/30/31 to Violet City (gym 1 badge), continue through all 8 Johto gyms in order, reach Route 27 with all 8 badges, traverse Tohjo Falls and Route 26, complete Victory Road (Rival Battle 5), enter Indigo Plateau Pokecenter, defeat all 5 Elite Four + Champion consecutively without healing between rooms, verify Hall of Fame sequence completes and post-game state is correct; this is the canonical end-to-end playtest of all 152 world areas

source_data.e4_door_mechanics: Standard E4 room locking: (1) On map entry scene_lock_door fires via sdefer — player auto-walks UP 4 steps, earthquake sound, south door tile changes to wall block ($2a for E4 rooms 1-3, $34 for Lance's room), ENTRANCE_CLOSED flag set, scene changes to NOOP. (2) On trainer defeat — EXIT_OPEN flag set, north door tiles change to open block ($16 for E4 rooms 1-4, $0b for Lance's room). (3) mapcallback_tiles on every tile refresh enforces block states from flags — this prevents door states from being lost on map reload. Entry south warps lead BACK to previous room (not to Pokecenter), forcing forward-only progression once started.
source_data.post_game_state: After Hall of Fame: EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_FOUR permanently set. EVENT_RED_IN_MT_SILVER cleared (Red spawns at Mt. Silver summit after all 16 gyms completed). EVENT_TELEPORT_GUY set (Gramps + Abra disappear from Indigo Plateau Pokecenter). Elm calls offering SS Ticket if not already obtained. S.S. Aqua becomes available from Olivine Port, connecting to Vermilion City (Kanto). All 8 Kanto gyms become accessible. Red at Mt. Silver is the true final boss of the game.
source_data.152_area_checklist: All 152 area IDs in build sequence order (1=new_bark_town through 152=hall_of_fame) should be verified as fully implemented. Key milestone areas: 1-4 New Bark Town block, 5-7 Route 29+Cherrygrove, 8-9 Route 30+Mr Pokemon, 10-12 Route 31+Violet, 13-17 Violet interiors+Sprout Tower, 18-22 Route 32+Ruins hub, 23-27 Ruins chambers, 28-31 Union Cave+Route 33, 32-37 Azalea+Slowpoke Well, 38-42 Ilex Forest+Route 34, 43-54 Goldenrod full block, 55-63 Routes 35-37+National Park, 64-68 Ecruteak, 69-70 Burned Tower, 71-80 Tin Tower all floors, 81-85 Routes 38-39+Moo Moo Farm, 86-95 Olivine+Lighthouse, 96-100 Sea Routes+Cianwood, 101-104 Route 42+Mahogany, 105-108 Route 43+Lake of Rage, 109-111 Rocket Hideout, 112-115 Mt Mortar, 116-121 Route 44+Ice Path, 122-124 Blackthorn+Gym 1F, 125-127 Blackthorn Gym 2F+Dragons Den, 128-131 Routes 45-46+Dark Cave, 132-139 Whirl Islands all 8, 140-144 Routes 27+26 approach, 145 Victory Road, 146 Indigo Plateau Pokecenter, 147-149 Will+Koga+Bruno, 150-151 Karen+Lance, 152 Hall of Fame.

unstructured_notes: This is the final world-building QA sprint. The critical tests are: (1) E4 reset callback correctness — this is the most bug-prone area since it must clear exactly the right set of flags on every Pokecenter entry; (2) Destiny Bond timing — Karen's Gengar must trigger Destiny Bond correctly; (3) Lance's Rain Dance + Thunder interaction — Thunder should have 100% accuracy while Rain Dance is in effect; (4) The Hall of Fame SRAM write must not corrupt adjacent save data; (5) The post-game flag EVENT_RED_IN_MT_SILVER being CLEARED (not set) to enable Red is counterintuitive and easy to implement inverted. The end-to-end traversal simulation is the ultimate test — the game engine must support a full uninterrupted playthrough from New Bark Town to the Hall of Fame without crashes, softlocks, or incorrect progression gate states. Team restoration between E4 members must be impossible (no warp back to Pokecenter from inside E4 rooms). All 152 world areas must be implemented and accessible.
technical_debt_notes: Final QA sprint for world-building. Verify complete game traversal from New Bark Town through Elite Four. All 152 areas should be fully implemented with correct tile layouts, collision, warps, connections, NPCs, trainers, and encounter tables matching canonical area specifications.

goal: Define all 17 types and the full 17x17 type effectiveness chart with immunities and special mechanics (Foresight ghost bypass, sandstorm typing)
abstract_tasks: Define an enum or constant set for all 17 types: Normal, Fire, Water, Electric, Grass, Ice, Fighting, Poison, Ground, Flying, Psychic, Bug, Rock, Ghost, Dragon, Dark, Steel
Build a 17x17 type effectiveness lookup table using scaled integers (0=immune, 5=0.5x, 10=1x, 20=2x) matching pokecrystal constants
Encode all 5 immunities: Normal/Fighting cannot hit Ghost, Electric cannot hit Ground, Poison cannot hit Steel, Ground cannot hit Flying, Psychic cannot hit Dark
Implement Foresight/Odor Sleuth flag that removes Ghost immunity to Normal and Fighting for the identified target
Implement sandstorm typing exemption: Rock, Ground, and Steel types are immune to sandstorm residual damage; all others take 1/8 max HP per turn
Add type-boosting held item associations for all 17 types, noting the Dragon Fang/Dragon Scale bug (Dragon Scale boosts Dragon, not Dragon Fang)
Write unit tests that verify every cell of the 17x17 table against expected values

## Immunities
- Normal and Fighting cannot hit Ghost
- Electric cannot hit Ground
- Poison cannot hit Steel
- Ground cannot hit Flying
- Psychic cannot hit Dark

## Special Mechanics
- Foresight/Odor Sleuth: Removes Ghost-type immunities to Normal and Fighting
- Sandstorm: Damages all types except Rock, Ground, and Steel for 1/8 max HP per turn

## Type-Boosting Held Items Bug
Dragon Fang does NOT boost Dragon-type moves. Dragon Scale does (which also evolves Seadra). Known bug in Pokemon Crystal.
source_data.game_constants.md:
### Type Effectiveness (scaled by 10)
- SUPER_EFFECTIVE = 20 (2x)
- MORE_EFFECTIVE = 15 (1.5x -- not used in Gen 2 type chart)
- EFFECTIVE = 10 (1x, neutral)
- NOT_VERY_EFFECTIVE = 5 (0.5x)
- NO_EFFECT = 0 (immune)

### Type Constants (type_constants.asm)
18 types in Crystal's type system:
- Physical: NORMAL, FIGHTING, FLYING, POISON, GROUND, ROCK, BUG, GHOST, STEEL
- Special: FIRE, WATER, GRASS, ELECTRIC, PSYCHIC, ICE, DRAGON, DARK
- CURSE_T (???) type for Curse move

unstructured_notes: The type chart is stored internally using scaled integers (multiplied by 10) so 2x = 20, 0.5x = 5, immune = 0. The physical/special split in Gen 2 is determined by TYPE (not the move category as in later gens): Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel = Physical; Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark = Special. This is critical for the damage formula. The CURSE_T (???) type is used internally for the Curse move but should not appear in the type chart. Ghost attacking Normal is 1x (not immune) — the immunity runs from Normal/Fighting ATTACKING Ghost, not the reverse. Foresight only removes the Ghost TYPE IMMUNITY, not the 0.5x resistances. Dragon Fang held item bug: held item HELD_DRAGON_BOOST is associated with Dragon Scale in the ASM, not Dragon Fang — must replicate this bug for accuracy. Sandstorm Rock/Ground/Steel immunity applies to the DEFENDING Pokemon's types, not the move type.

source_data.game_constants.md:
### Level Limits
- MAX_LEVEL = 100
- MIN_LEVEL = 2
- EGG_LEVEL = 5

### Stats
- NUM_MOVES = 4
- BASE_STAT_LEVEL = 7 (default stat stage, +0)
- MAX_STAT_LEVEL = 13 (maximum stat stage, +6)
- MAX_STAT_VALUE = 999
- NUM_STATS = 5
- STAT_MIN_NORMAL = 5
- STAT_MIN_HP = 10

### Sleep Turns
- REST_SLEEP_TURNS = 2
- TREEMON_SLEEP_TURNS = 7

### Move Priority
- BASE_PRIORITY = 1

### Shiny DVs
- ATKDEFDV_SHINY = $EA (Atk=14, Def=10)
- SPDSPCDV_SHINY = $AA (Spd=10, Spc=10)
- Shiny requires: Atk DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}, others = 10

### Battle Types (wBattleType)
0=BATTLETYPE_NORMAL, 1=BATTLETYPE_CANLOSE, 2=BATTLETYPE_DEBUG, 3=BATTLETYPE_TUTORIAL, 4=BATTLETYPE_FISH, 5=BATTLETYPE_ROAMING, 6=BATTLETYPE_CONTEST, 7=BATTLETYPE_FORCESHINY, 8=BATTLETYPE_TREE, 9=BATTLETYPE_TRAP, 10=BATTLETYPE_FORCEITEM, 11=BATTLETYPE_CELEBI, 12=BATTLETYPE_SUICUNE

### Status Conditions (wBattleMonStatus)
- Bits 0-2: SLP_MASK (sleep turns)
- Bit 3: PSN, Bit 4: BRN, Bit 5: FRZ, Bit 6: PAR

### Sub-Status Bits (see full tables in source)
SubStatus1: nightmare(0), curse(1), protect(2), identified(3), perish(4), endure(5), rollout(6), in_love(7)
SubStatus2: curled(0)
SubStatus3: bide(0), rampage(1), in_loop(2), flinched(3), charged(4), underground(5), flying(6), confused(7)
SubStatus4: x_accuracy(0), mist(1), focus_energy(2), substitute(4), recharge(5), rage(6), leech_seed(7)
SubStatus5: toxic(0), transformed(3), encored(4), lock_on(5), destiny_bond(6), cant_run(7)

### Screen Flags
SCREENS_SPIKES(0), SCREENS_SAFEGUARD(2), SCREENS_LIGHT_SCREEN(3), SCREENS_REFLECT(4)

### Weather Constants
0=WEATHER_NONE, 1=WEATHER_RAIN, 2=WEATHER_SUN, 3=WEATHER_SANDSTORM, 4=WEATHER_RAIN_END, 5=WEATHER_SUN_END, 6=WEATHER_SANDSTORM_END

### Move Struct (7 bytes)
Offset 0=MOVE_ANIM, 1=MOVE_EFFECT, 2=MOVE_POWER, 3=MOVE_TYPE, 4=MOVE_ACC, 5=MOVE_PP, 6=MOVE_CHANCE

### Party/Box Struct Sizes
PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=48, BOXMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=32, PARTY_LENGTH=6, MONS_PER_BOX=20

### Caught Data Packing
CAUGHT_TIME_MASK=%11000000, CAUGHT_LEVEL_MASK=%00111111, CAUGHT_GENDER_MASK=%10000000, CAUGHT_LOCATION_MASK=%01111111

### Growth Rates
6 rates: FAST, MEDIUM_FAST, MEDIUM_SLOW, SLOW, plus SLIGHTLY_FAST and SLIGHTLY_SLOW (unused)

### Trainer Classes
67 trainer classes from FALKNER (#1) through MYSTICALMAN (#67)

unstructured_notes: DVs in Gen 2 are 4-bit values (0-15) stored in two bytes: one byte for Atk/Def (high nybble/low nybble), one for Spd/Spc. HP DV is derived from the low bits of all four DVs. Gender is determined from Attack DV: species-specific thresholds define male/female cutoff. Shiny check: Attack DV must be in the set {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15} AND Def/Spd/Spc must all equal 10. The MOVE_TYPE byte encodes both type (upper bits) and physical/special classification. Move priority values in pokecrystal: Protect/Detect/Endure=3, Quick Attack/Mach Punch/ExtremeSpeed=2, normal moves=1, Counter/Mirror Coat/Roar/Whirlwind=0. The stat stage system runs from index 1 (MIN_STAT_LEVEL) to 13 (MAX_STAT_LEVEL) with 7 as neutral; this means the Rust code should use 1-indexed arrays or offset by 1. MONS_PER_BOX=20 and 14 boxes gives 280 max stored Pokemon.

- MORE_EFFECTIVE = 15 (1.5x -- not used in Gen 2)

source_data.implementation_gotchas.md:
### Gotcha #9: Pre-move check order is strict
CheckTurn evaluates conditions in this exact order: CANNOT_MOVE, Recharge, Sleep, Freeze, Flinch, Disable, Confusion, Attract, Disabled move check, Paralysis.

### Gotcha #6: Damage formula minimum is 1, not 0
After all modifiers, if damage is 0, it is set to 1. The only exception is type immunity which explicitly sets damage to 0.

unstructured_notes: QA sprints must not modify game logic — only write test cases and flag discrepancies for the next work sprint. The critical failure modes to look for: (1) Ghost immunity incorrectly bidirectional (Ghost vs Normal should be 1x, not immune); (2) Foresight only removes ONE specific immunity pair (Normal/Fighting vs Ghost), not all immunities; (3) MORE_EFFECTIVE=15 appears in the constants but is NOT used in Gen 2 — any code path that produces 15 is a bug; (4) Sub-status bit 3 of SubStatus4 (value 8) is unassigned — a bug that accidentally sets this bit would corrupt nearby flag reads. QA should also verify that the Rust enum for types matches the order expected by the type chart lookup (the chart is indexed by type ordinal, so the enum variant ordering must match the table row/column ordering exactly).

unstructured_notes: Pokemon #125 is Electabuzz (the last in the first half split for this sprint). The split at 125 is arbitrary for sprint sizing — the actual pokecrystal file stores all 251 in sequence. Single-type Pokemon store the same type in both type1 and type2 slots (e.g. Charmander has type1=Fire, type2=Fire). This must match exactly since the damage formula checks both types for STAB and both defending types for effectiveness. Gender ratio is encoded as a single byte where 0=all male, 254=all female, 255=no gender. The actual female probability is: if roll < gender_ratio then female. So a value of 31 means 31/256 ~= 12.1% female (close to the documented 12.5% — rounding is intentional). Medium-Slow growth rate at level 1 produces negative experience due to the polynomial formula; do NOT use this as a validation error. Hatch cycles in game are in 256-step increments; the egg step counter increments once per 256 steps, and hatches when counter reaches hatch_cycles.

unstructured_notes: The Gen 2 Pokemon span #152-#251. Note that in the internal ordering used by pokecrystal, Pokemon are NOT stored in Pokedex order — the internal IDs differ from the display IDs. The code must maintain both a Pokedex number and an internal index. Baby Pokemon (Pichu, Cleffa, Igglybuff, Togepi, Smoochum, Elekid, Magby, Tyrogue) are included in the 251 and have their own entries. Unown has only one entry in the base stats table even though there are 26 forms — all forms share the same base stats. Ditto special-cases the Ditto egg group which allows it to breed with almost any Pokemon. MissingNo does NOT exist in Crystal (unlike Gen 1) — there are exactly 251 valid species. Celebi (#251) has perfectly symmetrical 100/100/100/100/100/100 stats.

Blissey's HP of 255 combined with high catch rate is the species most likely to trigger the catch formula overflow bug (gotcha #7: 3*MaxHP overflows for HP>341 at high levels). Unown having Hidden Power as its signature move depends on the DV-to-type formula (sprint 71) but the base stat record itself is simple. The species count check is a critical integration test: any accidental duplication or gap would corrupt all indexed lookups.

## Move Priority
| Effect | Priority | Moves |
|--------|----------|-------|
| Protect/Detect | 3 | Protect, Detect |
| Endure | 3 | Endure |
| Priority Hit | 2 | Quick Attack, Mach Punch, ExtremeSpeed |

(No Move), Metronome, Struggle, Sketch, Mimic, Counter, Mirror Coat, Protect, Detect, Endure, Destiny Bond, Sleep Talk, Thief

unstructured_notes: Move accuracy is stored as a byte value 0-255 where 255 = guaranteed hit (not 100%). The accuracy check formula: if random(0-255) >= accuracy_value then miss. So Thunder (70% accurate) stores 178 (~70% of 255), NOT 70. However, some sources document it as a percentage — always convert to the 0-255 scale when implementing. Effect ID 0 means 'deal damage, nothing else.' The effect_chance byte for secondary effects like burn/freeze/para is also 0-255 scale where 255=always triggers. A 10% chance stores roughly 26 (25.5 rounded). Struggle (#165) is a special case — it has no PP and is used when all PP is exhausted; its effect causes 25% recoil. Moves that always hit (Swift, Aerial Ace are NOT in Gen 2, but Mind Reader/Lock-On sets the guaranteed-hit flag for the NEXT turn) should have accuracy=255 in the struct.

unstructured_notes: The HP thresholds for Flail/Reversal in pokecrystal are stored in PIXEL counts (of the 48-pixel HP bar), not percentages. The game draws the HP bar and then checks pixel count, which is why the thresholds are in pixels. Implementing as percentages is acceptable for simulation but must produce identical breakpoints. Present healing the OPPONENT (not the user) on the 20% outcome is a notorious gotcha — it heals the enemy, not the user. This is faithful to the original. ExtremeSpeed has priority 2 (same as Quick Attack) and is NOT a Metronome-choosable move — but actually ExtremeSpeed CAN be chosen by Metronome in Gen 2 (only the 12 listed moves are excluded). Beat Up hits once per healthy party member — if 4 party members are non-fainted/non-statused, it hits 4 times. Beat Up's individual hit power uses the BASE Attack stat (not staged) of each party member.

source_data.effect_commands_reference.md: Effect IDs are referenced by move effect bytes. Effect 0 = standard damage. Status moves have power=0. Each non-zero effect ID maps to a specific command script in engine/battle/effect_commands.asm.

unstructured_notes: QA priority: the accuracy byte mismatch is the most likely error. If a developer implements accuracy as a percentage (0-100) instead of the 0-255 scale, tests will pass visually but battle outcomes will differ. A move with accuracy=100% should store 255, not 100. Blizzard at 70% accurate should store ~178 (round(70/100*255)=178.5 -> 178). The freeze check on Blizzard: 10% chance means effect_chance byte ~= 26. High-crit moves should roughly double the critical hit frequency compared to normal moves (base crit chance in Gen 2 is approximately speed/512 stage, but high-crit adds a stage, roughly doubling it for typical speed values). The Magnitude test with 1000 samples should get within ~3 standard deviations of expected counts.

unstructured_notes: The evos_attacks.asm file in pokecrystal stores level-up moves and evolution data together in a single entry per Pokemon. Level-1 moves in evolved forms represent moves the Pokemon 'starts with' if it evolves at a level above where those moves would normally be learned — they are given automatically. Sorting by level is important: the level-up system checks moves in order and grants all moves at the reached level. Multiple moves can be learned at the same level (e.g. Bulbasaur learns both PoisonPowder and SleepPowder at level 15). TM/HM compatibility in pokecrystal is stored as a bitmask in the base stats data, not in a separate file. The TM list runs TM01-TM50 then HM01-HM07. The learnset_by_move.md reverse index is useful for cross-validation but all_pokemon.md is the primary source. Egg moves can only be inherited if a compatible father Pokemon knows the move — the exact breeding rules are in sprint 182.

Implement trade-with-item evolutions: Poliwhirl+KingsRock=Politoed, Slowpoke+KingsRock=Slowking, Onix+MetalCoat=Steelix, Scyther+MetalCoat=Scizor, Seadra+DragonScale=Kingdra, Porygon+UpGrade=Porygon2
Implement Everstone blocking: if the evolving Pokemon holds an Everstone, cancel evolution (applies to level and happiness methods, NOT stone or trade)
Build a family_chain function that given any species ID returns the full chain from base to final form

source_data.evolution_chains.md:
Key evolution families:
Bulbasaur->Ivysaur(16)->Venusaur(32)
Charmander->Charmeleon(16)->Charizard(36)
Squirtle->Wartortle(16)->Blastoise(36)
Eevee->Vaporeon(WaterStone), Jolteon(ThunderStone), Flareon(FireStone), Espeon(HappinessDay), Umbreon(HappinessNight)
Zubat->Golbat(22)->Crobat(Happiness)
Oddish->Gloom(21)->Vileplume(LeafStone) or Bellossom(SunStone) [branching]
Poliwhirl->Poliwrath(WaterStone) or Politoed(Trade+KingsRock) [branching]
Tyrogue->Hitmonlee(Atk>Def@20), Hitmonchan(Def>Atk@20), Hitmontop(Atk=Def@20) [stat-based]
Slowpoke->Slowbro(37) or Slowking(Trade+KingsRock) [branching]
Onix->Steelix(Trade+MetalCoat)
Scyther->Scizor(Trade+MetalCoat)
Seadra->Kingdra(Trade+DragonScale)
Porygon->Porygon2(Trade+UpGrade)
source_data.evolution_mechanics.md:
### Evolution Methods
1. EVOLVE_LEVEL: condition: level >= required
2. EVOLVE_ITEM: stone used from bag
3. EVOLVE_TRADE: during trade (item consumed if trade+item)
4. EVOLVE_HAPPINESS: level up with happiness>=220, day/night variants for Espeon/Umbreon
5. EVOLVE_STAT: Tyrogue only (Attack vs Defense comparison)

Happiness threshold = 220 out of 255
Everstone prevents level and happiness evolutions (NOT stone or trade)
Trade+item evolutions: item consumed on evolution

unstructured_notes: There are multiple branching evolution families where one Pokemon can evolve into one of several forms depending on conditions. These must be stored as a list of possible evolutions per species (not a single target). The branching families are: Eevee (5 possible evolutions), Poliwhirl (2), Slowpoke (2), Tyrogue (3), Oddish/Gloom (branching at Gloom). In pokecrystal, evolution data is stored as a sequence of possible evolutions per Pokemon, terminating with a null entry. The happiness threshold is 220 in the source code, but Bulbapedia sometimes cites 220 as well — they agree here. Day/night for Espeon/Umbreon: the game uses a time-of-day system with MORN (morning), DAY (day), NITE (night) periods, but happiness evolution only checks DAY vs NITE broadly. The exact time boundaries must match the game's period definitions.

unstructured_notes: The most common QA failures here: (1) Evolved forms missing their inherited level-1 moves — if Charizard is obtained by evolving Charmeleon at a level above where Charizard would learn Tackle/Scratch/Growl/Ember, those moves should have already been given at earlier levels, but the level-1 entries in Charizard's learnset represent moves given if it somehow starts at level 1 via cheats or debug. (2) Egg moves being added to evolved forms when they should only be on baby/base forms — egg moves are only inherited at birth. (3) Tyrogue tie-break: when Atk exactly equals Def, it must evolve to Hitmontop — confirm the equality case is handled as Hitmontop not defaulting to Hitmonlee or Hitmonchan. (4) Trade evolutions with items in the Time Capsule context: Gen 1 compatibility restrictions (sprint 224) may affect these, but for this sprint focus only on in-game Gen 2 trade evolution data.

source_data.item_details.md: Type-boost items are all held items with +10% to their respective type. Note: Dragon Fang listed as 'Powers up dragon-type moves' in description but HELD_DRAGON_BOOST in code points to Dragon Scale — this is a bug to replicate. Berries activate automatically in battle when HP drops below 50% (Berry/Gold Berry) or when the relevant status is inflicted. MysteryBerry restores PP of the first depleted move. Friend Ball sets caught Pokemon's happiness to 200.

unstructured_notes: Item pockets in Crystal: Items (general use), Balls (Poke Balls), Key Items (key items), TMs/HMs. Mail is stored separately but is part of the Items pocket. The Dragon Fang bug is critical to replicate: the held item boost lookup table in pokecrystal maps HELD_DRAGON_BOOST to Dragon Scale's item ID, not Dragon Fang. Implementing Dragon Fang as a +10% Dragon booster would be wrong — it should do nothing. Quick Claw activation chance is documented as 60% in pokecrystal source (not the commonly cited 23.4% from Gen 4 onwards). Friend Ball sets happiness to 200 on catch (compared to normal starting happiness of ~70). The Berserk Gene is a one-time-use item that sharply raises Attack (+2 stages) but causes confusion; it is consumed on first turn of use, not when equipped. Sacred Ash is a single-use key-like item that revives ALL fainted Pokemon in party to full HP — it is the only way to do this outside of separate Revives.

goal: Build memory layout and save data structures — WRAM/HRAM/SRAM layout, party struct, box struct, trainer card, options
abstract_tasks: Define Rust structs mirroring PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=48 and BOXMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=32 byte layouts with all fields (species, item, moves, PP, happiness, DVs, StatExp, level, status, HP, stats, caught data)
Implement WRAM battle union layout as Rust enums/unions: BattleState containing active battle fields (substatus1-5 bytes, stat level arrays, screen flags, weather, damage trackers, turn counters)
Define HRAM equivalents as Rust fields: RNG state (hRandomAdd/hRandomSub), math registers (hMultiplicand/hMultiplier/hProduct), hBattleTurn, hJoyPressed

Build SRAM save structure: sOptions (text speed, battle style, sound, frame), sPlayerData (name, ID, money, badges), sPartyMon array, sPokemonBoxes (14 boxes x 20 Pokemon), sMysteryGiftData, sBattleTowerData
Implement wEventFlags as a 1400+ bit array with named accessors for all key story events (EVENT_GOT_A_POKEMON_FROM_ELM, EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_FOUR, etc.)
Define player data fields: wPlayerName (7 chars), wPlayerID (u16), wMoney (3-byte BCD), wJohtoBadges/wKantoBadges (u8 each as bit arrays)
Implement caught data packing: CAUGHT_TIME_MASK (2 bits), CAUGHT_LEVEL_MASK (6 bits), CAUGHT_GENDER_MASK (1 bit), CAUGHT_LOCATION_MASK (7 bits)

source_data.memory_map.md:
## WRAM Bank 0 ($C000-$CFFF)
- wBattleMon: Player active Pokemon (battle_struct)
- wEnemyMon: Enemy active Pokemon
- wPlayerSubStatus1..5, wEnemySubStatus1..5: volatile substatus bytes
- wPlayerStatLevels/wEnemyStatLevels: 7 stages (Atk,Def,Spd,SAtk,SDef,Acc,Eva)
- wPlayerScreens/wEnemyScreens: Spikes, Safeguard, Light Screen, Reflect
- wBattleWeather: Current weather (0-6); wWeatherCount: Turns remaining
- wPlayerDamageTaken, wEnemyDamageTaken: 2 bytes each
- wPlayerStats/wEnemyStats: 5 stats x 2 bytes each
- Turn counters: Rollout, Confuse, Toxic, Disable, Encore, Perish, FuryCutter, Protect

## WRAM Bank 1 ($D000-$DFFF)
- wPlayerName: 8 bytes; wPlayerID: 2 bytes; wMoney: 3 bytes BCD
- wJohtoBadges, wKantoBadges: 1 byte each (8 bits = 8 badges)
- wPartyCount: 0-6; wPartySpecies: 6 bytes + terminator
- wPartyMon1..6: PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=48 bytes each
  - Species, Item, Moves (4), Move PP (4), Happiness, DVs (2 bytes)
  - StatExp (5 x 2 bytes), Level, Status, HP (current/max), Stats
  - Caught data (time/level, gender/location)
- wPokedexOwned/Seen: 32 bytes each (256 bits)
- wEventFlags: Large bit array (~1400 flags)

## HRAM ($FF80-$FFFE)
- hRandomAdd, hRandomSub: RNG state
- hMultiplicand (3 bytes), hMultiplier (1 byte), hProduct (4 bytes)
- hDivisor (1 byte), hQuotient (4 bytes)
- hBattleTurn: 0=player, 1=enemy
- hROMBank, hVBlank, hJoyPressed, hJoyDown, hInMenu

## SRAM
- sOptions: text speed, battle style, sound, frame
- sPlayerData, sPartyMon, sPokemonBoxes (14 boxes x 20 Pokemon)
- sMysteryGiftData, sBattleTowerData
source_data.game_constants.md:
### Party/Box Struct Sizes
- PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH = 48 bytes
- BOXMON_STRUCT_LENGTH = 32 bytes
- PARTY_LENGTH = 6 (max party size)
- MONS_PER_BOX = 20 (max Pokemon per box)

### Caught Data Packing
- CAUGHT_TIME_MASK = %11000000 (2 bits for time of day)
- CAUGHT_LEVEL_MASK = %00111111 (6 bits for level, max 63)
- CAUGHT_GENDER_MASK = %10000000 (1 bit for OT gender)
- CAUGHT_LOCATION_MASK = %01111111 (7 bits for location, max 127)

### Sub-Status Flags (5 bytes per side)
SubStatus1: NIGHTMARE, CURSE, PROTECT, IDENTIFIED, PERISH, ENDURE, ROLLOUT, IN_LOVE
SubStatus2: CURLED
SubStatus3: BIDE, RAMPAGE, IN_LOOP, FLINCHED, CHARGED, UNDERGROUND, FLYING, CONFUSED
SubStatus4: X_ACCURACY, MIST, FOCUS_ENERGY, SUBSTITUTE, RECHARGE, RAGE, LEECH_SEED
SubStatus5: TOXIC, TRANSFORMED, ENCORED, LOCK_ON, DESTINY_BOND, CANT_RUN

### Screen Flags
SCREENS_SPIKES=bit0, SCREENS_SAFEGUARD=bit2, SCREENS_LIGHT_SCREEN=bit3, SCREENS_REFLECT=bit4

### Weather Constants
WEATHER_NONE=0, WEATHER_RAIN=1, WEATHER_SUN=2, WEATHER_SANDSTORM=3
WEATHER_RAIN_END=4, WEATHER_SUN_END=5, WEATHER_SANDSTORM_END=6

### Move Struct (7 bytes)
Offset 0: MOVE_ANIM, 1: MOVE_EFFECT, 2: MOVE_POWER, 3: MOVE_TYPE, 4: MOVE_ACC, 5: MOVE_PP, 6: MOVE_CHANCE

unstructured_notes: The battle union at $C600+ is key: many unrelated data structures share the same WRAM region depending on game mode. In Rust, model this as an enum or tagged union. The PARTYMON struct differs from BOXMON by 16 bytes — party includes current HP, stats (10 bytes), and PP Up bits. Box Pokemon only store DVs, StatExp, moves, PP, and basic metadata, not computed stats. The caught_data byte pair packs two separate concerns: byte 1 has CAUGHT_TIME (2 bits) + CAUGHT_LEVEL (6 bits); byte 2 has CAUGHT_GENDER (1 bit) + CAUGHT_LOCATION (7 bits). This sprint depends on Sprint 2 (constants) and is prerequisite for Sprints 16-17 (stat calc) and Sprint 19 (RNG, uses HRAM fields). The wEventFlags bit array maps directly to the event_flags.asm file with ~1400 named bits — implement as a Vec<u64> with named accessor methods, not a huge struct. SRAM integrity in Crystal uses a checksum; simulate it for save/load correctness.

goal: Implement DV system — 4 stored DVs, derived HP DV, shared Special DV, gender determination from Attack DV, shiny check
abstract_tasks: Implement DV storage: two bytes encoding Attack(high nibble)+Defense(low nibble) in byte 1, Speed(high nibble)+Special(low nibble) in byte 2, all values 0-15
Implement HP DV derivation formula: HP_DV = (Attack_DV & 1)*8 + (Defense_DV & 1)*4 + (Speed_DV & 1)*2 + (Special_DV & 1)
Implement Special DV sharing: both SpAtk and SpDef use the same single Special DV value from byte 2 low nibble
Implement gender determination from Attack DV: compare against species gender ratio threshold (0=always male, 255=genderless, 2/4/8/12/14 for various ratios); if Attack_DV >= threshold -> male
Implement shiny check: shiny if Defense_DV==10 AND Speed_DV==10 AND Special_DV==10 AND Attack_DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}
Implement DV generation for wild Pokemon: call RNG twice, first byte = Attack DV (high nibble) + Defense DV (low nibble), second byte = Speed DV (high nibble) + Special DV (low nibble)
Write unit tests validating HP DV derivation for edge cases (all DVs 0, all DVs 15, mixed nibbles) and shiny probability (~1/8192)

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Determinant Values (DVs)
DVs are Gen 2's equivalent of IVs. Range: 0-15 for each stat.

### DV Storage
- Byte 1: AAAA DDDD (high nibble = Attack DV, low nibble = Defense DV)
- Byte 2: SSSS PPPP (high nibble = Speed DV, low nibble = Special DV)

### HP DV (derived)
HP_DV = (Attack_DV & 1)*8 + (Defense_DV & 1)*4 + (Speed_DV & 1)*2 + (Special_DV & 1)
Range: 0-15, determined by other 4 DVs.

### Special DV
Shared between Special Attack and Special Defense. Both use same value.

## Gender Determination
Gender determined by Attack DV vs species gender threshold.
If Attack_DV >= threshold: Male. If < threshold: Female.
Thresholds: 0=always male, 2=87.5% male, 4=75%, 8=50%, 12=25%, 14=12.5%, 255=genderless
Consequence: Female Pokemon never have Attack DV >= gender threshold.
For 87.5% male species (starter, Eevee, threshold=2), females can only have Atk DV 0 or 1.

## Shininess
Shiny if: Defense DV=10, Speed DV=10, Special DV=10, Attack DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}
Probability: ~1/8192 (5 valid Attack DVs x (1/16)^3 for the other three)
HP DV for shinies is always 0 or 8 (since Def/Spd/Spc are all even=10)
source_data.game_constants.md:
### Shiny DVs
- ATKDEFDV_SHINY = $EA (Atk=14, Def=10)
- SPDSPCDV_SHINY = $AA (Spd=10, Spc=10)
- Shiny requires: Atk DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}, others = 10
BASE_STAT_LEVEL = 7 (stored stage for neutral; actual stage = stored - 7)
Stat stages stored as 1-13, neutral is 7

unstructured_notes: The 'Special DV' being shared between SpAtk and SpDef is a fundamental Gen 2 quirk — when you calculate Special Attack, use the Special DV; when you calculate Special Defense, use the SAME Special DV. This is why high-Special-DV Pokemon are valuable for both offensive and defensive special stats. Female Pokemon of 87.5% male ratio species (starters, Eevee) are locked to Attack DV 0 or 1, capping their Attack stat severely — this matters for competitive play. Shiny DVs: the ATKDEFDV_SHINY constant $EA decodes as Atk=14 (high nibble of $E=14), Def=10 (low nibble of $A=10). Valid shiny Attack DVs {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15} are the 8 values where the low bit of Atk could be 0 or 1 — wait, actually: 2=0010(bit0=0), 3=0011(bit0=1), 6=0110(bit0=0), 7=0111(bit0=1), 10=1010(bit0=0), 11=1011(bit0=1), 14=1110(bit0=0), 15=1111(bit0=1). These are all values where bits 1 and 3 are set OR bits 1 and 3 are clear with bit0 varying... actually these are numbers >= 2 where the top bit pattern produces Def DV parity. Depends on Sprint 14 (memory layout for DV byte storage in PartyMon struct).

goal: Implement stat calculation — HP formula, other stat formula, stat experience contribution, level-up recalculation
abstract_tasks: Implement HP formula: floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + Level + 10, using integer arithmetic with correct rounding
Implement other stats formula: floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + 5, for Attack/Defense/Speed/SpAtk/SpDef
Implement StatExp contribution: floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp)) / 4) — note use of ceil(sqrt()) not floor(sqrt()); max contribution is 64 at StatExp=65535
Implement stat recalculation triggers: on level-up, on evolution, on PC deposit/withdraw, on vitamin use; NOT continuously during battle
Implement vitamin stat experience gain: each vitamin adds 2560 to the stat's StatExp, capped at 25600 (cannot raise above 25600 with vitamins)
Verify example: Level 50 Mewtwo SpAtk (base=154, DV=15, maxStatExp=65535): inner=(154+15)*2+64=402, stat=floor(402*50/100)+5=201+5=206
Write tests for: minimum stats (base=1, DV=0, StatExp=0, Lv1), maximum stats (base=255, DV=15, StatExp=65535, Lv100), and the specific Mewtwo example

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Stat Formulas
HP = floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + Level + 10
Other = floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + 5

Max StatExp contribution: floor(ceil(sqrt(65535))/4) = floor(256/4) = 64

## Stat Experience
Gain: when defeating enemy, each participating mon gains StatExp equal to defeated species base stats.
Accumulation: HP+=base HP, Atk+=base Atk, Def+=base Def, Spd+=base Spd, Spc+=base Spc (shared)
No cap on total (all 5 stats can be maxed at 65535)
Vitamins add 2560, capped at 25600 per stat.

## Stat Recalculation
Recalculated: on level-up, on evolution, on PC deposit/withdraw, on vitamin use
NOT continuously in battle (battle uses stat stages only)

## Example: Lv50 Mewtwo SpAtk (base=154, DV=15, maxStatExp)
Inner = (154+15)*2 + 64 = 338 + 64 = 402
Stat = floor(402*50/100) + 5 = 201 + 5 = 206
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Stat Calculation
- Base: Species base stat (0-255)
- DV: Determinant Value (0-15)
- StatExp: Stat Experience (0-65535)
- Level: 1-100
Source: engine/pokemon/mon_stats.asm — CalcMonStats

## Paralysis and Burn Stat Effects
Paralysis: Speed * 25% (quartered), applied to battle stat directly
Burn: Attack * 50% (halved), affects physical damage only
Critical hit + burn: if attacker Atk stage >= defender Def stage, burn halving NOT ignored

unstructured_notes: The key implementation trap is ceil(sqrt(StatExp)) vs floor(sqrt(StatExp)). The formula uses ceil, meaning sqrt(65535)=255.998..., ceil=256, /4=64. Using floor would give 255/4=63. Always use ceiling for the sqrt step. StatExp is per-stat, not shared — the Special StatExp is used for BOTH SpAtk and SpDef calculations. Paralysis and Burn effects on stats are battle-only multipliers applied AFTER CalcMonStats produces the base stat — they do not modify the party data stat values permanently. At Lv1, HP formula: ((base+DV)*2+0)*1/100+1+10 which floors to 0 for most species, producing HP=11. A Shedinja-like case (base HP=1) at Lv1 with DV=0 StatExp=0: floor((1+0)*2/100)+1+10=0+11=11 still. Minimum meaningful stat values need testing. Depends on Sprint 16 (DV system).

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Example Calculations
Lv50 Mewtwo SpAtk (base=154, DV=15, maxStatExp=65535):
Inner = (154+15)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(65535))/4) = 338 + 64 = 402
Stat = floor(402*50/100) + 5 = 201 + 5 = 206

Max StatExp contribution = floor(ceil(sqrt(65535))/4) = floor(256/4) = 64

## Shiny Conditions
Defense DV=10, Speed DV=10, Special DV=10, Attack DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}
HP DV for shinies: (Atk&1)*8 + 0 + 0 + 0 = 0 or 8
Probability: 8/16 * 1/16 * 1/16 * 1/16 = 8/65536 = 1/8192

## Gender Thresholds
0: Always male (all DVs male)
2: 87.5% male (threshold means Attack DV 0,1 = female; 2-15 = male)
4: 75% male (Attack DV 0-3 = female; 4-15 = male)
8: 50% male (0-7 female; 8-15 male)
12: 25% male (0-11 female; 12-15 male)
14: 12.5% male (0-13 female; 14-15 male)
255: Genderless (never satisfied >= 255)

| Stage | Multiplier | Fraction |

## Paralysis Speed Reduction

## Scenario 12: Sleep Counter on Switch

unstructured_notes: Critical testing note: the formula uses ceil(sqrt(StatExp)) not floor. At StatExp=65535, sqrt=255.998, ceil=256, /4=64. At StatExp=65536 (impossible, max is 65535), same result. At StatExp=0, contribution=0. At StatExp=1, sqrt=1, ceil=1, /4=0 (rounds down). At StatExp=15, sqrt=3.87, ceil=4, /4=1. These edge points are important for boundary tests. Gender determination has a real gameplay consequence: since female starters can only have Attack DV 0 or 1, their max Attack stat is significantly lower — this is authentic Crystal behavior. The QA agent should run simulation tests comparing against known Pokemon Crystal save file stat values where available.

goal: Implement RNG system — hardware RNG equivalent (PRNG for recreation), battle PRNG, link-synchronized PRNG
abstract_tasks: Implement hardware RNG substitute: use a fast PRNG (xorshift or similar) seeded at battle/game start instead of the DIV-register-based hardware RNG, returning a single random byte
Implement battle PRNG (BattleRandom): for non-link battles, use the hardware RNG substitute; for link battles, use the LCG stream system
Implement link battle LCG: 10-byte seed array (wLinkBattleRNs), counter (wLinkBattleRNCount); advance formula: new_seed = (old_seed * 5 + 1) & 0xFF; consume 9 values then regenerate all 10
Implement RandomRange(n): unbiased random int in [0, n) using rejection sampling — compute bias=256%n, reject if result < (256-bias), then take result%n
Implement per-frame VBlank RNG advancement for simulation accuracy: even during menu/idle states, advance RNG state every simulated frame
Expose an interface allowing the simulation to either use deterministic seeded PRNG (for testing) or non-deterministic PRNG (for gameplay)
Write distribution tests: call BattleRandom 256000 times, verify each byte value appears approximately 1000 times (uniform distribution within 5%)

source_data.rng_mechanics.md:
## Two RNG Systems
1. Hardware RNG (Random): reads hardware DIV register (16384 Hz); advances in VBlank too; returns hRandomSub
   NOT deterministic from seed. For recreation: use seeded PRNG instead.
2. Battle PRNG (BattleRandom): non-link = calls Random; link = uses LCG stream:
   - 10 seeds in wLinkBattleRNs, position in wLinkBattleRNCount
   - Advance: new = (old * 5 + 1) & 0xFF (LCG: a=5, c=1, m=256)
   - Consume 9 values per generation (off-by-one: cp SERIAL_RNS_LENGTH-1 = cp 9, index 8 triggers regen)
   - 10th value from old set returned while new set generated
   - Period: 256 per seed

## RandomRange
Unbiased: b = 256 % c; call Random until result < (256-b); return result % c

## VBlank RNG Advancement
Every frame (~59.7Hz): adds DIV to hRandomAdd, subtracts DIV from hRandomSub
Means RNG changes every frame even when idle — constant entropy injection

## Implementation Notes
1. Do NOT use hardware RNG. Use seeded PRNG.
2. Link battle PRNG is the only deterministic option.
3. BattleRandom vs Random distinction matters for replay determinism.
4. RNG consumption ORDER is critical — crit before accuracy = different outcomes.
5. Link battle LCG subtle off-by-one: index 8 (not 9) triggers regeneration.
source_data.core_routines.md:
## Random (home/random.asm)
Hardware-based. Returns hRandomSub in a. Called from VBlank.
NOT suitable for link battles (would desync).

## BattleRandom
Deterministic PRNG for battle. Uses 10-byte stream (wLinkBattleRNCount tracks position).
Shared seed ensures link battle sync. Returns random byte in a.

## RandomRange
Returns random in [0, a). Uses rejection sampling to avoid modulo bias.

unstructured_notes: The off-by-one in the link battle LCG is important: the PRNG stream has 10 slots but only 9 are consumed before regeneration. Index 8 (the 9th slot, 0-indexed) triggers regeneration; the 10th slot (index 9) from the OLD set is returned as the result while the NEW set is being computed. This subtlety means a fresh stream effectively gives you 9 values + 1 old value before full refresh. For the recreation: in non-link battles, use xorshift128 or a similar fast PRNG seeded with a u64. In link battles, implement the LCG exactly. The consumption ORDER of RNG calls in the battle engine is critical — damage variation is called AFTER accuracy, which is called AFTER critical hit check, etc. Getting this order wrong will produce correct individual probabilities but wrong correlated outcomes. This sprint is a prerequisite for sprints 22-23 (damage formula) since damage variation uses BattleRandom.

goal: Implement stat stages — 7 stats, levels 1-13, multiplier table (25/100 to 400/100), stage application to raw stats
abstract_tasks: Implement stat stage storage: 7 stats per side (Atk, Def, Spd, SpAtk, SpDef, Acc, Eva), stored as u8 values 1-13 where 7=neutral (stage 0), 1=stage -6, 13=stage +6
Implement attack/defense/speed multiplier table: stages 1-13 map to fractions 2/8, 2/7, 2/6, 2/5, 2/4, 2/3, 2/2, 3/2, 4/2, 5/2, 6/2, 7/2, 8/2 (i.e., 25% through 400%)
Implement accuracy/evasion multiplier table: stages 1-13 map to fractions 3/9, 3/8, 3/7, 3/6, 3/5, 3/4, 3/3, 4/3, 5/3, 6/3, 7/3, 8/3, 9/3
Implement stat stage application: apply stage multiplier to the base computed stat value, truncating to integer; enforce minimum stat value of 1
Implement paralysis speed reduction: multiply Speed by 25% (divide by 4) AFTER stat stage is applied, truncating to integer
Implement burn attack reduction: multiply Attack by 50% (divide by 2) AFTER stat stage is applied for physical moves only
Implement stage change bounds: stages cannot go below 1 or above 13; stage change fails if already at the limit with appropriate message

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Stat Stages (In-Battle Modifiers)
Range: -6 to +6 (stored 1-13, BASE_STAT_LEVEL=7=neutral)

### Attack/Defense/SpAtk/SpDef/Speed Multipliers
Stage -6: 2/8 (0.25)
Stage -5: 2/7 (0.286)
Stage -4: 2/6 (0.333)
Stage -3: 2/5 (0.40)
Stage -2: 2/4 (0.50)
Stage -1: 2/3 (0.667)
Stage  0: 2/2 (1.00)
Stage +1: 3/2 (1.50)
Stage +2: 4/2 (2.00)
Stage +3: 5/2 (2.50)
Stage +4: 6/2 (3.00)
Stage +5: 7/2 (3.50)
Stage +6: 8/2 (4.00)

### Accuracy/Evasion Multipliers
Stage -6: 3/9; -5: 3/8; -4: 3/7; -3: 3/6; -2: 3/5; -1: 3/4
Stage  0: 3/3; +1: 4/3; +2: 5/3; +3: 6/3; +4: 7/3; +5: 8/3; +6: 9/3

Accuracy formula: EffectiveAccuracy = MoveAccuracy * AccStageMultiplier * EvaStageMultiplier
If Foresight active AND target evasion stage > attacker accuracy stage: bypass evasion/accuracy entirely.

## Status Effects on Stats
Paralysis: Speed * 25% (quartered), applied after stat stage
Burn: Attack * 50% (halved), applied after stat stage for physical moves
Critical hit: if attacker Atk stage >= target Def stage, burn halving ignored (and all stages ignored)
source_data.game_constants.md:
BASE_STAT_LEVEL = 7 (default/neutral stat stage, stored value)
MAX_STAT_LEVEL = 13 (maximum stored value, = stage +6)
Stat stages: 7 stats tracked (Atk,Def,Spd,SAtk,SDef,Acc,Eva) + 1 unused byte
wPlayerStatLevels, wEnemyStatLevels: 8 bytes each (7 stats + padding)
source_data.move_effects_complete.md: Stat stage limits: stages cannot go above MAX_STAT_LEVEL or below 1. CheckFail condition for already-maxed/minimized stat. Haze resets ALL stat stages to BASE_STAT_LEVEL (7) for both sides. Psych Up copies all 7 stat stages from target to user. Baton Pass: all stat stages transferred to incoming Pokemon.

unstructured_notes: The stored value is 1-13, not -6 to +6. To convert: stage = stored_value - 7. A stored value of 7 = stage 0. All stat stage change moves modify the stored byte. When implementing Haze, reset all 14 stat level bytes (7 per side) to 7 (BASE_STAT_LEVEL). When implementing Psych Up, copy the opponent's 7 stat level bytes over the user's 7 stat level bytes. The accuracy check formula is: generate a random byte, compare to EffectiveAccuracy = (MoveBaseAccuracy * AccMultiplier_num / AccMultiplier_den) * (EvaMultiplier_den / EvaMultiplier_num) truncated to 0-255. The evasion stage effectively inverts: stage +6 evasion makes moves less likely to hit. Foresight/Mind Reader interaction: if SUBSTATUS_IDENTIFIED is set on the target AND target's evasion stage > attacker's accuracy stage, skip the evasion/accuracy modifier entirely (always use raw move accuracy). Depends on Sprints 16-17 (stat system) and Sprint 19 (RNG for battle calculations).

goal: QA audit of sprints 19-20 — verify RNG distribution, stat stage multipliers, edge cases at min/max stages
abstract_tasks: Run 1,000,000 BattleRandom calls and verify uniform byte distribution: each value 0-255 should appear ~3906 times; check chi-squared test passes at p=0.01
Verify stat multiplier at each of the 13 stages: apply stage to a base stat of 100 and confirm results: stages give 25,29,33,40,50,67,100,150,200,250,300,350,400 (rounded to integer)
Test stat stage clamping: attempt to raise an already-maxed (+6, stored=13) stat stage and verify it returns a failure code and leaves the stage at 13
Test stat stage clamping: attempt to lower an already-minimized (-6, stored=1) stat stage and verify failure and stored value remains 1
Verify paralysis speed reduction: a Pokemon with 100 Speed and paralysis should compute effective speed as 25; at stage +6 paralyzed, should be floor(400*25/100)=100
Verify burn attack halving: a Pokemon with 100 Attack and burn, stage +2, should compute effective attack as floor(200*50/100)=100
Test Foresight evasion bypass: target with +6 evasion, attacker with 0 accuracy stage, Foresight active — move should hit at its base accuracy percentage (not reduced by evasion)

source_data.rng_mechanics.md:
## RNG Consumption Order (critical for determinism)
Battle RNG consumers:
Accuracy check, Critical hit check, Damage variation, Move effect chance,
Confusion self-hit (50%), Infatuation (50%), Paralysis (25%),
Sleep counter (1-7 turns), Disable duration, Encore duration, Confusion duration,
Multi-hit count, Metronome, Present power, Magnitude, AI decisions,
Quick Claw activation, Speed tie, Protect/Detect consecutive check, Sleep Talk selection

## LCG Properties
Formula: next = (current * 5 + 1) & 0xFF
Period: 256. Full-period LCG (odd multiplier, odd c, power-of-2 m).
9 of 10 stream values consumed per generation cycle (index 8 triggers regen).
