source_data.mechanics/move_effects_complete.md:
### Paralyze (BattleCommand_ParalyzeTarget)
- Fails if: target has any status, substitute up
- Blocked by: HELD_PREVENT_PARALYZE, Safeguard
- After paralyzing: applies speed reduction (ApplyPrzEffectOnSpeed)

### Tri-Attack (BattleCommand_TriStatusChance)
- 1/3 chance each of: paralyze, freeze, burn
- Uses EffectChance first (secondary effect chance)

### Confuse (BattleCommand_Confuse)
- Confusion counter: random 2-5 turns
- 50% chance of self-hit per turn while confused
- Self-hit: 40 power, typeless, physical, uses own Attack/Defense

### Swagger
- Raises target's Attack by 2, then confuses them
- AI_Redundant: discouraged if target already confused
source_data.mechanics/status_effects.md:
### Paralysis
- Full paralysis: 25% chance each turn of being unable to act
- Speed reduction: Speed stat multiplied by 25% (quartered). Persistent battle stat modification.
- Immunity: Electric-type Pokemon cannot be paralyzed
- Paralysis check happens LAST in pre-move checks (after recharge, sleep, freeze, flinch, disable, confusion, attract)

### Confusion
- Duration: 2-5 turns (randomly set)
- Self-hit chance: 50% each turn
- Self-hit damage: typeless physical, 40 base power, own Attack and Defense
- No STAB, no type effectiveness, critical hits disabled for self-hits
- BUG: Type-boosting items and Explosion defense-halving DO affect confusion damage
- BUG: Focus Band cannot prevent KO from confusion self-hit in Gen 2

### Freeze
- Duration: Indefinite (no automatic thaw during turn checks)
- Thaw check: 20% chance at END of each turn cycle (HandleDefrost in HandleBetweenTurnEffects)
- Self-thaw moves: Flame Wheel and Sacred Fire thaw user before attacking
- Hit by Fire move: thaws target
- Immunity: Fire-type Pokemon cannot be frozen
- Cannot freeze in WEATHER_SUN
source_data.mechanics/effect_commands_reference.md:
| tristatuseffect | BattleCommand_TriStatusChance | 1/3 each: paralyze/freeze/burn (Tri Attack) |
| confusetarget | BattleCommand_ConfuseTarget | Inflict confusion (secondary effect) |
| confuse | BattleCommand_Confuse | Inflict confusion (primary move) |

unstructured_notes: Thunder's 30% paralysis chance makes it one of the most common para-inflicters and should be carefully verified with the effectchance command. The Swagger/Flatter ordering matters: the stat boost happens first, so if the target is already at +6 Attack, the 'won't rise anymore' message prints but confusion still applies. For Tri Attack, the effectchance check uses the move's CHANCE field (20% in the move data), then if it succeeds, picks one of the three statuses randomly. The Fire/Ice/Electric/Paralyze interactions with existing status are important: a burned Pokemon cannot also be frozen — the second status attempt just fails. The confusion self-hit bug (type-boosting items boost self-damage) must be preserved; do not add a guard against this. ApplyPrzEffectOnSpeed and ApplyBrnEffectOnAttack apply immediately on status infliction, not at the start of the next turn.

source_data.mechanics/status_effects.md:
### Badly Poisoned (Toxic)
- Escalating damage: N/16 of max HP, N increments each turn, max at 15
- Switch behavior: switching out converts badly poisoned to regular poison, toxic counter lost
- Internal storage: SUBSTATUS_TOXIC in substatus5 plus regular poison in status byte

### Safeguard
- Duration: 5 turns
- Prevents non-volatile status conditions from being inflicted
- Does NOT prevent: Confusion, attraction, stat drops, volatile conditions

### Confusion
- BUG: Type-boosting items DO affect confusion self-hit damage
- BUG: Focus Band cannot prevent KO from confusion self-hit in Gen 2
source_data.mechanics/bugs_and_glitches.md:
### 5. 1/256 Secondary Effect Miss
Moves with 100% secondary effect chance generate random 0-255 and compare with cp [hl]. When random=255, comparison fails. ~0.4% of 'guaranteed' secondary effects don't trigger.

### 8. Confusion Damage Boosted by Items
Self-hit confusion damage passes through full BattleCommand_DamageCalc, including type-boosting item checks and Explosion defense-halving.
source_data.mechanics/battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 20: 1/256 Effect Miss Glitch
Even with 100% accuracy, there's a 1/256 chance the move misses. The accuracy check generates a random byte 0-255 and compares with cp. If random byte equals exactly 255 and accuracy is 255 (100%), cp 255 clears carry (255 is not < 255), causing a miss.

## Scenario 15: Substitute Blocks Status but Not Stat Changes
Growl goes through the Substitute and lowers Attack. Most status moves (Thunder Wave, Toxic) are blocked. Stat-lowering sound-based moves and some effects can bypass it.

unstructured_notes: The 1/256 miss glitch is particularly important to preserve for authenticity — it affects competitive play significantly and is a known Gen 2 mechanic. The test for this requires a statistical sample (10,000+ attempts) to confirm the rate. For Safeguard, the distinction is: blocks non-volatile status (sleep/burn/freeze/paralyze/poison/toxic) but NOT volatile statuses (confusion, infatuation) and NOT stat drops. The substitute blocking behavior has edge cases: Growl bypasses sub but Thunder Wave doesn't. Read the ASM to determine which commands check for substitute (SubstituteCheck) and which don't. The Toxic counter conversion on switch-out must be verified: after switching, the battle struct shows regular poison (not toxic), and on re-entry, toxic counter starts fresh — this means Toxic's escalating damage is permanently neutralized by a single switch-out.

unstructured_notes: In Gen 2, Growth is only +1 Special Attack (not the +2 in sun that Gen 6+ introduced). Amnesia is +2 SpDef, which was changed from +2 Special (both SpAtk and SpDef) in Gen 1. The Minimize flag (SUBSTATUS_MINIMIZED) is separate from the evasion stage — it persists even if Haze resets evasion. Stomp and Body Slam deal double damage to Minimized targets and also always hit them. The allstatsup command for Ancientpower raises all 5 stats independently: if Attack is already maxed, it skips that boost but still raises the others. This is also relevant for the stat-boosting after KO bug (#21): AncientPower and similar moves call allstatsup AFTER checkfaint, so if the target faints, the boost never fires. The Defense Curl flag (SUBSTATUS_CURLED) is set by the curl command — it affects Rollout but is separate from the actual Defense stage change from Defense Curl move.

unstructured_notes: The Mist check (CheckMist) is a critical distinction: it only blocks PRIMARY stat-lowering moves (Growl, Leer, Screech, etc.), NOT secondary stat drops from damaging moves like Psychic's SpDef drop. This means Mist does NOT protect against Psychic's SpDef drop. The AI 25% handicap for stat-lowering moves also has an exception: EFFECT_ACCURACY_DOWN_HIT (moves that deal damage and lower accuracy as secondary — Sand-Attack is pure accuracy down so it does get the handicap) bypasses the handicap. Flash and Sand-Attack both use BattleCommand_AccuracyDown with the AI handicap. The secondary stat drop on damaging moves uses the effectchance command which performs the 1/256 miss bug check. Bug #9 (Acid/Iron Tail/Rock Smash secondary after Sub break) must be preserved: the move script has effectchance AFTER hittarget, so if the substitute was already broken, the effect still fires. The stat-down messages: -1 prints 'StatFell', -2 prints 'StatSharplyFell'.

source_data.mechanics/battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 20: 1/256 Effect Miss Glitch
Even with 100% accuracy, there's a 1/256 chance the move misses. The accuracy check generates a random byte 0-255. If random byte equals exactly 255, comparison fails (255 is not < 255 in the cp instruction).

## Scenario 28: Rollout + Defense Curl Combo
Defense Curl sets SUBSTATUS_CURLED. When Rollout reads this flag, it adds +1 to the doubling counter. Rollout progression: 30->60->120->240->480 power without curl, effectively higher with curl.

unstructured_notes: The accuracy/evasion combined check is: finalAccuracy = moveAccuracy * (attackerAccuracyStage / 100) / (targetEvasionStage / 100). At -6 accuracy (33%) vs +6 evasion (300%), the hit chance is approximately 11% = 100 * (33/100) / (300/100). The Minimize flag is stored separately from the evasion stage in wPlayerMinimized/wEnemyMinimized — a global flag per side, not per-Pokemon. Verify that when the Minimized Pokemon switches out, the flag clears. The 1/256 miss must be present in all secondary effect checks — run the statistical test with a large sample. For Bug #9, set up a scenario with a Sub at low HP, use Acid that would break it, and check if the Defense drop message appears after the Sub faded message.

source_data.moves/move_details.md:
## Move Descriptions
DoubleSlap: 'Repeatedly slaps 2-5 times.'
Fury Swipes: 'Scratches 2-5 times.'
Pin Missile: 'Sharp pins hit 2-5 times.'
Triple Kick: 'Kicks foe 3 times. Power up.'

unstructured_notes: The multi-hit damage calculation is important: each hit is calculated independently (separate critical hit rolls, separate damage variation 85-100%), not as a single hit multiplied. This means multi-hit moves get 2-5 independent crit checks. For Twineedle, the poison check fires after EACH of the 2 hits — so there are 2 chances to poison, but each is blocked if the target already has a status from the first hit. Triple Kick's cumulative power means the accuracy check matters: if hit 2 misses, hit 3 still occurs (with 3x power) — the loop continues regardless of individual hit accuracy within the turn. The 'X-hit attack' end-of-sequence message ('Hit 3 times!' etc.) needs a hit counter tracked across the loop. King's Rock (10% flinch per hit) applies independently per hit, so 5-hit moves have up to 5 chances to flinch — the flinch only matters if the target hasn't already been KO'd.

Implement Dig semi-invulnerability: while SUBSTATUS_UNDERGROUND, most moves miss; exceptions that hit: Earthquake/Magnitude (double damage), Surf (double damage)

Implement SolarBeam charge skip in sun: skipsuncharge command checks WEATHER_SUN, if active skips the charge turn entirely and goes directly to attack; in rain, charge still required AND damage halved (0.5x weather modifier)

## Sleep Talk
### Exclusions (moves Sleep Talk will NOT call)
- Two-turn moves: Fly, Dig, Razor Wind, Sky Attack, Skull Bash, SolarBeam, Bide

source_data.mechanics/battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 4: Fly/Dig vs Earthquake/Thunder
Earthquake misses against a Pokemon using Fly. Thunder hits a Pokemon using Fly. Earthquake hits Pokemon using Dig (double damage). Gust/Twister hit Pokemon using Fly (double damage). Surf hits Pokemon using Dig (double damage).
ASM: CheckHit checks SUBSTATUS_FLYING/SUBSTATUS_UNDERGROUND and compares against specific move effects.

## Scenario 30: Weather + SolarBeam Interaction
Solar Beam charges instantly in Sun (skips charge turn). In Rain, SolarBeam still requires charge turn AND power halved to 60. In Sandstorm, SolarBeam functions normally (2-turn, full power).

unstructured_notes: The two-turn move system in Gen 2 uses a flag/substatus on the charging Pokemon to track charge state. The CheckCharge command at the start of the second turn reads this flag and either (a) skips to the attack phase if charged, or (b) the charge is set and turn ends. The first-turn charge printing is via a 'storing energy' or specific move message (e.g., '[MON] flew up high!', '[MON] burrowed its way underground!', '[MON] is glowing!'). SolarBeam's rain interaction is: the charge turn is not skipped (no skipsuncharge fires in rain), and the attack turn applies the weather damage modifier (WEATHER_RAIN + SolarBeam type = 0.5x). Skull Bash is unique in raising Defense during the charge turn — this is intentional and the Defense stays after the second turn. Sky Attack has a 30% flinch chance on the attack turn. Two-turn moves must be in the Sleep Talk exclusion list (checked in sleep_talk.asm). On switch-out during a charge turn, the substatus clears and the move is abandoned (the PP was already consumed on turn 1).

source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 4: Fly/Dig vs Earthquake/Thunder
**Setup:** Pokemon A uses Fly on turn 1. Pokemon B uses Earthquake on turn 2 while A is in the air.
**Outcome:** Earthquake misses against a Pokemon using Fly (semi-invulnerable). Thunder hits a Pokemon using Fly. Earthquake hits a Pokemon using Dig (double damage). Gust/Twister hit a Pokemon using Fly (double damage). Surf hits a Pokemon using Dig (double damage).
**ASM:** CheckHit in effect_commands.asm checks SUBSTATUS_FLYING/SUBSTATUS_UNDERGROUND and compares against the specific move effects that can hit through semi-invulnerability.

## Scenario 25: Magnitude Underground (Dig)
**Setup:** Pokemon B is using Dig (underground). Pokemon A uses Magnitude.
**Outcome:** Magnitude hits Pokemon underground for double damage, same as Earthquake. The magnitude number is still randomly determined (4-10), and the doubled damage applies to whatever the rolled power was.

## Scenario 26: Fury Cutter Maximum Damage
**Setup:** Pokemon uses Fury Cutter successfully 5 times in a row.
**Outcome:** Base power 10 -> 20 -> 40 -> 80 -> 160. The 6th hit and beyond stay at 160. If any hit misses or a different move is used, the counter resets.

## Scenario 30: Weather + SolarBeam Interaction
**Setup:** Sunny Day is active. Pokemon uses SolarBeam.
**Outcome:** SolarBeam charges instantly in Sun (skips the charge turn). In Rain, SolarBeam still requires the charge turn AND its power is halved to 60. In Sandstorm, SolarBeam functions normally (2-turn charge, full power).

### Fury Cutter
- Starts at base power (10 in Gen 2)
- Doubles each consecutive successful hit: 10, 20, 40, 80, 160
- Caps at 5 turns worth (16x multiplier, so max effective power = 160)
- Missing resets the counter
- Switching resets the counter
- Using any other move resets the counter

unstructured_notes: Sprint 54 is a QA sprint auditing sprints 52 (multi-hit) and 53 (two-turn). Key areas: (1) The multi-hit distribution bytes: random 0-95=2 hits, 96-191=3 hits, 192-223=4 hits, 224-255=5 hits. (2) Semi-invulnerable interaction matrix: Fly blocks Earthquake/Magnitude/Fissure/BlazeKick etc., but Thunder/Gust/Twister/Sky Uppercut (not in gen2) still hit. Dig blocks most but not Earthquake/Magnitude/Magnitude underground deals double. (3) SolarBeam: must use `skipsuncharge` command to skip in sun; in rain the DoWeatherModifiers applies 0.5x. The 1/256 secondary effect miss bug (bug #5 in bugs_and_glitches.md) is relevant for Twineedle's secondary poison chance. Simulation tests should check RNG distribution over many trials.

source_data.move_edge_cases.md:
### Rollout Mechanics
- 5 consecutive hits, damage doubling each hit
- Hit 1: base damage, Hit 2: 2x, Hit 3: 4x, Hit 4: 8x, Hit 5: 16x
- If a hit misses, the sequence ends
- After 5 hits, the move ends naturally
- Rollout locks the user in (can't switch or choose another move)

### Defense Curl Interaction
- If the user has used Defense Curl (SUBSTATUS_CURLED is set), Rollout damage is further doubled
- Effectively: Hit 1: 2x, Hit 2: 4x, ... Hit 5: 32x
- Defense Curl's Rollout boost persists even after stat stages are reset (it's a separate flag)

### Fury Cutter
- Starts at base power (10 in Gen 2)
- Doubles each consecutive successful hit: 10, 20, 40, 80, 160
- Caps at 5 turns worth (16x multiplier, so max effective power = 160)
- Missing resets the counter
- Switching resets the counter
- Using any other move resets the counter

source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 26: Fury Cutter Maximum Damage
**Setup:** Pokemon uses Fury Cutter successfully 5 times in a row.
**Outcome:** Fury Cutter's power doubles each consecutive successful hit, capping at 5 doublings. Base power 10 -> 20 -> 40 -> 80 -> 160. The 6th hit and beyond stay at 160. If any hit misses or a different move is used, the counter resets.
**ASM:** FuryCutter caps at 5 via `cp 6` check. Damage doubled via sla/rl with $FFFF overflow cap.

## Scenario 28: Rollout + Defense Curl Combo
**Setup:** Pokemon uses Defense Curl, then starts Rollout.
**Outcome:** Defense Curl sets SUBSTATUS_CURLED. When Rollout's damage calculation reads this flag, it effectively adds +1 to the doubling counter. So Rollout's progression becomes: 30 -> 60 -> 120 -> 240 -> 480 power equivalents instead of 30 -> 60 -> 120 -> 240 -> 480 (base power 30 with up to 16x multiplier instead of 8x). MAX_ROLLOUT_COUNT is 5 hits regardless.

unstructured_notes: Rollout has base power 30 in Gen 2. With 5 hits that becomes effective powers of 30/60/120/240/480 (capped at damage formula limits). With Defense Curl the starting multiplier is doubled. Fury Cutter base power is 10 in Gen 2. The rampage end-confusion is applied via the confuse command in the effect script but gated by Safeguard (CheckSafeguard). Sleep Talk cannot call rampage moves. Implement all three distinct rampage moves as separate move IDs sharing the same BattleCommand_Rampage implementation. The `checkrampage` command decides whether to continue or apply post-rampage confusion.

goal: Implement recoil and self-KO moves — Take Down, Double-Edge (25% recoil), Submission, Struggle, Self-Destruct/Explosion (halve defense), Destiny Bond
abstract_tasks: Implement standard recoil moves (Take Down, Double-Edge, Submission): deal damage then apply 1/4 of damage dealt as recoil to the user via the `recoil` command
Implement Struggle: typeless 50-power move, 1/4 recoil, bypasses Ghost immunity, available when all move PP is exhausted, consumes no PP of its own

Implement Self-Destruct and Explosion: set user HP to 0 before dealing damage via `selfdestruct` command, halve target's effective Defense in damage calculation (EFFECT_SELFDESTRUCT check in BattleCommand_DamageStats), clear Leech Seed from user, clear opponent's Destiny Bond
Implement Destiny Bond: set SUBSTATUS_DESTINY_BOND on user, check on faint — if user fainted from direct attack, attacker also faints; clear the substatus at end of turn via EndUserDestinyBond
Ensure Self-Destruct vs Ghost: user HP set to 0, type immunity still prevents damage but user faints regardless

- File: move_effects/selfdestruct.asm (30 lines)

### Destiny Bond (BattleCommand_DestinyBond)
- File: move_effects/destiny_bond.asm (8 lines)
- Sets SUBSTATUS_DESTINY_BOND on user
- If user faints from damage, attacker also faints

- Selfdestruct/Explosion: defense halved
- Struggle recoil: 1/4 of damage dealt
source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 2: Explosion/Self-Destruct KOs the Last Enemy
**Setup:** Player uses Explosion against the opponent's last Pokemon. Both faint.
**Outcome:** The user faints first (the move sets the user's HP to 0 via BattleCommand_Selfdestruct before dealing damage). If the target also faints, the faint check order determines the outcome. In trainer battles, if the player's Explosion KOs the last enemy Pokemon, the player wins because the enemy ran out of Pokemon. The self-destruct HP reduction happens before damage calculation; the user is considered fainted even if the move misses.

## Scenario 3: Self-Destruct/Explosion Against Ghost Type
**Setup:** Player uses Explosion against a Ghost-type Pokemon.
**Outcome:** The user's HP is set to 0, the target's Defense is halved for the calculation, but the move deals 0 damage because Normal-type moves have no effect on Ghost types. The user faints for nothing. The Defense halving still occurs even though the move will miss due to type immunity.

## Scenario 7: Destiny Bond + Pursuit
**Setup:** Pokemon A uses Destiny Bond, then switches out next turn. Pokemon B uses Pursuit as A switches.
**Outcome:** Pursuit hits with doubled damage as A switches. If A faints from Pursuit, Destiny Bond does NOT activate because Destiny Bond's effect wears off at the start of the turn before the switch happens.

## Scenario 36: Struggle Recoil
**Setup:** Pokemon is forced to use Struggle.
**Outcome:** Struggle is typeless (Normal type in Gen 2), 50 power, and inflicts 1/4 of the damage dealt as recoil to the user. Struggle has no type effectiveness — it deals neutral damage to all types including Ghost.
source_data.effect_commands_reference.md:
| `recoil` | BattleCommand_Recoil | Apply 1/4 recoil damage to user |
| `selfdestruct` | BattleCommand_Selfdestruct | Set user HP to 0, animate |
| `destinybond` | BattleCommand_DestinyBond | destiny_bond.asm |

unstructured_notes: Recoil is 1/4 of damage dealt (wCurDamage / 4). Struggle is a special case: it's assigned a typeless/Normal type but bypasses Ghost immunity in the implementation (it's the absolute fallback). Explosion halves Defense BEFORE the damage calculation runs — this is phase 6 in the formula (see damage_formula.md). The `selfdestruct` command must run before `hittarget`. Destiny Bond: the user must have set it AND been KO'd by a direct hit that same turn for it to trigger. It is NOT triggered by: indirect damage (poison, burn, Leech Seed), the opponent switching, or weather. Cleared before the user's next action.

source_data.damage_formula.md:
### Phase 6: Self-Destruct/Explosion
If the move has EFFECT_SELFDESTRUCT, the target's Defense is halved (srl c). Minimum Defense is 1.

### Phase 15: Move-Specific Modifiers
- Rollout: Damage doubles each consecutive hit (up to 5 hits = 16x). Defense Curl doubles Rollout's initial power.
- Fury Cutter: Damage doubles each consecutive hit (capped at 5 doublings = 16x base 10 = 160 power)

unstructured_notes: This QA sprint should run systematic test scenarios. For Rollout: use a Pokemon with known stats, calculate expected damage at each of 5 hits, confirm output. For Defense Curl + Rollout: confirm multiplier is 2x at each step. For rampage confusion: simulate 20+ rampage endings to confirm confusion is applied ~100% (minus Safeguard). For Explosion: create test with a Ghost type target — confirm user goes to 0 HP, target takes no damage. For Destiny Bond: verify the exact timing window (cleared at turn end, not turn start). Cross-check against battle_scenarios.md scenarios 2, 3, and 7.

source_data.status_effects.md:
### Sleep
- Duration: 1-7 turns (randomly set when inflicted)
- Rest: Sets sleep to exactly 2 turns (wakes on the 3rd turn and can act)
- Counter behavior: Decremented at the START of the Pokemon's turn (CheckTurn). When the counter reaches 0, the Pokemon wakes up AND CAN ACT that same turn.
- Switch behavior: Sleep counter is preserved on switch

unstructured_notes: Moonlight/Morning Sun/Synthesis all use the same healing amounts, just different move IDs and text. The weather check for healing uses the current wWeather value. 2/3 rounding: in integer math, 2/3 of max HP = (max_HP * 2) / 3 truncated. Rest sets sleep counter to exactly 2 (not random), clears all status (including toxic substatus), and fully restores HP. After Rest, the Pokemon sleeps exactly 2 turns, then wakes and can move on turn 3. Interact with Snore and Sleep Talk normally after Rest. The `healmorn`, `healday`, `healnite` commands are three separate battle commands that each check the current weather and apply the appropriate fraction.

source_data.status_effects.md:
### Leech Seed
- Effect: Drains 1/8 max HP per turn, heals the opponent by the same amount
- Cleared by: Switching out
- Immunity: Grass-type Pokemon cannot be Leech Seeded
- Interaction with Substitute: Cannot Leech Seed a Pokemon behind a Substitute. Draining does NOT work through a Substitute (the seeded Pokemon still loses HP, but healing is blocked if the opponent has a Sub).
- Baton Pass: Leech Seed IS passed by Baton Pass

unstructured_notes: Drain calculation: wCurDamage / 2 (integer division, minimum 1). This is applied after hittarget via `draintarget` command. Dream Eater is identical except CheckHit step 1 gates it on target being asleep. Leech Seed drain is separate: it runs in ResidualDamage each turn end, calculated as max_HP / 8 (minimum 1). If the seeded Pokemon has a Substitute, the Leech Seed still drains from the Pokemon's real HP (not the Sub), but the healing to the opponent is blocked if the opponent has a Substitute. Rapid Spin clears Leech Seed from the user. The drain moves all have their own names/power values but share the same drain mechanic implementation.

### Badly Poisoned (Toxic)

- Internal storage: Badly poisoned is stored as SUBSTATUS_TOXIC in substatus5, plus regular poison in the status byte.

goal: Implement protection moves — Protect, Detect (diminishing success), Endure (survive at 1 HP), Substitute (25% HP cost, blocks most moves)
abstract_tasks: Implement Protect and Detect using shared ProtectChance function: success threshold starts at 255, halves with each consecutive use (255 -> 127 -> 63 -> 31...); fails if user moved first this turn; counter resets on any non-protect move
Implement Endure: same ProtectChance diminishing success as Protect, sets SUBSTATUS_ENDURE; when user would be KO'd, survive with exactly 1 HP instead (ApplyDamage checks Endure before Focus Band)

Implement Substitute: costs 1/4 max HP (fails if HP not strictly above 1/4 max), creates Sub with HP = 1/4 max HP, sets SUBSTATUS_SUBSTITUTE, clears wrap/trap effects, blocks most incoming damage and most status moves
Ensure Protect is checked in CheckHit (step 2) so moves directed at the protected Pokemon fail immediately
Track consecutive protection counter per Pokemon (wPlayerProtectCount / wEnemyProtectCount), reset when any non-protecting move is used

source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 13: Protect/Detect Success Rates
**Setup:** Pokemon uses Protect on consecutive turns.
**Outcome:** First use: always succeeds. Second consecutive use: 1/3 chance (255/3 = 85, random < 85). Third consecutive: 1/7 (255/7 = 36). Fourth: 1/15. Pattern: success threshold = 255 / (2^N - 1) where N = consecutive uses. If the move fails, the counter resets.
**ASM:** wProtectCount is checked in BattleCommand_Protect. Random value compared against (255 >> ProtectCount).

## Scenario 15: Substitute Blocks Status but Not Stat Changes
**Setup:** Pokemon A has a Substitute up. Opponent uses Growl (stat-lowering move).
**Outcome:** Growl goes through the Substitute and lowers Attack. Most status moves (Thunder Wave, Toxic, etc.) are blocked by Substitute.

unstructured_notes: Protect/Detect check: the move must go last (user went second); if the user has speed priority or goes first for any reason, Protect fails. The ProtectChance formula: first use threshold is 255 (always succeeds since random < 255 is always true for 0-254). Consecutive: threshold = 255 >> ProtectCount. After 8 consecutive uses, threshold = 0, effectively always fails. Endure + residual damage: if Endure survives at 1 HP and then residual damage (Poison, Burn) would KO, Endure does NOT protect against residual damage — only direct move damage. Substitute HP cost is floor(max_HP / 4). Fails if current HP <= floor(max_HP / 4). Note: sound-based moves do NOT bypass Substitute in Gen 2 (unlike Gen 3+).

abstract_tasks: Implement Counter: priority -1, returns 2x the physical damage received this turn; check that opponent's last counter move type < SPECIAL threshold (physical types: Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel); fail if user went first, last move was Counter itself, or damage was 0

Implement Mirror Coat: priority -1, returns 2x the special damage received this turn; check that opponent's last counter move type >= SPECIAL threshold (special types: Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark); same fail conditions as Counter
Implement the item bug: wLastCounterMove and wCurDamage are NOT cleared when opponent uses an item, allowing Counter/Mirror Coat to reflect damage from a previous turn
Ensure Counter checks BattleCommand_ResetTypeMatchup and CheckOpponentWentFirst in the exact sequence documented in move_effects/counter.asm
Verify Counter/Mirror Coat interact correctly with Substitute: damage stored in wCurDamage is still set even if Sub absorbed it, so both moves still work

source_data.move_edge_cases.md:
### Counter
- Type check: Only reflects damage from physical moves (type < SPECIAL constant)
- Damage: Returns 2x the damage received from the last physical move
- Priority: -1 (always goes last in its priority bracket)
- Conditions for success:
  1. Opponent used a move last turn (wLastCounterMove is not 0)
  2. The last move was not Counter itself
  3. Type matchup is not immune (checked via BattleCommand_ResetTypeMatchup)
  4. The opponent went first this turn (CheckOpponentWentFirst)
  5. The last move had non-zero base power
  6. The last move was physical type
  7. Damage taken was non-zero (wCurDamage > 0)
- BUG: Counter still works if the opponent used an item instead of a move

### Mirror Coat
- Type check: Only reflects damage from special moves (type >= SPECIAL constant)
- Priority: -1
- Same conditions as Counter, but checks for special type instead of physical
- BUG: Same item bug as Counter

### Key Edge Cases
- Counter/Mirror Coat fail if the user moves first
- They use the damage value stored in wCurDamage, which persists from the last hit taken
- If a Substitute absorbed the damage, wCurDamage is still set
- Counter cannot reflect special moves; Mirror Coat cannot reflect physical moves
source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 16: Counter vs Mirror Coat — Wrong Type Check
**Outcome:** The type of the move determines physical/special, not the move's category tag. In Gen 2, all types are either physical or special: Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel are physical. Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark are special.

## Scenario 17: Counter/Mirror Coat After Using an Item
**Outcome:** BUG: Counter/Mirror Coat still work even if the opponent used an item instead of a move. wCurDamage and the move type byte are not reset when items are used in battle.

unstructured_notes: The physical/special split in Gen 2 is purely type-based, not a move category flag. This is critical for Counter/Mirror Coat. Normal-type, Fighting-type, Flying-type, etc. are physical. Fire-type, Water-type, etc. are special. Ghost and Steel are physical in Gen 2. Priority -1 means Counter/Mirror Coat always go last within priority tier -1 (they compete with Vital Throw and similar moves). The wLastCounterMove variable stores the move type of the last move used against the Counter/Mirror Coat user. The bug (scenario 17) must be reproduced faithfully: do NOT clear wLastCounterMove when the opponent uses an item.

source_data.bugs_and_glitches.md:
### 9. Defense-Lowering After Substitute Break
Acid, Iron Tail, and Rock Smash can lower Defense after breaking a Substitute. The secondary effect check runs even though the target's Substitute just broke. (effectchance is called twice in the move script.)

### 10. Counter/Mirror Coat Item Bug
Counter and Mirror Coat don't clear wLastCounterMove when opponent uses an item. If opponent attacks turn 1, then uses Potion turn 2, Counter on turn 2 still reflects turn-1 damage.
source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 13: Protect/Detect Success Rates
**Outcome:** First use: always succeeds. Second consecutive use: 1/3 chance (255/3 = 85, random < 85). Third consecutive: 1/7 (255/7 = 36). Fourth: 1/15. Pattern: success threshold = 255 / (2^N - 1) where N = consecutive uses.

unstructured_notes: Run at least 1000 simulated Protect uses for each consecutive-use scenario to verify the probability distribution matches the formula. For Endure + residual: specifically test the scenario where Endure saves the Pokemon at 1 HP, then the Pokemon has poison — verify it faints at end of that turn from residual. For Counter type checks: test with a Ghost-type move (physical in Gen 2) to confirm Counter reflects it, and with a Water-type move (special) to confirm Mirror Coat reflects it. ## Scenario 30: Weather + SolarBeam Interaction
**Outcome:** SolarBeam charges instantly in Sun (skips the charge turn). In Rain, SolarBeam still requires the charge turn AND its power is halved to 60. In Sandstorm, SolarBeam functions normally (2-turn charge, full power).

unstructured_notes: Weather lasts exactly 5 turns (decremented each turn in HandleWeather). At turn 0 (after final decrement) weather clears. Sandstorm immunities: Rock type, Ground type, Steel type (both type slots for dual types). The sandstorm damage is 1/16 max HP per turn (NOT 1/8 — cross-check source: battle_core_details.md mentions sandstorm damage, status_effects.md line 195+ mentions weather). Freezing cannot happen in Sun weather — BattleCommand_FreezeTarget checks WEATHER_SUN. Moonlight/Synthesis/Morning Sun healing is also weather-dependent (see sprint 58 for amounts). Thunder in Rain also has perfect accuracy via CheckHit .ThunderRain, which runs before the normal accuracy roll.

Implement Reflect: set SCREENS_REFLECT on user's side, doubles physical defense in damage calculation for 5 turns; cannot stack (fails if already active)
Implement Light Screen: set SCREENS_LIGHT_SCREEN on user's side, doubles special defense in damage calculation for 5 turns; cannot stack
Implement screen decrement via HandleScreens called each turn end (decrement both reflect/light screen counters)

Implement Safeguard: set SCREENS_SAFEGUARD for 5 turns, blocks infliction of non-volatile status (sleep, poison, burn, freeze, paralysis) on the protected side; does NOT prevent confusion, attraction, or stat drops

### Phase 2: Stat Selection and Screens
Reflect doubles the target's Defense value before stat stage application
Light Screen doubles the target's Special Defense value before stat stage application
Screens double the raw stat value (sla c; rl b in ASM), which can cause overflow past 1024 -- see Glitches section.
source_data.bugs_and_glitches.md:
### 4. Reflect / Light Screen Defense Overflow
Reflect/Light Screen double Defense/SpDef. In link battles, the overflow fix is disabled for Gold/Silver compatibility, so doubling above 1024 wraps around. Single-player has a partial fix.

unstructured_notes: Spikes switch-in check: apply 1/8 max HP damage when a non-Flying Pokemon switches in on a side with SCREENS_SPIKES. Flying-type Pokemon and Pokemon in the air (SUBSTATUS_FLYING) are immune. Baton Pass triggers Spikes on the incoming Pokemon. Rapid Spin clears Spikes from the user's side. Reflect/Light Screen screen overflow bug (bug #4): in link battles, the overflow fix is omitted — reproduce faithfully per pokecrystal source. Safeguard blocks: checksafeguard is called before sleeptarget, poisontarget, burntarget, freezetarget, and paralyzetarget commands. Safeguard does NOT block confusion (confuse command does not call checksafeguard) or attraction.

### Standard Damaging Move Script
checkobedience
usedmovetext
doturn
critical
damagestats
damagecalc
stab
damagevariation
checkhit
hittarget
failuretext
checkfaint
criticaltext

unstructured_notes: OHKO moves use EFFECT_OHKO which routes through BattleCommand_OHKO. The `ohko` command sets wCurDamage to the target's current HP, effectively guaranteeing a faint. The accuracy formula is: accuracy = max(1, user_level - target_level + 30). If user_level < target_level, the formula result is < 30 but still uses the modified accuracy. If the level check makes accuracy <= 0, the move always fails. Guillotine and Horn Drill are Normal-type physical; Fissure is Ground-type physical. Ground immunity (Flying types, Pokemon with Levitate) blocks Fissure. Ghost immunity to Normal blocks Guillotine and Horn Drill. criticaltext in the script is repurposed to print 'It's a one-hit KO!' via a special branch. The `hittarget` command still runs so the HP bar animation triggers. Semi-invulnerable states: Fissure cannot hit Fly users (Ground vs Flying special immunity), but Guillotine/Horn Drill can in principle -- verify against pokecrystal CheckHit logic.

unstructured_notes: All these moves share the constantdamage command which writes a precomputed value to wCurDamage and skips the normal damage formula. The resettypematchup command is used before hittarget to prevent STAB or type effectiveness from altering the stored damage. Night Shade is Ghost-type special; Seismic Toss is Normal-type physical — both deal level as damage. Psywave is Psychic-type; damage = BattleRandom() mod floor(level * 3 / 2) + 1 (never 0). Dragon Rage and SonicBoom use the same constant damage path. Super Fang: Normal-type, Ghosts immune. At 1 HP, floor(1/2) = 0 but minimum 1 is enforced. These moves do NOT interact with critical hits, STAB, weather, or badge boosts. Headless tests should verify Psywave hits every value in [1, floor(level*1.5)] across enough random seeds.

source_data.move_edge_cases.md:
## Baton Pass
### What IS Passed
- All stat stage changes
- Confusion (with remaining turn count)
- Focus Energy / Dire Hit
- Substitute (with remaining HP)
- Perish Song counter (continues counting down on the new Pokemon)
- Leech Seed
- Lock-On / Mind Reader
- Curse (non-Ghost, stat changes are passed; Ghost-type Curse damage IS passed as volatile status)

### What is NOT Passed
- Nightmare (explicitly cleared if the new Pokemon is not asleep)
- Disable (explicitly cleared via ResetActorDisable)
- Attraction / Infatuation (explicitly cleared for both sides)
- Transform status (cleared)
- Encore (cleared, along with its counter)
- Wrap/Bind/Fire Spin (wPlayerWrapCount and wEnemyWrapCount set to 0)
- Last move used (set to 0)
- Type changes from Conversion/Conversion2

### Edge Cases
- If the user has no other Pokemon to switch to, Baton Pass fails
- Wild Pokemon cannot use Baton Pass
- Baton Pass does trigger Spikes damage on the incoming Pokemon
- Baton Pass does NOT trigger Pursuit

## Perish Song + Mean Look Interactions
- Perish Song affects both Pokemon
- Mean Look prevents switching
- Perish Song counter IS passed by Baton Pass
- Mean Look trap is NOT passed by Baton Pass
- Classic combo: Use Mean Look, then Perish Song. The opponent is trapped and will faint in 3 turns.
source_data.move_effects_complete.md:
### Baton Pass (BattleCommand_BatonPass)
- Passes: stat levels, confusion, Focus Energy, Substitute (with HP), Mean Look trap, Perish Song counter
- Clears: Nightmare (if not asleep), Disable, Attraction, Transform, Encore, wrap counts, last move

### Perish Song (BattleCommand_PerishSong)
- Affects BOTH sides (sets SUBSTATUS_PERISH on each)
- Counter starts at 4, decremented each turn in HandlePerishSong
- Faints at 0
- Skips a side if already under Perish Song
- Fails only if both sides already have Perish Song active

### Mean Look / Spider Web (EFFECT_MEAN_LOOK)
- Sets SUBSTATUS_CANT_RUN on opponent
- Prevents switching and fleeing

unstructured_notes: The Baton Pass implementation is complex (baton_pass.asm is 215 lines in pokecrystal). The key invariant: passed substatus bits are the ones NOT in the ResetBatonPassStatus clear list. Mean Look's SUBSTATUS_CANT_RUN is intentionally NOT cleared by Baton Pass, making it the key mechanic of the Mean Look + Baton Pass combo (pass the trap to the next teammate). But note: the Mean Look entry in move_edge_cases.md says 'WAIT, NO: Actually NOT passed' — verify against the ASM. The Perish Song counter interaction with Baton Pass means the counter IS passed. The faint at 0 is handled by HandlePerishSong setting HP to 0, then the normal faint check runs. Perish Song fails only if BOTH sides already have it active. Headless test: Mean Look + Perish Song combo, then Baton Pass — new Pokemon should have Perish counter but opponent should still be trapped.

source_data.move_edge_cases.md:
## Sleep Talk
### Core Mechanics
- Can only be used while asleep
- Randomly selects one of the user's other 3 moves and executes it
- The selected move's PP is NOT consumed (Sleep Talk's PP is consumed instead)

### Exclusions (moves Sleep Talk will NOT call)
- Sleep Talk itself
- The disabled move (if any)
- Two-turn moves: Fly, Dig, Razor Wind, Sky Attack, Skull Bash, SolarBeam, Bide
- Empty move slots

### Special Interactions
- Sleep Talk CAN call Rest, but Rest fails (the Pokemon is already asleep)

## Hidden Power
### Type Calculation
TypeIndex = ((AtkDV & 3) << 2) + (DefDV & 3)
TypeIndex++ (skip Normal)
if TypeIndex >= BIRD: TypeIndex++ (skip Bird type)
if TypeIndex >= UNUSED_TYPES: TypeIndex += (UNUSED_TYPES_END - UNUSED_TYPES)

### Power Calculation
Power = ((AtkDV & 8) | ((DefDV & 8) >> 1) | ((SpdDV & 8) >> 2) | ((SpcDV & 8) >> 3)) * 5
       + (SpcDV & 3)
Power = Power / 2 + 31
Range: 31 to 70.

## Return and Frustration
### Return
Power = Happiness * 10 / 25
BUG: When happiness is 0-2, formula rounds down to 0 power.

### Frustration
Power = (255 - Happiness) * 10 / 25
BUG: When happiness is 253-255, formula rounds down to 0 power.

## Present
| Random value range | Power | Probability |
|-------------------|-------| -----------|
| 0-101 (40%) | 40 | 40% |
| 102-179 (30%) | 80 | 30% |
| 180-203 (10%) | 120 | 10% |
| 204-255 (20%) | Heal | 20% |
Healing effect: Restores 1/4 of TARGET's max HP (heals the opponent!).
source_data.move_effects_complete.md:
### Sleep Talk (BattleCommand_SleepTalk)
- File: move_effects/sleep_talk.asm (142 lines)
- Must be asleep to use
- Randomly selects one of user's other moves
- Excludes two-turn moves: Skull Bash, Razor Wind, Sky Attack, SolarBeam, Fly, Bide
- Fails if: not asleep, only has Sleep Talk, all other moves are two-turn or disabled

### Snore (BattleCommand_Snore)
- File: move_effects/snore.asm (11 lines)
- Must be asleep to use -- if not asleep, resets damage and fails
- If asleep: executes normally as a damaging move with 30% flinch chance

### Present (BattleCommand_Present)
- Random outcome from PresentPower table
- BUG: damage incorrect in link battles (uses wrong damage variable)

unstructured_notes: Sleep Talk exclusion list is critical to get right: Fly, Dig, Razor Wind, Sky Attack, Skull Bash, SolarBeam, Bide are all excluded. The selected move runs through ResetTurn which re-runs DoMove with the chosen move — this means the chosen move's full effect script executes including any secondary effects. PP for the chosen move is NOT consumed; only Sleep Talk's PP goes down. Hidden Power must correctly skip Normal type (index 0 becomes 1), the internal Bird type, and unused type slots — the final type is always one of the 16 valid non-Normal types. Return/Frustration must faithfully reproduce the 0-damage bug at extreme happiness values. Present's heal branch heals the TARGET (opponent!), ignoring type immunity — this is the intended behavior and must not be filtered by Ghost type immunity.

## Baton Pass
### What IS Passed
- All stat stage changes, Confusion, Focus Energy, Substitute (with HP), Perish Song counter, Leech Seed, Lock-On, Curse flag
### What is NOT Passed
- Nightmare, Disable, Attraction, Transform, Encore, Wrap counts, Last move used, Type changes
### Edge Cases
- Baton Pass does NOT trigger Pursuit
- Baton Pass triggers Spikes on incoming Pokemon

## Sleep Talk Exclusions
- Two-turn moves: Fly, Dig, Razor Wind, Sky Attack, Skull Bash, SolarBeam, Bide
- Disabled moves, empty slots, Sleep Talk itself

## Hidden Power
TypeIndex = ((AtkDV & 3) << 2) + (DefDV & 3), then skip Normal and Bird
Power = (top bits of 4 DVs * 5 + SpcDV & 3) / 2 + 31, range [31,70]

## Scenario 1: Both Pokemon Faint to Perish Song
Whoever is checked first faints. Player's faint is checked first in most cases.
## Scenario 5: Baton Pass Chain with Stat Boosts
ResetBatonPassStatus clears: Nightmare, Disable, Attraction, Transform, Encore, Wrap counts, and last move used.
## Scenario 27: Mean Look + Baton Pass
Mean Look sets SUBSTATUS_CANT_RUN which Baton Pass does NOT clear.

unstructured_notes: Key discrepancy to audit: move_edge_cases.md has a comment 'WAIT, NO: Actually NOT passed' for Mean Look, but battle_scenarios.md Scenario 27 says it IS passed. Cross-check against pokecrystal baton_pass.asm to resolve. The authoritative source is the ASM. Scenario 27 states SUBSTATUS_CANT_RUN is NOT cleared by ResetBatonPassStatus, making it passed by default. This is counter-intuitive and must be verified in tests. For Hidden Power: DVs {15,15,15,15} → TypeIndex = (15&3)<<2|(15&3) = 12+3=15, skip Normal →16, check Bird skip, check unused types skip. Power bits = (1|1|1|1)*5 + (15&3) = 5*5+3 = 28, /2+31 = 45. Run this calculation through the engine.

## Transform, Mimic, and Sketch
### Transform
- Copies: species (appearance), types, stats (except HP), stat stages, moves (all 4), current PP set to 5
- Does NOT copy: HP, level, DVs, status conditions
- BUG: Catching a Transformed Pokemon always catches a Ditto
- BUG: A Transformed Pokemon CAN use Sketch to learn moves (checks opponent instead of user)

### Mimic
- Copies target's last-used move, replacing Mimic in moveset (battle-only)
- PP set to move's normal max PP

### Sketch
- Permanently copies target's last-used move
- Cannot copy Struggle or Sketch itself
- Fails if target hasn't used a move

## Metronome
- Cannot call: Metronome, Struggle, Sketch, Mimic, Sleep Talk, Counter, Mirror Coat, Protect, Detect, Endure, Destiny Bond, Thief
- Selected move uses Metronome's PP (not the called move's PP)
- Can call two-turn moves

### Sketch (BattleCommand_Sketch)
- File: move_effects/sketch.asm (117 lines)
- Permanently replaces Sketch with opponent's last used move
- Copies base PP from move data
- Updates party struct (permanent change)
- Fails in link battles (always fails)
- BUG: Transformed Pokemon can Sketch and learn unobtainable moves

## Metronome Exceptions
Metronome, Struggle, Sketch, Mimic, Counter, Mirror Coat, Protect, Detect, Endure, Destiny Bond, Sleep Talk, Thief

unstructured_notes: Transform in pokecrystal copies DVs from the target, which is relevant for Hidden Power type — a Transformed Pokemon's Hidden Power will be based on the TARGET's DVs. The Transform stat copy copies stats including current stat stages. Mirror Move executes as if the user used the opponent's last move but the user's stats and type are used for calculations. Sketch's permanence means it must write through to the party struct before the battle ends. The Transform+Sketch bug is interesting: the code checks SUBSTATUS_TRANSFORMED on the opponent (checking if the target is transformed) instead of checking if the SELF is transformed. This allows a Transformed Smeargle to Sketch the opponent's move. Metronome's ResetTurn re-executes DoMove entirely, so the called move's full effect (including multi-turn setup) runs. Mimic PP: the mimicked move uses the move's own base max PP (not 5 like Transform).

## Baton Pass
### What is NOT Passed
- Wrap/Bind/Fire Spin (wPlayerWrapCount and wEnemyWrapCount set to 0)

## Rapid Spin
- Removes: Leech Seed, Spikes (from user's side), Bind/Wrap/Clamp/Fire Spin/Whirlpool
- Also deals damage (20 base power, Normal type)
- The removal effects happen even if the damage portion misses

unstructured_notes: Wrap-family moves (Wrap, Bind, Fire Spin, Whirlpool, Clamp) all use EFFECT_TRAP which calls traptarget. The duration is 2-5 turns stored in wPlayerWrapCount or wEnemyWrapCount (the count includes the current turn). The 1/16 HP damage per turn happens in HandleBetweenTurnEffects; min damage is 1. The wrap also prevents the trapped Pokemon from switching or fleeing, similar to Mean Look but it's stored differently (wrap count > 0 implies trapped). Mean Look uses a separate SUBSTATUS_CANT_RUN bit. Key difference: wrap is cleared when the trapper switches out (since the trapper is gone), whereas Mean Look persists even if the user switches. The turn count display in the Psych Up message should be correct. Wrapping through Substitute: the traptarget command checks for Substitute and fails if up. Once the Substitute breaks, the Pokemon is not wrapped because the wrap check failed at application.

### Belly Drum (BattleCommand_BellyDrum)
- Costs 50% max HP, maxes Attack to +6
- BUG: raises attack even if under 50% HP (calls AttackUp2 before HP check succeeds)

### Psych Up (BattleCommand_PsychUp)
- Copies all 7 stat levels from opponent to user
- Fails if opponent has no modified stats (all at base level 7)
- Recalculates stats after copying

### Magnitude (BattleCommand_GetMagnitude)
- M4:10(5%), M5:30(10%), M6:50(20%), M7:70(30%), M8:90(20%), M9:110(10%), M10:150(5%)

### Reversal / Flail (EFFECT_REVERSAL)
- <=1 pixel: 200, <=4: 150, <=9: 100, <=16: 80, <=32: 40, else: 20
- Uses FlailReversalPower lookup table (48-pixel HP bar)

### Pain Split (BattleCommand_PainSplit)
- Averages both Pokemon's current HP
- Each Pokemon's HP capped at its own max HP

### Curse (BattleCommand_Curse)
- Ghost-type user: costs 50% max HP, sets SUBSTATUS_CURSE on target (1/4 HP damage per turn)
- Non-Ghost user: +1 Attack, +1 Defense, -1 Speed

### Conversion (BattleCommand_Conversion)
- Changes user's type to match one of its moves' types
- Fails if no valid type available

### Conversion2 (BattleCommand_Conversion2)
- Changes user's type to resist opponent's last used move
- Fails if: missed, no last move, last move was CURSE_TYPE

### Spite (BattleCommand_Spite)
- Reduces PP of opponent's last used move by 2-5 (random & 3, +2)
- Respects PP_MASK
- Fails if: missed, no last move, Struggle, 0 PP
source_data.move_edge_cases.md:
## Belly Drum
- BUG: The game checks Attack boost success BEFORE checking HP. If Attack can be boosted, it first does a +2 boost, then checks HP. If HP < 50%, the +2 boost STILL APPLIES but the move fails.

## Pain Split
- Averages both Pokemon's current HP: each gets (user HP + target HP) / 2
- If total is odd, user gets the smaller half
- Can raise HP above current (but not above max)
- Ignores type immunity and Substitute

## Psych Up
- Copies ALL of target's stat stage changes
- Does NOT copy: Focus Energy, type changes, or other volatile conditions
- Overwrites user's current stat stages entirely
source_data.bugs_and_glitches.md:
### 6. Belly Drum HP Glitch
Belly Drum calls AttackUp2 BEFORE checking if user has 50% HP. If Attack can be boosted but HP is below 50%, the +2 Attack boost happens free without HP cost, then 'But it failed!' prints.

unstructured_notes: Eight moves in one sprint — this is the densest batch. Prioritize Belly Drum bug (most tricky), Curse ghost detection (must check both type slots), and Magnitude (needs the exact probability table). Flail/Reversal converts current HP to 48-pixel bar scale: pixels = current_hp * 48 / max_hp, then look up FlailReversalPower table with pixel thresholds (<=1, <=4, <=9, <=16, <=32, else). Pain Split: if sum is odd, integer division gives user the floor value (smaller half). Conversion should pick randomly from valid move types each time — if first attempt is already held type or CURSE_TYPE, continue random until finding valid or fail. Conversion2 randomly generates types until finding one that resists the opponent's last move type. Spite's PP_MASK ensures the PP-Up bits (upper 3 bits of PP byte) are preserved during reduction.

source_data.move_edge_cases.md:
## Beat Up
- Hits once for each non-fainted, non-statused Pokemon in the user's party
- Each hit uses the base Attack of the party member and a fixed base power
- Type: Dark (all hits)
- BUG: With only one Pokemon in the party, Beat Up fails to raise Substitute and King's Rock doesn't proc
- BUG: Can desynchronize link battles
- BUG: May trigger King's Rock even if all hits failed

## Future Sight
- Delay: Stored damage delivered 3 turns later (counter starts at 4, triggers at 1)
- Damage calculation: Uses user's stats at time of USING Future Sight
- Type effectiveness: Ignored on delivery
- Cannot stack: Using Future Sight again while pending fails
- Cannot be reflected by Counter/Mirror Coat
source_data.bugs_and_glitches.md:
### 13. Lock-On/Mind Reader vs Fly/Dig
Lock-On/Mind Reader guarantee next move hits, but CheckHiddenOpponent doesn't check Lock-On substatus. Status moves (Attract, Curse, Foresight, Mean Look, Mimic, Nightmare, Spider Web, Transform, stat-lowering) still miss during Fly/Dig semi-invulnerable turns.

unstructured_notes: This is the final move effects sprint completing all 251 moves. Beat Up is the most complex: it loops through the party array, hitting once per eligible member. In Rust, this means iterating party slots 0..5, skipping fainted (HP=0) and statused (non-zero status byte) Pokemon. The base power for each hit is the base Attack of the party member's SPECIES (from base stats), not the battle stat. Future Sight's damage is pre-computed using the caster's stats at cast time — the damage stored must survive stat stage resets and user switching. Lock-On bug: status moves still miss through Fly/Dig even with Lock-On (the CheckHiddenOpponent check doesn't consult Lock-On substatus for status moves). Rapid Spin clearance happens AFTER dealing damage, and the text is printed for each hazard cleared. Thief in trainer battles: the stolen item is returned to the opponent after battle (not in Rust scope right now, but note it).

unstructured_notes: This is the final QA for Phase 4 (Move Effects), covering all 251 moves. The completeness check is critical: every EFFECT_* constant defined in pokecrystal must map to a handler. Any unimplemented effect ID must result in a controlled failure state (not an engine panic) or be explicitly confirmed as unreachable. Key bugs to reproduce faithfully: Belly Drum HP glitch (the +2 Atk always happens if Atk is not maxed, regardless of HP), Beat Up single-Pokemon Substitute failure (one hit at 0 power or some encoding issue), Lock-On not helping status moves through Fly/Dig. Future Sight timing: the counter is set to 4 on use, decremented at END of each turn, so it fires at the start of turn-4 end-of-turn processing (3 full turns pass before delivery). Confirm Future Sight cannot stack — a second use while one is pending should print 'But it failed!'

abstract_tasks: Implement AI score array: 4 entries (one per move), each initialized to base score 20; disabled/0-PP moves initialized to 80
Implement score modification API: encourage (-1), greatly encourage (-2), discourage (+1), greatly discourage (+2), AIDiscourageMove (+10); use as BattleScoreLayer trait/enum in Rust
Implement AIScoringPointers dispatch table: 8 layers indexed by trainer AI flags; each trainer class specifies a bitmask of active layers
Implement lowest-score selection with random tiebreak: after all layers run, decrement each move score round-robin until one reaches 0; choose randomly among tied lowest
Implement AI probability helpers: AI_50_50 (50% chance via BattleRandom() < 128), AI_80_20 (80% chance via BattleRandom() < 205)
Implement layer-0 base initialization: set all 4 move scores to 20; set score 80 for any move with 0 PP or disabled; reject moves with no effect (empty move slot)
Write headless test: 4 moves with no layer modifiers, verify random selection among all 4; 3 moves at score 20 one at 80, verify 80-scored move is never selected

source_data.ai_scoring_details.md:
## AI System Overview
### Score System
- Each of the enemy's 4 moves starts with a base score of 20
- Disabled or 0-PP moves get score 80 (effectively never chosen)
- Up to 16 AI scoring layers applied based on trainer class attributes
- Each layer can increment (discourage) or decrement (encourage) a move's score
- After all layers, scores are decremented round-robin until one reaches 0
- Ties broken by re-incrementing partially decremented scores
- A random move among the lowest-scoring moves is chosen
- Lower score = better move

### Score Modification Conventions
- dec [hl] = encourage (-1)
- inc [hl] = discourage (+1)
- dec [hl] x2 = greatly encourage (-2)
- inc [hl] x2 = greatly discourage (+2)
- AIDiscourageMove = add 10 to score (essentially dismiss)
- dec [hl] x5 = maximally encourage (used by AI_Risky)

### Probability Helpers
- AI_50_50: 50% chance (returns carry)
- AI_80_20: 80% chance
- BattleRandom: generates random byte 0-255
source_data.ai_behavior.md:
## AI Architecture Overview
The AI system works through scoring layers. Each AI layer adjusts a score for each of the enemy's 4 moves. Lower scores = more likely to be chosen. Each move starts with a base score, and layers add/subtract from it.

### AI Layer Assignment
| Layer | Constant | Function | Description |
|-------|----------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | AI_BASIC | AI_Basic | Dismiss redundant moves |
| 2 | AI_SETUP | AI_Setup | Encourage setup on turn 1 |
| 3 | AI_TYPES | AI_Types | Consider type effectiveness |
| 4 | AI_OFFENSIVE | AI_Offensive | Discourage status moves |
| 5 | AI_SMART | AI_Smart | Context-specific scoring |
| 6 | AI_CAUTIOUS | AI_Cautious | Risk-averse behavior |
| 7 | AI_STATUS | AI_Status | Encourage status moves |
| 8 | AI_RISKY | AI_Risky | High-risk high-reward behavior |

unstructured_notes: The AI scoring architecture is the foundation for sprints 80-90. The Rust implementation should represent the score array as a [u8; 4] or [i16; 4] (i16 safer for additive operations). The 'round-robin decrement until one reaches 0' is the pokecrystal selection algorithm: start from score[0], decrement each score by 1 in order, wrapping around, until a score reaches 0; that move is selected. Ties occur when multiple moves have the same minimum score — the algorithm naturally picks the first one found in the round-robin, but the random tiebreak implies a second pass. Trainer class data stores the layer bitmask as part of trainer attributes in the trainer data structure. The 8 layers are a bitmask (bit 0 = AI_BASIC, bit 1 = AI_SETUP, etc.). The AI does NOT run for the player. Disable/0-PP score of 80 makes the move 60 discourages above base — effectively unreachable by decrement unless all other moves are also highly discouraged.

goal: Implement AI_Basic (layer 1) — dismiss redundant moves, status on statused target, Safeguard blocking, healing at full HP
abstract_tasks: Implement base score initialization: all 4 moves start at 20; disabled/0-PP moves get score 80
Implement AI_Basic status-on-statused-target check: if target already has non-volatile status, add +5 penalty to pure-status move effects (Sleep, Toxic, Poison, Paralyze, Confuse, etc.)
Implement AI_Basic Safeguard check: if target is behind Safeguard, dismiss (AIDiscourageMove +10) all status-inflicting move effects

Implement AI_Redundant table: check each of the 29 listed redundant conditions (Light Screen already up, already seeded, already confused, Transform already active, etc.) and AIDiscourageMove (+10) when redundant
Implement move selection final step: decrement all scores round-robin until one reaches 0, re-increment for tie resolution, randomly pick among tied minimum-score moves

## AI_Basic (Layer 1)
Applied to almost all trainers. Prevents obviously stupid moves:
- Redundant moves: Calls AI_Redundant to check if a move would be useless
- Status on statused target: If the target already has a non-volatile status condition, dismiss pure status moves
- Safeguard blocking: If the target is behind Safeguard, dismiss status moves
Effect: Adds a large penalty (via AIDiscourageMove) to make the move very unlikely.
source_data.ai_scoring_details.md:
### Layer 1: AI_Basic
- Dismiss status-only moves if the player already has a status condition
- Dismiss status moves if the player has Safeguard active
- Uses AI_Redundant to check if a move's effect is already in play

### Score System
- Each of the enemy's 4 moves starts with a base score of 20
- Disabled or 0-PP moves get score 80
- AIDiscourageMove = add 10 to score (essentially dismiss)

### AI Redundancy Checks (redundant.asm)
Checks if a move effect is already active/useless:
| Effect | Redundant When |
|--------|---------------|
| Light Screen | Already active on enemy side |
| Mist | Already has Mist substatus |
| Focus Energy | Already has Focus Energy |
| Confuse | Player already confused OR has Safeguard |
| Transform | Already transformed |
| Reflect | Already active on enemy side |
| Substitute | Already has Substitute |
| Leech Seed | Player already seeded |
| Disable | Player already disabled |
| Encore | Player already encored |
| Snore/Sleep Talk | Enemy NOT asleep (redundant) |
| Mean Look | Player already trapped |
| Nightmare | Player not asleep OR already has Nightmare |
| Spikes | Already have Spikes on player side |
| Foresight | Player already identified |
| Perish Song | Player already has Perish count |
| Sandstorm | Weather already Sandstorm |
| Attract | Can't attract OR player already attracted |
| Safeguard | Already active on enemy side |
| Rain Dance | Weather already Rain |
| Sunny Day | Weather already Sun |
| Dream Eater | Player NOT asleep |
| Swagger | Player already confused |
| Future Sight | BUG: checks SCREENS_UNUSED bit |
| Heal/Morning Sun/Synthesis/Moonlight | Enemy HP already full |
| Teleport | Always redundant (NPC trainers can't flee) |

- Status-only effects discouraged if foe already has status: Sleep, Toxic, Poison, Paralyze effects get +5 penalty

unstructured_notes: AI_Basic is the foundational layer applied to virtually every trainer. The key is calling AI_Redundant for each move before checking status-on-status and Safeguard. The redundant.asm table has exactly 29 entries matching pokecrystal — the Future Sight one is a known bug (checks SCREENS_UNUSED bit instead of future sight pending count) and must be faithfully reproduced. The score floor is 1 (never 0 after the round-robin decrement unless it is the selected move). Wild Pokemon bypass all AI layers and pick completely at random — that short-circuit must go first in AIChooseMove.

source_data.ai_scoring_details.md:
### AIChooseMove
1. Wild Pokemon: attack completely at random (return immediately)
2. Link battles: return (link opponent handles their own moves)
3. If locked in (Rollout, Outrage, etc.): return
4. Initialize all 4 scores to 20; disabled/no-PP moves get 80
5. Apply each enabled AI layer from TrainerClassAttributes
6. Decrement all scores round-robin until one reaches 0
7. Re-increment to resolve ties
8. Zero out all non-minimum scores
9. Randomly pick among tied minimum-score moves

unstructured_notes: QA sprint for the AI architecture foundation. The scoring round-robin is subtle: all scores are decremented by 1 in sequence until one hits 0. That move is selected unless another already-at-0 exists — then re-increment to find ties. The final random pick must be uniform. Check that no trainer with 0 AI layers assigned crashes (should just use all 4 moves randomly or fall through to some default). Confirm layer bit flags are correctly read from trainer class attributes.

goal: Implement AI_Setup (layer 2) and AI_Types (layer 3) — encourage setup turn 1, type effectiveness scoring, immune move penalty
abstract_tasks: Implement AI_Setup: on enemy Pokemon's first turn, 50% chance to greatly encourage (-2) stat-up moves; on player's first turn, 50% chance to greatly encourage (-2) stat-down moves; after turn 1, ~88% chance to greatly discourage (+2) all stat-modifying moves
Implement AI_Types type immunity dismissal: call CheckTypeMatchup for each damaging move; if result is immune (0 effectiveness), add AIDiscourageMove (+10)
Implement AI_Types super-effective encouragement: if type matchup result is super-effective, decrease score by 1 (-1)

Implement AI_Types not-very-effective discouragement: if type matchup is not-very-effective AND the enemy has at least one other damaging move of a different type, increase score by 1 (+1); if all damaging moves share the same type, skip this penalty
Track turn counters: enemy Pokemon first-turn flag and player Pokemon first-turn flag needed by AI_Setup

source_data.ai_behavior.md:
### AI_Setup (Layer 2)
Encourages stat-modifying moves on turn 1:
- Stat-up moves: 50% chance to strongly encourage (-2 to score) on FIRST turn of the enemy Pokemon
- Stat-down moves: 50% chance to strongly encourage on the first turn of the PLAYER's Pokemon
- After turn 1, ~88% chance to greatly discourage (+2 to score)

### AI_Types (Layer 3)
Considers type matchups:
- Immune moves: Heavily penalized (AIDiscourageMove)
- Super effective moves: Score decreased by 1
- Not very effective moves: Score increased by 1 IF the enemy has another move of a different type that deals damage
- Neutral moves: No change
source_data.ai_scoring_details.md:
### Layer 2: AI_Setup
- 50% chance to greatly encourage stat-up moves on enemy's first turn
- 50% chance to greatly encourage stat-down moves on player's first turn
- ~88% chance to greatly discourage stat-modifying moves on later turns

### Layer 3: AI_Types
- Dismiss immune moves (add 10)
- Encourage super-effective damaging moves (-1)
- Discourage not-very-effective damaging moves (+1) ONLY if the enemy has at least one damaging move of a different type

### Probability Helpers
- AI_50_50: 50% chance (returns carry)
- AI_80_20: 80% chance (returns carry ~20% of the time, so 80% proceeds)
- BattleRandom: generates random byte 0-255

unstructured_notes: AI_Setup is designed to reward trainers for using setup moves on the opening turn and punish them for wasting setup on later turns. The ~88% discouragement figure comes from comparing BattleRandom against $E0 (224/256 ≈ 87.5%). For AI_Types, the 'different type damaging move' check requires iterating all 4 moves — if all four damaging moves are the same type, the not-very-effective penalty is skipped because the AI has no better type option. Note: AI_Types only modifies scores for damaging moves (base power > 0). Status moves are not scored by AI_Types.

source_data.ai_scoring_details.md:
### Layer 4: AI_Offensive
- Greatly discourage (+2) any move with 0 base power

### Layer 5: AI_Smart
Purpose: Context-specific per-effect scoring. The most complex layer with 80+ effect handlers.

### Sleep Moves (EFFECT_SLEEP)
- If enemy has Dream Eater or Nightmare: 50% chance to greatly encourage (-2)
- Otherwise: 50% chance to greatly encourage (-2)

### Selfdestruct/Explosion (EFFECT_SELFDESTRUCT)
- If enemy's last mon AND player's last mon: don't discourage
- If enemy's last mon AND player has others: greatly discourage (+3)
- If enemy HP > 50%: greatly discourage (+3)
- If enemy HP < 25%: do nothing
- If enemy HP 25-50%: ~92% chance to greatly discourage (+3)

### Counter (EFFECT_COUNTER)
- Count player's known physical damaging moves
- 3+ physical moves: ~60% chance to encourage (-1)
- Last player move was physical damaging: ~60% chance to encourage (-1)
- No physical moves: discourage (+1)

### Mirror Coat (EFFECT_MIRROR_COAT)
- Same as Counter but checks for special (type >= SPECIAL) moves

### Encore (EFFECT_ENCORE)
- Enemy slower: greatly discourage (+3)
- Player's last move is in EncoreMoves list: ~72% chance to greatly encourage (-2)
- Otherwise: greatly discourage (+3)

### Mean Look (EFFECT_MEAN_LOOK)
- Enemy HP < 50%: discourage (+1)
- Player is last mon: dismiss (+10)
- BUG: Checks enemy's own toxic status instead of player's

### Conversion2 (EFFECT_CONVERSION2)
- BUG: Checks wLastPlayerMove but condition discourages WHEN player HAS used a move (backwards)

### Sunny Day (EFFECT_SUNNY_DAY)
- BUG: Does NOT encourage Sunny Day with SolarBeam, Flame Wheel, or Moonlight

### Rain Dance / Encourage list:
Water Gun, Hydro Pump, Surf, BubbleBeam, Thunder, Waterfall, Clamp, Bubble, Crabhammer, Octazooka, Whirlpool

### Sunny Day / Encourage list:
Fire Punch, Ember, Flamethrower, Fire Spin, Fire Blast, Sacred Fire, Morning Sun, Synthesis

### Encore encourage list (EncoreMoves):
Swords Dance, Whirlwind, Leer, Roar, Disable, Mist, Leech Seed, Growth, PoisonPowder, String Shot, Meditate, Agility, Teleport, Screech, Haze, Focus Energy, Dream Eater, Poison Gas, Splash, Sharpen, Conversion, Super Fang, Substitute, Triple Kick, Spider Web, Mind Reader, Flame Wheel, Aeroblast, Cotton Spore, Powder Snow

unstructured_notes: AI_Smart is the largest layer with ~80 effect handlers. Two known bugs must be faithfully reproduced: (1) Mean Look checks the AI's own toxic status flag instead of the player's, causing it to encourage Mean Look when the AI's own Pokemon is poisoned. (2) Conversion2 condition is backwards — it discourages when the player HAS used a move rather than when they haven't. Both are documented in ai_behavior.md bugs summary. The Sunny Day synergy bug (missing SolarBeam/Flame Wheel/Moonlight) must also be reproduced. The Rain Dance encourage list and Sunny Day encourage list are hardcoded move-ID tables in the ASM — implement them as static lookup tables.

### Layer 6: AI_Opportunist
- If HP < 25%: discourage stall moves (+1)
- If HP 25-50%: 50% chance to discourage stall moves (+1)
- Stall moves defined in data/battle/ai/stall_moves.asm

### Layer 7: AI_Aggressive
- Calculates actual damage for each damaging move (using AIDamageCalc)
- Discourages (+1) all damaging moves except the one that does the most damage
- Ignores moves with power 0-1 (Seismic Toss, Counter, etc.)
- Ignores reckless moves (Selfdestruct, etc.) from the comparison

### Layer 8: AI_Cautious
- 90% chance to discourage (+1) residual-effect moves after the first turn
- Residual moves defined in data/battle/ai/residual_moves.asm
- BUG: The ret nc after the random check should be jr nc, .loop, causing early return without checking all moves

### Layer 9: AI_Status
- Dismiss Toxic/Poison against Poison-type targets
- Dismiss any status or damaging move with 0 type matchup (immune)

### Layer 10: AI_Risky
- If damage >= player's remaining HP: maximally encourage (-5)
- Risky moves (Self-Destruct effects) at max HP: skip or 80% chance to skip

### Score Modification Conventions
- dec [hl] x5 = maximally encourage (used by AI_Risky for KO moves)

AIDamageCalc in AI_Aggressive and AI_Risky must use the same damage formula as actual battles but with approximate stat values from trainer party data. The layer 8 ret-nc bug is specifically that after a failed 90% random check, the function returns from the entire layer instead of skipping just that move.

source_data.ai_behavior.md:
## AI Switching (engine/battle/ai/switch.asm)
The AI considers switching when:
- Its Pokemon is at a significant type disadvantage
- Its Pokemon has been badly statused
- A party member has a much better type matchup

Switch decision factors:
- Type effectiveness of the player's last move against current Pokemon
- HP remaining on the current Pokemon
- Whether a party member resists/is immune to the player's moves
- Whether the current Pokemon is trapped (Mean Look, etc.)

In practice, most regular trainers rarely switch. Gym Leaders and E4 members with AI_SMART are more likely to switch strategically.
source_data.ai_scoring_details.md:
## AI Switch Logic (switch.asm)

### CheckAbleToSwitch
1. Whether the enemy has alive mons to switch to
2. Perish Song at count 1: always try to switch
3. Player has super-effective moves: try to switch
4. Search for a mon that: has > 25% HP, resists the player, has a super-effective move

### Switch Frequency (from trainer attributes)
| Attribute | Low Priority (score $10) | Med Priority ($20) | High Priority ($30) |
|-----------|--------------------------|---------------------|---------------------|
| SwitchOften | 50% switch | 79% switch | 96% switch |
| SwitchSometimes | 20% switch | 50% switch | 80% switch |
| SwitchRarely | 8% switch | 12% switch | 79% switch |

### Switch Candidate Selection
1. FindAliveEnemyMons -- find all non-fainted mons
2. FindEnemyMonsWithAtLeastQuarterMaxHP -- filter to > 25% HP
3. FindEnemyMonsThatResistPlayer -- filter to those resisting player's shown moves
4. FindAliveEnemyMonsWithASuperEffectiveMove -- further filter to those with SE moves

unstructured_notes: The switch score ($10/$20/$30 priority tiers) comes from how urgently the situation demands a switch: $10 is marginal (player has SE move), $20 is moderate (both SE move and type disadvantage), $30 is critical (Perish Song count 1 or severe type disadvantage). The switch candidate selection is a cascading filter — if no candidate passes all four filters, the AI does not switch. The trap check (Mean Look, Wrap, Spider Web, etc.) blocks switching entirely for the trapped Pokemon. Regular trainers without SwitchOften or SwitchSometimes effectively never switch in practice.

source_data.ai_behavior.md:
## AI Item Usage (engine/battle/ai/items.asm)
Trainers can be configured to use items. The AI checks conditions before using each item type:

### Healing Items
- Potion / Super Potion / Hyper Potion / Max Potion / Full Restore: Used when HP drops below a threshold
- Full Restore also heals status conditions

### Status Items
- Full Heal: Used when the Pokemon has a non-volatile status condition
- BUG: Doesn't cure Nightmare or confusion
- BUG: Doesn't restore Attack/Speed drops from burn/paralysis

### Stat Items
- X Attack, X Defend, X Speed, X Special, X Accuracy, Dire Hit: Used strategically
- Guard Spec: Prevents stat drops

### Usage Limits
- Each trainer has a fixed set of items they can use
- Items are consumed when used (can't be used again)
- The AI uses items INSTEAD of attacking (costs the turn)
- BUG: The AI might try to use its base reward money as an item index
source_data.ai_scoring_details.md:
## AI Item Usage (items.asm)

### AI_TryItem
- Not used in Battle Tower (items banned)
- Checks wEnemyTrainerItem1 and wEnemyTrainerItem2
- Only uses items on the highest-level party member
- Looks up item in AI_Items table
- Each item has a condition check and use routine
- BUG: Base reward value can accidentally be read as an item due to pointer math

### AI Item Categories
Trainers carry up to 2 items, used based on HP thresholds and status conditions:
- Full Heal: used when current mon is statused
- Full Restore: used when HP is critical (varies by trainer)
- Potions: used at various HP thresholds
- X Items: used to boost stats

unstructured_notes: The 'only use items on the highest-level party member' rule is quirky — it means the AI only heals or boosts its ace Pokemon, not a weakened lower-level slot member. This effectively buffers the AI's strongest Pokemon at the cost of fairness. Battle Tower explicitly disables items (items banned), so AI_TryItem must check if it's a BT battle and skip immediately. The reward-money-as-item bug is a pointer arithmetic error in the ASM — the fix would be a bounds check, but pokecrystal does not have it, so we must not add one.

## Held Item Battle Effects
### Consumable Effects
- HELD_BERRY (Berry, Gold Berry) — Auto-heal HP
- HELD_HEAL_POISON (PSNCureBerry) — Auto-cure poison
- HELD_HEAL_FREEZE (Burnt Berry) — Auto-cure freeze
- HELD_HEAL_BURN (Ice Berry) — Auto-cure burn
- HELD_HEAL_SLEEP (Mint Berry) — Auto-cure sleep
- HELD_HEAL_PARALYZE (PRZCureBerry) — Auto-cure paralysis
- HELD_HEAL_STATUS (MiracleBerry) — Auto-cure any status
- HELD_ESCAPE (Smoke Ball) — Guaranteed escape

### Stat-Up Items (held_stat_up.asm)
| Held Effect | Stat |
|------------|------|
| HELD_ATTACK_UP | Attack |
| HELD_DEFENSE_UP | Defense |
| HELD_SPEED_UP | Speed |
| HELD_SP_ATTACK_UP | Sp. Attack |
| HELD_SP_DEFENSE_UP | Sp. Defense |
| HELD_ACCURACY_UP | Accuracy |
| HELD_EVASION_UP | Evasion |

unstructured_notes: These data tables are the ground truth for all stat and accuracy calculations during battle. The accuracy multiplier table is deliberately different from the stat multiplier table — they do NOT share the same values. The critical hit cap at stage 4+ = 1/2 means Lucky Punch Chansey (base +2) with Focus Energy (+1) and Dire Hit (+1) reaches stage 4 and caps at 50%. The weather modifier system uses the same MORE_EFFECTIVE / NOT_VERY_EFFECTIVE encoding as the type chart — it stacks multiplicatively with type effectiveness.
