source_data.overworld_engine.md:
## Main Loop (events.asm)

### OverworldLoop
Three-state machine driven by wMapStatus:
1. MAPSTATUS_START (StartMap) -- Clear state, init phone delay, run map setup
2. MAPSTATUS_ENTER (EnterMap) -- Run map setup script, set 5-step wild encounter cooldown
3. MAPSTATUS_HANDLE (HandleMap) -- Main per-frame processing:
   - ResetOverworldDelay (2 frames max)
   - HandleMapTimeAndJoypad (UpdateTime, GetJoypad, TimeOfDayPals)
   - HandleCmdQueue (queued commands)
   - MapEvents (PlayerEvents + ScriptEvents)
   - HandleMapObjects (NPC step + player step + object visibility)
   - NextOverworldFrame (delay remaining)
   - HandleMapBackground (sprites, scrolling, map name sign)
   - CheckPlayerState (enable/disable events based on step flags)

### Player Event Priority (PlayerEvents)
Checked in order; first match wins:
1. CheckTrainerEvent -- Trainer line of sight
2. CheckTileEvent -- Warps, connections, coord events, step counting, wild encounters
3. RunMemScript -- Queued reentry scripts
4. RunSceneScript -- Scene-based scripts (wCurMapSceneScriptCount)
5. CheckTimeEvents -- Bug Contest timer, daily reset, Pokerus tick, phone calls
6. OWPlayerInput -- A-button (talk/interact), Start (menu), Select (registered item)

### Enabled Player Events (wEnabledPlayerEvents)
Bit flags controlling which events are active:
- Bit 0: PLAYEREVENTS_WARPS_AND_CONNECTIONS
- Bit 1: PLAYEREVENTS_COORD_EVENTS
- Bit 2: PLAYEREVENTS_COUNT_STEPS
- Bit 3: PLAYEREVENTS_WILD_ENCOUNTERS
- Bit 5: PLAYEREVENTS_UNUSED

### Player Event Types (PLAYEREVENT_*)
| 0 | PLAYEREVENT_NONE | No event |
| 1 | PLAYEREVENT_SEENBYTRAINER | Spotted by trainer |
| 2 | PLAYEREVENT_TALKTOTRAINER | Talked to trainer |
| 3 | PLAYEREVENT_ITEMBALL | Found item ball |
| 4 | PLAYEREVENT_CONNECTION | Map connection |
| 5 | PLAYEREVENT_WARP | Warp tile |
| 6 | PLAYEREVENT_FALL | Pit/fall hole |
| 7 | PLAYEREVENT_WHITEOUT | White out |
| 8 | PLAYEREVENT_HATCH | Egg hatching |
| 9 | PLAYEREVENT_JOYCHANGEFACING | Direction change |

unstructured_notes: This sprint begins Phase 6: Overworld Engine. The 3-state map machine is the outermost architectural structure of the entire overworld — everything else is called from MAPSTATUS_HANDLE. The priority chain order is critical: trainer line-of-sight firing before tile events means a trainer can intercept the player before a warp fires. The 5-step wild encounter cooldown set on MAPSTATUS_ENTER prevents instant encounters when entering a grass area. wEnabledPlayerEvents gives fine-grained control over what can trigger — used to lock warps during cutscenes, disable wild encounters during scripted events, etc.

goal: Implement player movement — joypad input, 4-direction walking, running shoes equivalent, step counting, collision checking
abstract_tasks: Implement DoPlayerMovement: GetDPad reads joypad and applies downhill forcing; TranslateIntoMovement dispatches based on player state (PLAYER_NORMAL, PLAYER_BIKE, PLAYER_SURF, PLAYER_SURF_PIKA, PLAYER_SKATE)

Implement normal/bike movement pipeline: CheckForced (ice locks direction) -> GetAction (maps D-pad to walking direction, facing, deltaX/Y, collision tile) -> CheckTile (forced movement tiles override input) -> CheckTurning (turn in place if facing != walking direction) -> TryStep (land permissions + NPC collision + ice + bike speed) -> TryJump (ledge hopping on tiles $A0-$A7) -> CheckWarp (edge warp detection)
Implement movement step types and speeds: STEP_WALK (normal slow_step), STEP_BIKE (fast big_step), STEP_ICE (fast_slide_step), STEP_LEDGE (jump_step), STEP_TURN (turn in place)

Implement collision checks: CheckLandPerms (tile permissions + walkability), CheckSurfPerms (0=water, 1=land exit, carry=blocked), CheckNPC (scan all 16 object structs for collision at target coordinates), CheckStrengthBoulder (if Strength active and STRENGTH_BOULDER_F set, trigger BOULDER_MOVING_F)

Implement ice sliding: PLAYER_SKATE state locks direction input (CheckForced); movement continues in same direction until hitting WALL_TILE or COLL_BRAKE tile; no bump sound when hitting wall on ice (HitWall suppressed)
Implement ledge jump: tiles $A0-$A7 encode which directions allow jumping via low 3 bits mask table; plays SFX_JUMP_OVER_LEDGE on success; movement result = PLAYERMOVEMENT_JUMP

source_data.overworld_engine.md:
## Player Movement (player_movement.asm)

### DoPlayerMovement
1. GetDPad -- Read joypad, apply downhill forcing
2. TranslateIntoMovement -- Based on player state:

Player States:
| PLAYER_NORMAL | Standard walking |
| PLAYER_BIKE | Fast movement (STEP_BIKE) |
| PLAYER_SURF | Water movement |
| PLAYER_SKATE | Ice sliding |

Movement Pipeline (Normal/Bike):
1. CheckForced -- On ice, forces same direction
2. GetAction -- Maps D-pad to walking direction, facing, delta X/Y, collision tile
3. CheckTile -- Forced movement tiles override input
4. CheckTurning -- If facing != walking direction, turn first
5. TryStep -- Check land permissions, NPC collision, ice, bike speed
6. TryJump -- Ledge hopping (HI_NYBBLE_LEDGES tiles)
7. CheckWarp -- Edge warp detection

Step Types:
| STEP_WALK | Normal (slow_step) |
| STEP_BIKE | Fast (big_step) |
| STEP_ICE | Slide (fast_slide_step) |
| STEP_LEDGE | Jump (jump_step) |
| STEP_TURN | Turn in place |

Movement Results:
- PLAYERMOVEMENT_NORMAL (0) -- No movement
- PLAYERMOVEMENT_WARP (1) -- Entered warp
- PLAYERMOVEMENT_TURN (2) -- Changed facing
- PLAYERMOVEMENT_FINISH (4) -- Step started
- PLAYERMOVEMENT_JUMP (7) -- Ledge hop

### Ledge System
Collision tiles $A0-$A7 (HI_NYBBLE_LEDGES). Each tile encodes which facing directions allow jumping. Plays SFX_JUMP_OVER_LEDGE. The low 3 bits index into a mask table.

### Collision Checks
- CheckLandPerms: Tile permissions (wTilePermissions) AND facing direction
- CheckNPC: IsNPCAtCoord checks all 16 object structs
- CheckStrengthBoulder: If Strength active and STRENGTH_BOULDER_F, set BOULDER_MOVING_F

### Ice Sliding
- Input locked to current direction (CheckForced)
- Movement continues until hitting wall or non-ice tile
- If hitting wall on ice, no bump sound (HitWall)
source_data.collision_and_tilesets.md:
## Collision Tile Types
| LAND_TILE | walkable | Player can walk on this tile |
| WATER_TILE | surfable | Requires Surf to traverse |
| WALL_TILE | impassable | Player cannot walk through |

### Floor/Walkable (LAND_TILE)
| 0x23 | COLL_ICE | Ice floor (player slides) |
| 0x40 | COLL_BRAKE | Stops sliding on ice |
| 0x41-0x44 | COLL_WALK_RIGHT/LEFT/UP/DOWN | Forced movement tiles |
| 0x60 | COLL_PIT | Fall-through pit |

### Warp Tiles
| 0x70 | COLL_WARP_CARPET_DOWN | Warp with downward animation |
| 0x71 | COLL_DOOR | Standard door warp |
| 0x72 | COLL_LADDER | Ladder warp |
| 0x73 | COLL_STAIRCASE_73 | Staircase warp |
| 0x74 | COLL_CAVE_74 | Cave entrance warp |

unstructured_notes: Player movement is the most frame-sensitive system in the overworld. The turning delay (CheckTurning) is important for authentic feel — the player must face a direction before walking into it. PLAYER_BIKE moves at big_step speed which is faster than normal walking but can still be blocked by walls and ledges. The 8 ledge tile variants ($A0-$A7) each correspond to a directional bitmask — $A0 might be 'south only' while $A4 might be 'south and east'. The downhill forcing in GetDPad creates the effect of being pushed downhill on specific map tiles. NPC collision checks all 16 object slots which includes both NPCs and interactable objects.

source_data.state_machine.md:
### Map Loading Sequence
When transitioning between maps:
1. Save current map state (NPC positions, event flags)
2. Load new map data (tilemap, collision, NPCs)
3. Execute map setup scripts
4. Initialize NPC sprites and positions
5. Check scene scripts
6. Process map triggers
7. Resume player control

### Event Processing Order (per step)
Each overworld step:
1. Joypad input processed
2. Player movement applied
3. Step counter events checked
4. Wild encounter check (if applicable tile)
5. Map connection/warp check
6. Trainer line-of-sight check
7. Tile-based event trigger check

unstructured_notes: This is a QA sprint verifying the work done in sprints 91-92. Key correctness tests: (1) wMapStatus transitions must never skip ENTER (which sets 5-step cooldown), (2) movement pipeline must try turning before stepping (player faces new direction one frame before moving), (3) ledge hop tiles (0xA0-0xA7) are one-directional and must be verified against the pokecrystal tile mask table, (4) ice tiles must lock input direction. Known overworld bugs to intentionally reproduce: no bump sound on tile $3E, swimming NPCs ignore radius. Verify these bugs exist in the Crusty implementation.

source_data.overworld_engine.md:
### Ice Sliding
On PLAYER_SKATE state or on ice tiles (CheckIceTile):
- Input is locked to current direction (CheckForced)
- Movement continues until hitting wall or non-ice tile
- If hitting wall on ice, no bump sound (HitWall)
- CheckStandingOnIce checks wPlayerTileCollision and wPlayerState

### Collision Checks
- CheckLandPerms: Tile permissions AND facing direction, plus walkability check
- CheckSurfPerms: Returns 0 for water, 1 for land (exit water), carry for blocked
- CheckNPC: IsNPCAtCoord checks all 16 object structs for collision at target coordinates

### Ledge System
Collision tiles $A0-$A7 (HI_NYBBLE_LEDGES). Each tile encodes which facing directions allow jumping. Plays SFX_JUMP_OVER_LEDGE. The low 3 bits index into a mask table checking if the player's facing matches.

### Warp SFX
- Doors: SFX_ENTER_DOOR
- Warp panels: SFX_WARP_TO
- Everything else: SFX_EXIT_BUILDING

unstructured_notes: The collision system is the foundation for all overworld gameplay correctness. The tile permission table from data/collision/collision_permissions.asm must be faithfully reproduced — every tile code must map to exactly the right permission. Ice sliding is a common source of bugs: the player must be locked to their current direction (CheckForced) and slide across multiple tiles in a single step cycle. COLL_BRAKE (0x40) stops sliding. Pit tiles (0x60) must trigger fall animation not just a warp. Counter tiles (0x90) allow talking from one additional tile away (doubles interaction distance). The warp carpet tile directions are counterintuitive (CARPET_DOWN triggers on walking right) — take care.

## Tilesets
The game uses multiple tilesets for different map environments. Key tilesets referenced in field_move_blocks:
- TILESET_JOHTO -- Standard outdoor Johto tileset
- TILESET_JOHTO_MODERN -- Modern buildings in Johto
- TILESET_KANTO -- Kanto outdoor tileset
- TILESET_PARK -- National Park tileset
- TILESET_FOREST -- Ilex Forest / forest areas

Additional tilesets from data/tilesets.asm (37 total tileset definition files):
- Cave, Gate, House, Lab, Facility, Port, Pokecenter, Mart
- Radio Tower, Lighthouse, Ruins, Ice Path, Underground
- Ho-Oh/Lugia towers, Mansion, Game Corner, Battle Tower

## Collision-Triggered Scripts
Walking into these tiles triggers a standard script:
| Tile | Script | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
| COLL_BOOKSHELF | MagazineBookshelfScript | Pokemon magazines text |
| COLL_PC | PCScript | Opens Pokemon Center PC |
| COLL_RADIO | Radio1Script | Turns on radio |
| COLL_TOWN_MAP | TownMapScript | Shows town map |
| COLL_MART_SHELF | MerchandiseShelfScript | Merchandise shelf text |
| COLL_TV | TVScript | Displays TV text |
| COLL_WINDOW | WindowScript | Window description |
| COLL_INCENSE_BURNER | IncenseBurnerScript | Incense burner description |

## Field Move Blocks (Cut and Whirlpool)
### Cut Trees
| Tileset | Facing Block | Replacement | Animation |
|---------|-------------|-------------|----------|
| Johto | Grass ($03) | Cleared ($02) | Grass cut |
| Johto | Tree ($5B) | Stump ($3C) | Tree fall |
| Kanto | Grass ($0B) | Cleared ($0A) | Grass cut |
| Park | Grass ($13, $03) | Cleared ($03, $04) | Grass cut |
| Forest | Tree ($0F) | Cleared ($17) | Tree fall |
### Whirlpool
| Tileset | Facing Block | Replacement |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| Johto | Whirlpool ($07) | Calm water ($36) |

### Sprite Loading
- AddMapSprites: Outdoor maps use OutdoorSprites table; indoor maps enumerate objects
- SPRITE_GFX_LIST_CAPACITY entries in wUsedSprites
- Sprites sorted by type (bubble sort), arranged in VRAM tiles
- Two tile tables: tiles $00-$7F in VRAM bank 1, tiles $80+ in bank 0

### Sprite Types
- WALKING_SPRITE: 12 tiles (4 directions x 3 frames)
- STANDING_SPRITE: 12 tiles
- STILL_SPRITE: 4 tiles (single direction)

unstructured_notes: Tilesets are the visual backbone of every map. Each of the 37 tilesets has its own collision data, graphics, and palette map. The collision-triggered scripts (bookshelf, PC, radio, etc.) are walk-into effects, not A-button effects — the player must face and press A for object events, but these collision scripts fire automatically on tile entry. Field-move block replacement is tileset-specific: the same cut animation block IDs differ between Johto and Kanto. This sprint is primarily data-entry and plumbing work, ensuring the right tileset is loaded per map and that animated tile frames cycle correctly.

source_data.overworld_engine.md:
### Ice Sliding
On PLAYER_SKATE state or on ice tiles (CheckIceTile):
- Input is locked to current direction (CheckForced)
- Movement continues until hitting wall or non-ice tile
- If hitting wall on ice, no bump sound (HitWall)
- CheckStandingOnIce checks wPlayerTileCollision and wPlayerState

### Known Bugs
1. No bump noise on tile $3E: Standing direction indexes into edge warp table incorrectly
3. ChooseWildEncounter validates level as species

unstructured_notes: Key QA checks for this sprint: (1) Ledge tiles 0xA0-0xA7 encode both the tile direction AND the facing match — use the pokecrystal mask table to verify each direction maps correctly. (2) Ice Path in Johto uses ice + forced movement tiles in combination — test sequences of ice tiles followed by COLL_BRAKE. (3) The A-button tile event order matters: Cut must fire before Surf when facing a cut-tree tile adjacent to water. (4) Warp carpet tiles (0x70) trigger on walking right, not down despite the name — verify this counterintuitive mapping is correct. Reproduce the known 'no bump noise on tile $3E' bug intentionally.

unstructured_notes: Crystal is the first mainline game with a female protagonist (Kris). The sprite system must correctly gate on wPlayerGender for all player states including bike and surf. The 102 sprite count must be exact — each sprite in data/sprites/sprites.asm defines its GFX pointer, tile count, movement type, and palette. The emote system is separate from regular sprites (EMOTE_OBJECT_F flag) and the 12 emote types must be indexed correctly by event scripts ($5E loademote, $5F showemote commands). VRAM layout (bank 0 vs bank 1 tile regions) must be respected for CGB compatibility.

source_data.overworld_engine.md:
## NPC Movement (npc_movement.asm)

### CanObjectMoveInDirection
Per-NPC movement validation:
1. Swimming NPCs: Check WillObjectBumpIntoLand (BUG: no radius check)
2. Normal NPCs: Check WillObjectBumpIntoWater
3. Unless NOCLIP_TILES_F set
4. Check WillObjectBumpIntoSomeoneElse (unless NOCLIP_OBJS_F)
5. Check HasObjectReachedMovementLimit (unless MOVE_ANYWHERE_F)
6. Check IsObjectMovingOffEdgeOfScreen

### Movement Radius
OBJECT_RADIUS stores X radius in low nybble, Y radius in high nybble. Object cannot move beyond OBJECT_INIT_X/Y +/- radius.

### NPC Flags (OBJECT_FLAGS1)
- NOCLIP_TILES_F: Ignore tile collision
- NOCLIP_OBJS_F: Ignore NPC collision
- MOVE_ANYWHERE_F: Ignore radius and screen bounds
- EMOTE_OBJECT_F: Is an emote bubble, not a real NPC

### Counter Tiles
CheckFacingObject doubles the interaction distance for COLL_COUNTER tiles, allowing talking across shop counters.

### Player Event Priority (PlayerEvents)
1. CheckTrainerEvent -- Trainer line of sight (highest priority)
2. CheckTileEvent -- Warps, connections, coord events, step counting, wild encounters
3. RunMemScript -- Queued reentry scripts
4. RunSceneScript -- Scene-based scripts
5. CheckTimeEvents -- Bug Contest timer, daily reset, Pokerus tick, phone calls
6. OWPlayerInput -- A-button (talk/interact), Start (menu), Select (registered item)

### Movement Types (for object_event)
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_STILL -- Stationary
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_WANDER -- Random walk within radius
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_SPINRANDOM_SLOW/FAST -- Random facing
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_WALK_UP_DOWN -- Pace vertically
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_WALK_LEFT_RIGHT -- Pace horizontally
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_STANDING_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT -- Fixed facing
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_POKEMON -- Pokemon movement
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_SUDOWOODO -- Sudowoodo blocking
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_SMASHABLE_ROCK -- Rock Smash boulder
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_STRENGTH_BOULDER -- Strength boulder
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_SPINCOUNTERCLOCKWISE/CLOCKWISE -- Spin
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_SWIM_WANDER -- Water wandering
- SPRITEMOVEDATA_BIGDOLL/BIGDOLLSYM/BIGDOLLASYM -- Decorations

unstructured_notes: Trainer line-of-sight is gameplay-critical: it fires CheckTrainerEvent on every player step before any tile events. The trainer must be facing toward the player, the player must be in the same column/row as the trainer, and no other NPCs or objects can be blocking the line of sight. The shock (!) emote must play before the battle script begins. The known swimming NPC bug (CanObjectMoveInDirection skips HasObjectReachedMovementLimit for swimming types) should be reproduced faithfully. Strength boulders (SPRITEMOVEDATA_STRENGTH_BOULDER) require the Strength field move to be activated and BOULDER_MOVING_F set before they can be pushed.

unstructured_notes: Map connections in Crystal work by defining a strip of tiles at the edge of one map that 'belongs' to an adjacent map. When the player steps into this strip, EnterMapConnection fires instead of a warp. The connection system computes an offset so that position (X,Y) on the old map translates to the correct (X',Y') on the new map — for example, walking south from New Bark Town places you at the corresponding X position on Route 29. This offset math must be pixel-perfect or the player will teleport to the wrong position. The MAPSTATUS_START path is used for connections (different from door warps which use MAPSTATUS_DOOR). Test New Bark -> Route 29 -> Cherrygrove City connections as a baseline corridor.

source_data.overworld_engine.md:
## Warp System (warp_connection.asm)

### Warp Processing
EnterMapWarp:
1. SaveDigWarp -- If going from outdoor to indoor, save for Dig/Escape Rope
   - Exception: MOUNT_MOON_SQUARE and TIN_TOWER_ROOF (outdoor maps inside indoor groups)
2. SetSpawn -- If entering Pokemon Center (TILESET_POKECENTER or TILESET_POKECOM_CENTER), set respawn point
3. Copy wNextWarp/wNextMapGroup/wNextMapNumber to active map state

### Edge Warps
Directional warp carpets ($70-$73) trigger when walking in the matching direction:
- COLL_WARP_CARPET_DOWN: Walking right
- COLL_WARP_CARPET_UP: Walking left
- COLL_WARP_CARPET_LEFT: Walking up
- COLL_WARP_CARPET_RIGHT: Walking down

### Map Entry Methods (MAPSETUP_*)
- MAPSETUP_CONNECTION: Map connection crossing
- MAPSETUP_DOOR: Standard warp
- MAPSETUP_FALL: Falling through pit
- MAPSETUP_RELOADMAP: Reload (resets poison counter)
source_data.warp_connections.md:
## Summary Statistics
- Total warp events in game: 1,312
- Johto outdoor maps: ~50 warps
- Kanto outdoor maps: ~50 warps
- Interior/dungeon maps: ~1,200 warps
- Largest warp count: Goldenrod City (15 warps)
- Most complex dungeon: Tin Tower (10 floors, ~25 internal warps)

## New Bark Town
| From | To |
|------|----||
| New Bark Town (6,3) | Elm's Lab |
| New Bark Town (13,5) | Player's House 1F |
| New Bark Town (3,11) | Player's Neighbor's House |
| New Bark Town (11,13) | Elm's House |

### Tin Tower (Bell Tower)
- 1F entrance from Ecruteak City (37,7)
- 1F -> 2F -> 3F -> 4F -> 5F -> 6F -> 7F -> 8F -> 9F -> Roof
- Multiple staircases per floor creating a maze
- 7F has internal teleport warps
- Roof: Ho-Oh encounter

unstructured_notes: The warp system must handle 1,312 warp events bidirectionally. Every def_warp_event in the map ASM files stores the source tile (x,y), destination map, and destination warp index. Critically: warp IDs are 0-indexed per-map, so warp 0 in Map B is the first def_warp_event declared in Map B's map events. The Dig/Escape Rope save (SaveDigWarp) must track the last outdoor warp entered so the player returns to the correct outdoor location. The two exceptions (Mt. Moon Square and Tin Tower Roof) are outdoor-type maps embedded within indoor map groups and must NOT overwrite the dig warp. Pokemon Center detection is by tileset (TILESET_POKECENTER) not by map name.

### Warp Processing
EnterMapWarp:
1. SaveDigWarp -- If going from outdoor to indoor, save for Dig/Escape Rope
   - Exception: MOUNT_MOON_SQUARE and TIN_TOWER_ROOF

unstructured_notes: QA focus: stuck states are the primary risk. A warp that leads to the wrong destination warp_id can lock the player into an unreachable room. Test bidirectional warp pairs: every warp in map A that goes to map B must have a corresponding return warp in map B that lands the player on a walkable tile. Particularly validate multi-entrance dungeons: Union Cave has 2 floor entrances from Route 32 and Route 33, each must land at a different position in Union Cave 1F. Goldenrod Underground has north and south entrances to the same tunnel — verify these connect correctly. The Burned Tower floor-fall (MAPSETUP_FALL) must not save a Dig warp since the floor breaking is not a voluntary cave entry.

source_data.scripting_commands.md:
## Event Script Commands (Overworld)
Defined in macros/scripts/events.asm. ~140 commands total.

### Flow Control
| Opcode | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| $00 | scall script | Call a script (same bank) |
| $01 | farscall script | Call a script (any bank) |
| $02 | memcall script | Call script at address in memory |
| $03 | sjump script | Jump to script (same bank) |
| $04 | farsjump script | Jump to script (any bank) |
| $06 | ifequal byte, script | Jump if wScriptVar == byte |
| $07 | ifnotequal byte, script | Jump if wScriptVar != byte |
| $08 | iffalse script | Jump if wScriptVar == 0 |
| $09 | iftrue script | Jump if wScriptVar != 0 |
| $0A | ifgreater byte, script | Jump if wScriptVar > byte |
| $0B | ifless byte, script | Jump if wScriptVar < byte |
| $0E | callasm asm | Call assembly routine |
| $0F | special special_pointer | Call special function |

### Scene/Map Control
| Opcode | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| $11 | checkmapscene map | Check scene ID for a map |
| $12 | setmapscene map, id | Set scene ID for a map |
| $13 | checkscene | Check current map's scene |
| $14 | setscene id | Set current map's scene |

### Variables
| Opcode | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| $15 | setval value | wScriptVar = value |
| $16 | addval value | wScriptVar += value |
| $17 | random value | wScriptVar = random(0..value-1) |
| $19 | readmem addr | wScriptVar = [addr] |
| $1A | writemem addr | [addr] = wScriptVar |

### Event Flags
| Opcode | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| $30 | checkevent flag | Check event flag |
| $31 | clearevent flag | Clear event flag |
| $32 | setevent flag | Set event flag |
| $33 | checkflag flag | Check engine flag |
| $34 | clearflag flag | Clear engine flag |
| $35 | setflag flag | Set engine flag |

### Misc
| Opcode | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| $7B | changeblock x, y, block | Change map block |
| $7C | reloadmappart | Reload visible map |
| $7D | reloadmap | Full map reload |
| $8B | end | End script |
source_data.event_engine.md:
### Callback Types
- MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP -- Runs when entering map for first time
- MAPCALLBACK_TILES -- Modify tiles on load
- MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS -- Modify objects on load
- MAPCALLBACK_SPRITES -- Modify sprites on load
- MAPCALLBACK_CMDQUEUE -- Set up command queue (boulders)

### Map Scripts Header
MapName_MapScripts:
    def_scene_scripts -- Scene-triggered scripts
    def_callbacks -- Map load callbacks
    callback TYPE, script

unstructured_notes: The event scripting engine is the workhorse of all overworld interaction. Scripts are byte-streams of opcodes processed sequentially with a call stack for subroutines. The wScriptVar register is a single byte used for all comparisons and returns from callasm/special routines. Scene IDs gate which version of a map's events run (e.g., scene 0 = before event, scene 1 = during event, scene 2 = after event). Map callbacks (MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP etc.) run once at specific points during map loading and are separate from per-step scene scripts. The $8B end command is essential: missing it causes script overrun. The callasm ($0E) and special ($0F) commands bridge into Rust-native logic for complex operations.

## Text Commands
Defined in macros/scripts/text.asm.

| Opcode | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| $00 | text_start | Start writing text until @ |
| $01 | text_ram addr | Write text from RAM address |
| $02 | text_bcd addr, flags | Write BCD number |
| $09 | text_decimal addr, bytes, digits | Print number |
| $0A | text_pause | Pause 30 frames |
| $06 | text_promptbutton | Wait for button, show arrow |
| $07 | text_scroll | Push text up two lines |
| $14 | text_buffer id | Write from buffer (0-6) |
| $16 | text_far addr | Write text from other bank |
| $50 | text_end | End text processing |

### Control Characters (inline)
| Code | Macro | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| $4E | next text | Move one line down |
| $4F | line text | Start bottom line |
| $50 | page text | New Pokedex page |
| $51 | para text | New paragraph |
| $55 | cont text | Scroll to next line |
| $57 | done | End text box |
| $58 | prompt | Prompt to close |

### Text Buffers
| ID | Address | Content |
|----|---------|--------|
| 0 | wStringBuffer3 | General purpose |
| 1 | wStringBuffer4 | General purpose |
| 2 | wStringBuffer5 | General purpose |
| 3 | wStringBuffer2 | General purpose |
| 4 | wStringBuffer1 | General purpose |
| 5 | wEnemyMonNickname | Enemy Pokemon name |
| 6 | wBattleMonNickname | Player Pokemon name |

### Text Speed
- Fast: TEXT_DELAY_FAST (1 frame per character)
- Medium: TEXT_DELAY_MED (3 frames per character)
- Slow: TEXT_DELAY_SLOW (5 frames per character)
- Stored in low nibble of wOptions

### Frame
- 8 different text box border styles (Type 1 through Type 8)
- Changes the visual border around all text boxes

### Known Bug
- Options menu fails to clear joypad state on initialization, meaning a button press from the previous screen can inadvertently change a setting

### Move Learning Text
| Label | Text |
|-------|------|
| LearnedMove | [MON] learned [MOVE]! |
| AskForgetMove | [MON] is trying to learn [MOVE]. But [MON] can't learn more than four moves. Delete an older move to make room for [MOVE]? |
| MoveForgot | [MON] forgot [MOVE]. And... |
| MoveCantForgetHM | HM moves can't be forgotten now. |

### Field Move HM Text

|-------|------|
| UseCut | [MON] used CUT! |
| AskSurf | The water is calm. Want to SURF? |
| UseStrength | [MON] used STRENGTH! |

unstructured_notes: The text system is one of the most player-visible systems in the game. Text speed must be exactly correct: FAST=1, MED=3, SLOW=5 frames per character — not per word or per scroll. The 7 text buffer slots have fixed indices and fixed memory addresses — buffer 5 is always the enemy Pokemon's nickname (used in battle), buffer 6 is always the player's active Pokemon's nickname. The $57 'done' code closes the text box and returns control; $58 'prompt' shows a scroll arrow and waits for button. Text boxes use the frame style from wOptions (1-8), and swapping frame styles must take effect immediately on next text box open. The 'cont' ($55) scrolls text up by 2 lines — used for long NPC dialogue. The options menu joypad bug should be reproduced as a known pokecrystal quirk.

unstructured_notes: This QA sprint is critical before moving to wild encounters (sprint 106) because encounter triggers depend on the scripting engine for repel checks, encounter table switching, and Bug Contest timer. Key verification: (1) scall vs sjump difference must be exact — sjump that is mistakenly treated as scall will corrupt the call stack; (2) event flags must be bitfield-packed (not byte-per-flag) to match pokecrystal memory layout; (3) scene IDs must be per-map-group storage, not global; (4) the $17 random command generates a value in range [0, value-1] inclusive, not [0, value]; (5) HM move forgetting restriction ('HM moves can't be forgotten now') is enforced via the scripting text system, not in the move deleter engine.

source_data.overworld_engine.md:
## Wild Encounters (wildmons.asm)

### Encounter Flow
1. TryWildEncounter -- Get encounter rate, apply modifiers, random check
2. ChooseWildEncounter -- Pick species from probability table
3. CheckRepelEffect -- Compare wild level to lead Pokemon level

### Encounter Rate Calculation
Base rate = wMornEncounterRate / wDayEncounterRate / wNiteEncounterRate (time-dependent)
           or wWaterEncounterRate (if surfing)
Then apply:
  - Pokemon March radio: double rate
  - Ruins of Alph radio: double rate
  - Pokemon Lullaby radio: halve rate
  - Cleanse Tag on any party member: halve rate
Finally: Random() < modified_rate triggers encounter

### Wild Mon Probability Tables
Grass (7 slots per time of day):
| Slot | Cumulative % | Individual % |
|------|-------------|---------------|
| 0 | 30% | 30% |
| 1 | 60% | 30% |
| 2 | 80% | 20% |
| 3 | 90% | 10% |
| 4 | 95% | 5% |
| 5 | 99% | 4% |
| 6 | 100% | 1% |

Water (3 slots):
| Slot | Cumulative % | Individual % |
|------|-------------|---------------|
| 0 | 60% | 60% |
| 1 | 90% | 30% |
| 2 | 100% | 10% |

### Surf Level Variation
When surfing, wild Pokemon levels get random bonuses:
- 35% chance: +0
- 30% chance: +1
- 20% chance: +2
- 10% chance: +3
- 5% chance: +4

### Roaming Pokemon (Raikou/Entei)
- Initialized at level 40
- ~29.3% chance per grass step: 25/64 base * 3/4 not-zero = 75/256
- 50/50 Raikou vs Entei
- Must be on same map as player
- HP preserved between encounters (wRoamMon1HP)

### Wild Encounter Cooldown
- 5-step cooldown on map entry
- No encounters in first 5 steps after entering a map

### Repel
- wRepelEffect counts down each step
- If active: wild encounter only triggers if wild level >= lead Pokemon level
- Checks first non-fainted Pokemon's level

### Bug Catching Contest
- 40% encounter rate in super tall grass, 20% in normal grass
- Uses ContestMons table with species, min level, max level, probability

source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Stat Calculation
### HP Formula
HP = ((Base + DV) * 2 + floor(sqrt(StatExp)) / 4) * Level / 100 + Level + 10

### DV System
- HP DV: Derived. HP_DV = (Atk_DV & 1) << 3 | (Def_DV & 1) << 2 | (Spd_DV & 1) << 1 | (Spc_DV & 1)

unstructured_notes: The encounter rate system has multiple stacked multipliers that must be applied in the exact order: base rate selection by time-of-day -> radio modifiers -> Cleanse Tag -> random roll. Applying them in wrong order gives different probabilities. The 7-slot grass table uses cumulative probabilities (roll 1-100, slot 0 if <=30, slot 1 if <=60, etc.) with separate tables for morning/day/night. The roaming Raikou/Entei check happens inside the same encounter check flow: if the roaming check triggers (75/256), it overrides the normal table result and initiates a Raikou or Entei battle. Wild Pokemon levels for surf encounters get +0 to +4 bonus via the surf level variation table — this makes surf encounters inherently variable. Route 29 at night switches from Pidgey/Sentret to Hoothoot/Rattata — verify time-of-day switching works on all Johto routes.

## Catch Rate Formula

### Ball Multipliers
| Ball | Modifier Logic |
|------|---------------|
| Lure Ball | If fishing: rate * 3; else: no modification |

unstructured_notes: Fishing uses a two-phase bite system: (1) cast rod -> random bite check each frame, (2) player presses A at prompt -> catch encounter begins. Old Rod has a near-instant bite, Super Rod requires timing. Headbutt is TM02 in Gen 2, not an HM — any Pokemon that can learn it can use it in the field without a badge requirement. The tree set (Canyon/Town/Route/Forest/Rock) is determined by the map's tileset and assigned per-map in pokecrystal data. The rare table has lower probability slots. Heracross is headbutt-exclusive and valuable. Lake of Rage Super Rod only gives Magikarp because Red Gyarados is a static encounter — no Gyarados in fishing table by design.

source_data.overworld_engine.md:
### A-Button Tile Events (TryTileCollisionEvent)
Checked in order when pressing A facing a tile:
1. CheckFacingTileForStdScript
2. Cut tree
3. Whirlpool
4. Waterfall
5. Headbutt tree
6. Surf

**Player States:**
| State | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| PLAYER_NORMAL | Standard walking |
| PLAYER_BIKE | Fast movement (STEP_BIKE) |
| PLAYER_SURF | Water movement |
| PLAYER_SURF_PIKA | Surfing Pikachu variant |

### Collision Checks
- **CheckSurfPerms**: Returns 0 for water, 1 for land (exit water), carry for blocked

### Map Setup
- CheckSurfing: If on water tile, force PLAYER_SURF
- ResetSurfingOrBikingState: Reset to PLAYER_NORMAL when entering indoor/dungeon

**Movement Results (PLAYERMOVEMENT_*):**
- PLAYERMOVEMENT_EXIT_WATER (6) -- Leaving water

unstructured_notes: Surf requires Fog Badge (gym 4, Morty). Waterfall requires Rising Badge (gym 8, Clair). Whirlpool requires Glacier Badge (gym 7, Pryce). These gate checks must be enforced. The Whirlpool block replacement is permanent for the session (not saved between sessions unless flagged). Water currents on Route 41 push the player in a direction — cannot be resisted. PLAYER_SURF_PIKA is a Crystal-specific feature where a surfing Pikachu from a special event can be used; functionally identical to PLAYER_SURF but with different sprite. When entering an indoor map while surfing, state resets to PLAYER_NORMAL.

unstructured_notes: Cut block replacement is tileset-specific — must read from field_move_blocks.asm table. Strength boulders are map objects with STRENGTH_BOULDER_F flag; once Strength is active (set after using it in that session), pushing any boulder in a direction will slide it. Boulders do not reset on map re-entry in Crystal (unlike Gen 1). Fly destination list includes all towns/cities with ENGINE_FLYPOINT_* flags set — these flags are set when the player first enters a location. Flash as a field move is only needed for the Dark Cave (Route 31 and Route 45 entrances) to remove the darkness overlay; it does not affect other maps. Rock Smash is a TM in Gen 2 (TM48), not an HM, but still requires a badge (Storm Badge) for overworld use.

unstructured_notes: New Bark Town is the game's starting location. The player begins in their bedroom (2F Player's House). The starter selection is one of the most critical scripted events — each starter Poke Ball must set the correct species flag and disable the others. The teacher NPC uses a coord_event (not a standard NPC collision) to block the player from walking off the south edge of town. Tuscany (Tuesday sibling) on Route 29 is the first of 7 day-of-week siblings; she only appears after the player has the Zephyr Badge. The Rival Battle 1 is a scripted event triggered by a coord_event when the player walks from New Bark toward Cherrygrove (or Cherrygrove toward New Bark, depending on direction).

unstructured_notes: Sprout Tower has a story beat where Silver is seen on 3F but leaves before the player arrives — this should be handled via a flag check that adjusts NPC placement. The flexible central pillar in Sprout Tower is a visual element, not a gameplay mechanic. Earl's Academy has multiple students who give hints about game mechanics. The Onix-for-Bellsprout trade at Kyle's House is version-specific (in Crystal, Kyle trades Onix for Bellsprout). After the player defeats Falkner, Elm calls on the Pokegear and asks the player to pick up the Mystery Egg from his aide at the Violet Pokemon Center — this is a phone call event, not a warp.

unstructured_notes: Kurt is an important NPC: after Team Rocket is cleared from Slowpoke Well, Kurt gives the player a Lure Ball immediately. He also accepts Apricorns once per day and delivers the corresponding ball after one real-time day. This day timer system is a real-time mechanic tied to the RTC. The Ruins of Alph puzzle chambers are accessed from the Inner Chamber (main hall) — the outer chambers are entered from the inner hall, not directly from the exterior. Union Cave B2F Friday Lapras is a static encounter (only one available) at a fixed coordinate; it only appears on Fridays and reappears the following Friday if not caught. Bugsy's gym has a bug trainer layout — the gym is a maze of ledges and trainers, not a simple hallway.

unstructured_notes: Goldenrod City is the most complex map in the game with the most NPCs and buildings. Whitney's gym requires the player to talk to a specific gym Lass after defeating Whitney before the badge is awarded — this is a two-step event. The Farfetch'd puzzle in Ilex Forest requires directing each bird back using specific approach angles; getting too close from the wrong direction causes the bird to run away again. The Underground connects north and south Goldenrod City and includes a basement Warehouse that is locked until the Basement Key is obtained during the Radio Tower Rocket takeover arc. The SquirtBottle is given by the Flower Shop girl only after the Plain Badge is obtained — this is the only way to proceed past Sudowoodo on Route 36.

unstructured_notes: Ecruteak City is the narrative hub of the Johto game — the Burned Tower, Tin Tower, Dance Theater, and legendary beast release all converge here. The Burned Tower fall-through to B1F is a scripted fall event triggered by walking on a specific floor tile. Morty's invisible floor gym is implemented as invisible tile objects that block the player from walking certain paths; the player must find the correct invisible path. Bill meeting the player here (before they visit Goldenrod) is flagged — this flag is what allows Bill's Family's House in Goldenrod to give the Eevee. The Tin Tower exterior (10 floors) is part of this sprint; the Ho-Oh encounter logic comes in the special encounters sprint.

unstructured_notes: Jasmine is unique among gym leaders in that she is not available to battle when the player first arrives — she is at the lighthouse top tending to the sick Ampharos (Amphy). The player must climb the lighthouse, speak to Jasmine, Surf to Cianwood, battle Chuck, obtain the SecretPotion from the pharmacy, and Fly back to Olivine before the gym unlocks. Olivine Lighthouse has fall-through holes (like Burned Tower) that shortcut certain floors. The Moomoo Farm berry quest is not flagged per berry — the counter tracks total berries given and gives rewards at 7 total. Tim's House contains a Voltorb-for-Bellsprout in-game trade (Tim trades his Voltorb for your Bellsprout). The S.S. Aqua dock is only accessible post-Elite Four; the port area exists but the ship is absent during the main game.

unstructured_notes: Sprint 120 is a QA audit covering the Ecruteak-to-Olivine corridor from sprints 118-119. Key verification points: (1) Burned Tower floor-fall trigger — player must walk to tile (10,9) on 1F which fires a fall animation dropping them to B1F; this is the canonical way to see the legendary beast cutscene. (2) Invisible floor puzzle in Ecruteak Gym — the walkable path must be encoded exactly per pokecrystal; stepping off triggers a warp back to the gym entrance. (3) Jasmine's gym lock — the gym must be inaccessible until the EVENT_JASMINE_RETURNS_TO_GYM flag is set (fires after delivering SecretPotion). (4) S.S. Aqua docking is post-game only; the Port Passage warp to the ship should be gated by the S.S. Ticket event flag. (5) Tin Tower entrance building gates entry on Clear Bell flag in Crystal. Test harness should confirm warp indices match the pokecrystal source numbering — a common off-by-one error is warp index 0 vs 1 for dual-tile warps like the Route 42 gate pair.

source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Suicune (Crystal Storyline)

**Location:** Multiple encounters, final battle at Tin Tower
**Level:** 40
**Mechanics:** In Pokemon Crystal, Suicune has a unique storyline. You encounter it at:
1. Burned Tower (flees, alongside Raikou and Entei)
2. Cianwood City (cutscene, flees)
3. Route 42 (cutscene, flees)
4. Vermilion City (cutscene, flees)
5. Route 14 (cutscene, flees)
6. **Tin Tower B1F** (static battle, Lv40) — requires Clear Bell + all 3 Legendary Beasts seen

unstructured_notes: Route 40 and 41 are pure Surf routes — no land-based content. The Battle Tower Gate on Route 40 at (9,5) leads to the Battle Tower area (a separate map region). Whirl Islands is a complex 4-entrance dungeon; the canonical path to Lugia is: Route 41 SE entrance -> Whirl Island SE -> B1F passage -> B2F -> Lugia's Chamber. The SW entrance leads to a dead end with an Ultra Ball. NW and NE connect to B1F but require navigating through to SE side. Cianwood Gym waterfall puzzle: the waterfall blocks the direct path to Chuck; player must navigate around it (there is a switch or boulder mechanic in pokecrystal — verify the exact puzzle trigger in the ASM). Mania's Shuckle: he loans it and may call to ask for it back later (this call is scheduled with the phone system). SecretPotion is given freely by the Pharmacist — no cost, no flag check other than being in Cianwood after reaching the Olivine Lighthouse cutscene. The Suicune spot north of Cianwood is a coord_event trigger — when player steps past the northern edge of Cianwood, the cutscene fires if EVENT_SUICUNE_BURNED_TOWER is set (i.e., they've seen Suicune flee from Burned Tower). Eusine battles the player here in pokecrystal; confirm his team: Drowzee Lv25, Haunter Lv26, Electrode Lv35.

unstructured_notes: The Rocket Hideout event flag chain is critical: player cannot enter the true B1F of the Rocket HQ until Lance has been met at Lake of Rage (EVENT_LANCES_DRAGONITE_USED or similar). Lance's NPC at Lake of Rage triggers after the Red Gyarados encounter clears (EVENT_RED_GYARADOS_SEEN). The Electrode puzzle: three Electrodes at Lv23 guard generator switches. The player must defeat/catch all three to disable the signal equipment. If the player catches them, they are removed as obstacles; if KO'd, same result. The password 'HAIL GIOVANNI' is learned by talking to a Team Rocket Murkrow NPC in B2F — it lets you pass a locked door in B2F. Lance heals the player's party at a specific point in B3F before the Executive Archer battle. Mahogany Gym requires EVENT_CLEARED_ROCKET_HQ to unlock (Pryce will not battle until the Hideout is cleared). The toll gate on Route 43 is a special warp-blocking NPC script: during the Rocket arc, the two grunts at (17,31)/(17,35) demand payment; after clearing Rocket HQ they disappear. Route 42 Suicune coord_event: Suicune appears on Route 42 (near west Mt. Mortar entrance) during the Suicune storyline as encounter #3; trigger is EVENT_SUICUNE_CIANWOOD being set.

unstructured_notes: Key audit concern for Rocket HQ: the hidden entrance is scripted as a scene script on the Mahogany Mart 1F map — the shop NPC normally blocks the back area, but after EVENT_LANCE_AT_LAKE_OF_RAGE fires, the blocking NPC disappears and a staircase warp becomes active. Verify this NPC removal logic works. Also verify Lance's mid-dungeon heal script: in pokecrystal, Lance uses a script command to heal the party at a fixed map coordinate in B3F; the heal is unconditional once he's present. Red Gyarados: in pokecrystal it is implemented as a static object using trainer_object_event with a special flag for shininess; confirm that the shiny DVs (Atk=2, Def/Spd/SpAtk=10 for Red Gyarados's specific DV spread) produce the correct shiny status check. Route 43 toll gate: after clearing Rocket HQ, the two blocking Grunt NPCs should have their event flags cleared so they disappear and Route 43 is freely passable.

- Ice sliding puzzle connections

## Blackthorn City
### NPCs
- Clair (gym leader)
- Move Deleter (Blackthorn house)

### Gym
**Blackthorn Gym — Clair (Dragon)**
- Puzzle: Lava/platform puzzle on 2F, fall through holes
- Leader: Dragonair Lv37 x3, Kingdra Lv40
- Special: Clair refuses badge — must pass Dragon's Den quiz
- Reward: Rising Badge, TM24 DragonBreath (after Dragon's Den)

### Dragon's Den
- B1F: Water area (requires Surf)
- Items: Calcium (itemball), Max Elixer (itemball)
- Dragon Fang (from Dragon Shrine Elder)
- Hidden: Revive, Max Potion, Max Elixer
- Dragon Shrine: Quiz from Elder (emphasize kindness -> ExtremeSpeed Dratini)

unstructured_notes: Ice Path has no trainers — it is purely a puzzle dungeon. The ice sliding mechanic works as follows: when the player steps onto an ice tile they slide until hitting a wall tile or a normal-friction tile. The B2F split into 'Mahogany Side' and 'Blackthorn Side' is important — these are actually the same floor level but separated by the ice puzzle path. Players must navigate through B3F to get between the two sides. HM07 Waterfall is the key find in Ice Path 1F and is the last HM in the game. Clair's refusal scene is a unique scripted event: after the battle ends, instead of the normal badge-giving script, a special scene plays where Clair walks away and says the player hasn't proven themselves; a scene_event triggers the Dragon's Den quest. After passing the Elder's quiz, Clair appears outside the Dragon's Den shrine and gives TM24 DragonBreath plus the Rising Badge. The ExtremeSpeed Dratini: if the player answers ALL questions optimally (not just one 'correct' answer), the Elder also gives a Dratini Lv15 knowing ExtremeSpeed. This Dratini cannot be obtained any other way. Move Deleter is critical for HM moves — implement as a menu-based NPC that lists all party Pokemon then their moves, deletes the chosen move, and confirms. Unlike Gen 1, there is no restriction on which moves can be deleted.

unstructured_notes: Route 45 is notable for being a one-way route — the southern portion is only reachable by jumping ledges, so players cannot backtrack from Route 46 to Blackthorn via Route 45. The 8 trainers include a mix of Hikers (Rock/Ground types), Blackbelts (Fighting), and Cooltrainers (mixed). Wild Pokemon on Route 45 in Crystal differ from Gold/Silver: Gligar is Gold-exclusive in wild, Skarmory is Silver-exclusive; Crystal has Phanpy and Donphan instead (check pokecrystal wild tables for exact Crystal Route 45 encounter data — the johto_locations.md may reflect composite data). Dark Cave is functionally two separate dungeon sections that happen to be the same cave — the Violet entrance and Blackthorn entrance do not connect without full HM access. TM13 Snore is found in the Blackthorn entrance section of Dark Cave. The Blackglasses item mentioned in johto_locations.md for Dark Cave is likely in the Blackthorn entrance section. The Route 29/Route 46 Gate is a simple pass-through building with Tuscany's scheduled appearance. Tuscany appears only on Tuesdays after the player has earned the Zephyr Badge; she is one of the 7 'Day-of-Week Siblings' who each appear on a different day and give an item. The gate building itself has no warp complexity — it is a straight north-south pass-through.

unstructured_notes: Victory Road is a single-level cave (despite being called 'Victory Road', it is simpler than the Gen 1 version). TM22 SolarBeam listed in johto_locations.md as hidden — verify this exists in pokecrystal; it may be a different hidden item. Rival Battle 5 in Victory Road has Silver's final Johto team. His starter is fully evolved (Feraligatr/Meganium/Typhlosion at Lv37). After this battle, Silver has a moment of reflection dialogue — he questions his approach to Pokemon training. This is story-important: he begins his character arc toward redemption. E4 room structure: each room is a separate map. After defeating an E4 member, a warp opens to the next room. The player cannot skip rooms. The Pokemon Center at Indigo Plateau is accessible between attempts — the player can heal their party at the Pokecenter, then re-enter the E4 rooms. However, once they start the E4 sequence (enter Will's room), they must complete it or black out. Lance has three Dragonite — technically Dragonite only evolves at Lv55 but his Lv47 Dragonites are underleveled for evolution (pokecrystal uses trainer_pokemon data which overrides normal evolution checks). This is a known 'design flaw' in the original game — do NOT fix it; reproduce faithfully. Hall of Fame: each Pokemon's sprite, name, level, OT, and ID are recorded. The Hall of Fame data is stored in SRAM.

unstructured_notes: Tin Tower is the most warp-complex dungeon in the game with 10 floors each having maze-like staircase arrangements. The 7F teleport warps are unique — stepping on specific floor tiles teleports the player to different positions on 7F or adjacent floors. This is implemented in pokecrystal using coord_events with warp commands. The 'Roof' is technically floor 10 but labeled separately; Ho-Oh is on the Roof, not 7F (the special_encounters.md entry of '7F' appears to be an error in that file — cross-reference with pokecrystal Tin Tower map scripts for correct floor placement). In Crystal, the Suicune encounter at Tin Tower base is a static encounter BEFORE entering the tower interior, triggered by a coord_event on the Ecruteak Tin Tower Entrance map when the player has the Clear Bell and all beast flags set. The Silver Wing requirement for Lugia: in Crystal the Silver Wing comes from a Pewter City old man (post-all-16-badges), not the Radio Tower Director. The Radio Tower Director in Crystal gives the Clear Bell. This is a Crystal-specific difference from Gold/Silver. Confirm this in pokecrystal data. Whirl Islands: the 4 entrance caves each deposit the player at different points of B1F. NW and NE entrances both connect to the main B1F passage. SE entrance is the correct path to Lugia (leads to a specific section of B1F not reachable from NW/NE without navigating). SW leads to a dead end with only an Ultra Ball.

unstructured_notes: The most critical audit concern for this sprint is the E4 sequential locking. In pokecrystal, each E4 room after the first is a map object that is only accessible after the preceding trainer's battle flag is set. The implementation must check each preceding flag before rendering the warp to the next room. A subtle bug to test: if the player blacks out during E4, they should respawn at the Indigo Plateau Pokemon Center with their money reduced, and all E4 battle flags should be cleared so they must restart from Will. Verify this reset behavior. For Tin Tower: cross-reference the Ho-Oh floor location against pokecrystal TinTower_Roof map scripts in the ASM source — the data files have an inconsistency between 'Roof' (warp_connections.md) and '7F' (special_encounters.md). The pokecrystal ASM is the ground truth. Whirl Islands water maze: the Waterfall HM is required to ascend a waterfall inside the SE entrance path — confirm the waterfall tile check fires correctly when the player lacks the HM or the Rising Badge. Lugia's Silver Wing check: the flag must be set in the player's bag before the static encounter fires; just having seen Silver Wing is not sufficient.

Implement Unown DV-to-letter formula — Unown's letter form is determined by taking middle 2 bits of each DV (Atk, Def, Speed, Special), combining into 8-bit value, dividing by 10 (integer); 0=A through 25=Z; wild Unown DVs must be constrained to only produce letters in the unlocked set; each Unown letter has an associated Pokedex 'word' (ANGRY, BEAR, CHASE, etc.)

source_data.ruins_of_alph.md:
## Puzzle Chambers

### 1. Kabuto Chamber
**Puzzle:** Rearrange tiles to form Kabuto
**Wall Hint:** ESCAPE (written in Unown text)
**Secret:** Use an Escape Rope while in the chamber to open the hidden wall
**Unlocks:** Unown A through K (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_A_TO_K)
**Hidden Items (Item Room):** Berry, PsnCureBerry, Heal Powder, EnergyPowder

### 2. Omanyte Chamber
**Puzzle:** Rearrange tiles to form Omanyte
**Wall Hint:** WATER (written in Unown text)
**Secret:** Have a Water Stone in your bag while in the chamber
**Unlocks:** Unown L through R (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_L_TO_R)
**Hidden Items (Item Room):** MysteryBerry, Mystic Water, Stardust, Star Piece

### 3. Aerodactyl Chamber
**Puzzle:** Rearrange tiles to form Aerodactyl
**Wall Hint:** LIGHT (written in Unown text)
**Secret:** Use Flash while in the chamber
**Unlocks:** Unown S through W (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_S_TO_W)
**Hidden Items (Item Room):** Gold Berry, Moon Stone, Heal Powder, Energy Root

### 4. Ho-Oh Chamber
**Puzzle:** Rearrange tiles to form Ho-Oh
**Wall Hint:** HO OH (written in Unown text)
**Secret:** Have Ho-Oh in your party while in the chamber
**Unlocks:** Unown X through Z (ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS_X_TO_Z)
**Hidden Items (Item Room):** Gold Berry, MysteryBerry, Revival Herb, Charcoal

## Unown Pokedex Words

| Letter | Word |
|--------|------|
| A | ANGRY |
| B | BEAR |
| C | CHASE |
| D | DIRECT |
| E | ENGAGE |
| F | FIND |
| G | GIVE |
| H | HELP |
| I | INCREASE |
| J | JOIN |
| K | KEEP |
| L | LAUGH |
| M | MAKE |
| N | NUZZLE |
| O | OBSERVE |
| P | PERFORM |
| Q | QUICKEN |
| R | REASSURE |
| S | SEARCH |
| T | TELL |
| U | UNDO |
| V | VANISH |
| W | WANT |
| X | XXXXX |
| Y | YIELD |
| Z | ZOOM |

## Unown Stats and Mechanics

**Type:** Psychic
**Base Stats:** 48 HP / 72 Atk / 48 Def / 48 Spd / 72 SpAtk / 48 SpDef (BST: 336)
**Only Move:** Hidden Power (type and power vary by DVs)
**Catch Rate:** 225 (fairly easy)
**Wild Levels:** 5 (inner chamber encounters)

**Determining Unown's Letter:**
- Take the middle 2 bits of each DV: Atk, Def, Speed, Special
- Combine into an 8-bit value
- Divide by 10 to get the letter (0=A, 1=B, ..., 25=Z)

## Unlockable Unown Letters
| Puzzle Solved | Unown Letters Unlocked |
|--------------|----------------------|
| Kabuto | A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K (11 letters) |
| Omanyte | L, M, N, O, P, Q, R (7 letters) |
| Aerodactyl | S, T, U, V, W (5 letters) |
| Ho-Oh | X, Y, Z (3 letters) |

unstructured_notes: The Ruins of Alph puzzle mechanic: the sliding tile puzzle is a 4x4 or similar grid where tiles must be rearranged to form the image of the target Pokemon. In pokecrystal, this is implemented as a dedicated mini-game engine within the script system. Each puzzle chamber checks if the puzzle is solved on exit; a solved puzzle plays a special animation and drops the player into the Inner Chamber. The secret wall mechanic: each chamber has a bg_event (background event) that checks a specific condition (Escape Rope used / Water Stone in bag / Flash used / Ho-Oh present). When the condition is met, a wall tile changes to a passable tile. This must be persistent (stored in event flags) so the wall stays open on return visits. The Unown DV constraint: when generating wild Unown, the RNG must produce DVs that map to an unlocked letter. In pokecrystal, this is achieved by re-rolling the Atk and Def DVs until the letter index falls within the unlocked range, or by using a lookup table for valid DV combinations. The ENGINE_UNLOCKED_UNOWNS flags are a bitmask system — implement as a 4-bit field where bit 0 = Kabuto puzzle solved, bit 1 = Omanyte, bit 2 = Aerodactyl, bit 3 = Ho-Oh. The radio signal inside the ruins ('??? channel') plays unique audio — this is cosmetic only and can be stubbed as a special frequency check in the radio system. Research Center Pokedex upgrade: requires a new Pokedex mode that shows all 26 Unown letters as separate 'caught' entries, separate from the main 251-species Pokedex.

unstructured_notes: Route 26 and 27 are the post-game connector routes between Johto and Kanto. They contain relatively high-level wild Pokemon (via Surf) and some of the strongest trainer battles in the game outside the E4. The Day of Week Siblings House on Route 26 contains 7 NPC siblings who each appear on a specific day of the week and give a held item: they are the 7 'Day Siblings' (Todd on Monday gives Odd Incense or similar — verify exact schedule against pokecrystal phone_call_content or event_data_tables). The Route 26 Heal House allows the player to heal their party for free — useful when grinding or before/after the E4. Tohjo Falls is important as the gateway between regions and requires Waterfall to ascend. The Moon Stone in Tohjo Falls is found at a specific coordinate and is a one-time pickup. Mt. Mortar is the largest optional dungeon in Johto — it requires multiple HMs for full exploration but the Karate King and Tyrogue are in a section reachable without all HMs (just multiple Blackbelt trainers to defeat). Dragon Scale in 2F Inside is the only way to get this item in the game (used to evolve Seadra into Kingdra when traded).

unstructured_notes: The Unown DV formula is the most technically subtle aspect of this audit. The formula from ruins_of_alph.md: extract middle 2 bits from each of 4 DVs (Atk, Def, Speed, Special), pack into 8 bits, divide by 10. Example: if Atk DV = 0b1010 (10), middle 2 bits = 0b01 = 1; if Def = 0b0110 (6), middle 2 = 0b01 = 1; if Speed = 0b1110 (14), middle 2 = 0b11 = 3; if Special = 0b0100 (4), middle 2 = 0b10 = 2. Combined 8-bit: 0b01013210 -- wait, the packing is sequential: (Atk_bits << 6) | (Def_bits << 4) | (Speed_bits << 2) | Special_bits. This 8-bit value / 10 gives letter 0-25. Run a unit test on this formula with known test vectors from pokecrystal. Ruins of Alph has a known interaction: if the player uses Escape Rope to exit the Kabuto Chamber BEFORE solving the puzzle, the secret wall should NOT open -- the secret wall only opens AFTER the puzzle is solved AND the Escape Rope is used. Verify this ordering. Mt. Mortar audit: verify that Surf encounters in Mt. Mortar produce Marill (one of the Johto-only Pokemon). Route 26/27 border gate: the border NPCs check EVENT_JOHTO_E4_BEATEN; if not set, they say 'Go back to Johto, trainer!' and block movement. Once set, they clear and the player can walk freely between regions.

unstructured_notes: Sprout Tower and Tin Tower have different encounter tables per floor but share the same species (Rattata morn/day, Gastly night). Encounter tables must be stored as an array indexed by floor. The encounter rate mechanic uses a step counter to roll a 255-check per step; grass rate is typically 25/255 and cave rate is 10/255 (slightly less frequent). Time boundaries: morning 4:00-9:59, day 10:00-17:59, night 18:00-3:59. Fishing tables: Old Rod catches Magikarp almost exclusively. Good Rod and Super Rod have location-specific tables. National Park has special contest-only tables that activate during Bug Catching Contest hours (Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday). Route 43 has Yanma and Girafarig which are version-specific rarities. Mareep appears on Route 32 but is not in the numeric encounter list from wild_encounters.md — cross-check with johto_locations.md which mentions Mareep Lv6 on Route 32. The wild_encounters.md data from pokecrystal takes precedence.

unstructured_notes: Trainer money reward formula: base_money * highest_party_level. Each trainer class has a base_money value. Johto gym trainers must be placed in exact map object positions matching pokecrystal map scripts. Gym leader rematch data is handled in a later sprint (166). Critical data points: Morty's gym uses invisible floor puzzle — trainers are positioned on the invisible floor path. Chuck's Poliwrath has DynamicPunch at 50% accuracy, which frequently misses but is devastating. Clair refuses to give badge after battle; player must pass Dragon's Den quiz — implement flag to track quiz completion gating badge receipt. Jasmine's Steelix has Iron Tail, the move Jasmine's gym is themed around. Note that Whitney's Miltank uses Rollout which doubles each hit and pairs with Attract to be infamously difficult. The Team Rocket Radio Tower takeover is a later-game event (sprint 158) and uses a different set of grunts from the Mahogany arc. Route trainer data for all 18 Johto routes must be cross-referenced with johto_map_scripts.md for exact trainer placement coordinates.

source_data.kanto_locations.md:
Vermilion City: Pokemon Center, Poke Mart, Lt. Surge Gym (Electric), S.S. Aqua dock (post-game), Diglett's Cave entrance, Route 6 North gate. Key items: SS Ticket access point, Bike Pass. Gym: Lt. Surge — Raichu Lv44, Electrode Lv40 x2, Magneton Lv40, Electabuzz Lv46. Badge: Thunder Badge.

Route 6: Connects Saffron (north) to Vermilion (south). Wild encounters: Drowzee, Rattata, Pidgey (morn/day). Trainers: Schoolboys, Lasses.

Saffron City: Silph Co. (exterior only, interior closed post-game), Copycat's house, Mr. Psychic (TM29 Psychic), Fighting Dojo (Hitmonlee or Hitmonchan choice, Gen 1 callback — in Crystal gives no Pokemon), Sabrina Gym (Psychic). Magnet Train Station. Key items: TM29 Psychic (Mr. Psychic), Pass (after Copycat quest). Gym: Sabrina — Espeon Lv34, Mr. Mime Lv34, Jynx Lv38, Alakazam Lv38. Badge: Marsh Badge. Copycat wants lost Clefairy Doll — obtain from Vermilion Magnet Train station lost and found after talking to Copycat.

unstructured_notes: Vermilion is the entry point to Kanto post-game arriving by S.S. Aqua from Olivine. The dock area of Vermilion must handle the S.S. Aqua warp trigger. Lt. Surge uses Lv40-46 Pokemon (significantly stronger than his Gen 1 version — Kanto gyms are post-game). The Magnet Train is critical for fast travel between Johto and Kanto post-game. The Copycat quest chain: player talks to Copycat in Saffron -> Copycat wants Clefairy Doll -> player finds Clefairy Doll at Vermilion Train Station lost and found -> returns it -> Copycat gives Magnet Train Pass. Silph Co. interior is inaccessible in Crystal (the Gen 1 Team Rocket plot is resolved). Fighting Dojo in Crystal only contains one trainer with Hitmonlee + Hitmonchan but gives no prize Pokemon unlike Gen 1. Route 6 has the Saffron Tunnel system — a north/south underground passage. Wild encounters on Route 6: Drowzee (morn/day), Rattata, Pidgey, Abra (rare).

source_data.kanto_locations.md:
Cerulean City: Pokemon Center, Poke Mart, Misty Gym (Water). Cerulean Cave entrance — requires all 16 badges. Gym: Misty — Golduck Lv42, Quagsire Lv42, Lapras Lv44, Starmie Lv47. Badge: Cascade Badge. Key NPC: Misty is absent until player speaks to her at Route 25 cape (she's on a date; encountering her triggers her return to gym).

Route 24/25: Route 24 has Nugget Bridge (5 trainers, Nugget prize). Route 25 has Bill's original house (Sea Cottage) and NPC near cape. Misty can be found at the cape.

Power Plant: Located on Route 10. Requires Surf to access from Route 10. Contains Zapdos in Gen 1 (no legendary here in Crystal). Machine Part is hidden in the water inside. Sidequest: Gym in Cerulean has Team Rocket Grunt (no trainers to battle in gym normally until Misty returns). Find the grunt, get hint about Machine Part location in Cerulean Gym pool, retrieve part, return to Power Plant.

Route 10 (south of Cerulean Rock Tunnel): connects Cerulean/Rock Tunnel area southward. Wild: Voltorb, Magnemite, Electrode.

unstructured_notes: Misty's gym in Cerulean is empty when player first arrives (her trainers are absent too). She is found at Route 25 cape near the Sea Cottage. The sidequest flow: Player arrives in Cerulean -> gym is unlocked but no Misty -> optional to proceed to Power Plant issue first -> talk to Power Plant manager who mentions machine part theft -> find Rocket Grunt in Cerulean Gym (standing in the gym) -> battle grunt -> grunt says part is in the Cerulean Gym pool -> Surf in the gym to find Machine Part -> return to Power Plant -> EXPN Card reward. After EXPN Card, Kanto Radio Tower (Lavender Town) starts broadcasting and Misty returns to her gym. The Cerulean Cave only unlocks after defeating Red (all 16 badges). Mewtwo is inside Cerulean Cave. Route 24 Nugget Bridge was originally a set piece in Gen 1 with a Team Rocket Grunt at the end offering to recruit the player — the Crystal version retains 5 bridge trainers and a Rocket Grunt but with different dialogue. Wild on Route 24: Oddish/Bellsprout, Caterpie/Weedle, Psyduck.

unstructured_notes: Lavender Town's Pokemon Tower from Gen 1 is fully replaced by the Kanto Radio Tower in Crystal/Gen 2. The Radio Tower in Lavender requires the EXPN Card from Power Plant sidequest to have active broadcasts. The critical broadcast is the Poke Flute channel which enables waking Snorlax on Routes 12 and 16. Snorlax (Lv50) blocks those routes until woken via radio. Celadon Game Corner in Kanto-side is separate from Goldenrod and has different prizes; however, it may share the same slot machine implementation. Celadon Mansion rooftop has TM03 Curse — this is one of the harder items to find. Erika's gym uses Sleep Powder and Sunny Day/SolarBeam strategies. Celadon Dept Store 5F has elemental TMs in Crystal: TM14 Blizzard, TM25 Thunder, TM38 Fire Blast. The Route 7/8 underground passage tunnel connects the two routes without needing to enter a city — implement the gate buildings as interior maps.

unstructured_notes: Janine is Koga's daughter (Koga became an E4 member in Gen 2). Her gym uses the same invisible wall puzzle style as Koga's in Gen 1. Her Crobat has Wing Attack + Confuse Ray, Weezing has Explosion as a last resort (high threat), and Venomoth is the star with Psychic and high Speed. The Safari Zone being closed is important — several species from Gen 1's Safari Zone are now unavailable (Kangaskhan, Tauros, Scyther via Safari) and must be obtained elsewhere or traded. The Super Rod is given by a fisherman on Route 12 — this is the last fishing rod and needed for high-level water encounters (Magikarp 85-100, Horsea, Seadra, Dratini). Route 12 has a second Snorlax (Route 16 has the other one). Both Snorlax are Lv50 and blocking path — exact coordinates needed for event scripting. Routes 13-15 form the 'cycling road' in Gen 1 (Bike required in Gen 1, free in Gen 2). Route 15 connects to Route 14 via gate that leads to Fuchsia.

source_data.all_trainers.md:
## Brock
- Lv41 Graveler: Defense Curl, Rock Slide, Rollout, Earthquake
- Lv41 Rhyhorn: Fury Attack, Scary Face, Earthquake, Horn Drill
- Lv42 Omastar: Bite, Surf, Protect, Spike Cannon
- Lv44 Onix: Bind, Rock Slide, Bide, Sandstorm
- Lv42 Kabutops: Slash, Surf, Endure, Giga Drain

unstructured_notes: Brock's Crystal team is far more powerful than his Gen 1 team and includes fossil Pokemon (Omastar, Kabutops) with Surf — unexpected for a Rock gym leader. His Onix is notably his iconic Gen 1 ace. The 5-Pokemon team is a full post-game challenge. Mt. Moon in Crystal is largely the same layout as Gen 1 but the Team Rocket encounter is replaced by regular trainers. Clefairy in Mt. Moon is rare but important — it is the key source for Moon Stone evolution. The Moon Stone is a ground item in Mt. Moon (B1F). Cerulean Cave is the final dungeon for completionists. In Crystal, Mewtwo is not present (only accessible in FireRed/LeafGreen), but all other species still appear. The cave has a complex looping layout on 3 floors. Ditto and Chansey appear here as relatively rare post-game encounters useful for breeding.

source_data.kanto_locations.md:
Viridian City: Blue's Gym (Normal — all types). Pokemon Center, Poke Mart, Trainer House. Viridian Forest no longer exists as a dungeon (trees were cut down). Route 2 now passes through open area where forest was. Gym: Blue — Pidgeot Lv50, Alakazam Lv54, Rhydon Lv56, Gyarados Lv58, Arcanine Lv58, Exeggutor Lv58. Badge: Earth Badge.

Trainer House: Multi-link Mystery Gift building. Cal is a default NPC trainer when no link data is present: Lv10 Chikorita/Cyndaquil/Totodile (preset 1), Lv30 Bayleef/Quilava/Croconaw (preset 2), Lv50 Meganium/Typhlosion/Feraligatr (preset 3). Cal's team is the same trio as the player's starter.

Route 1: Connects Viridian (north) to Pallet (south). Wild: Rattata, Pidgey (all times). Simple first-route.

Route 22: West of Viridian, leads to Victory Road gate. Wild: Spearow, Ekans (morn/day), Nidoran-F, Nidoran-M. Rival battle in Gen 1 (not in Crystal).

Route 23: North of Victory Road, leads to Indigo Plateau (post-game). Wild: Nidorina, Nidorino, Fearow, Golduck, Ditto.

## PKMNTrainer

unstructured_notes: Blue's Crystal team is a final Kanto boss challenge. His team of Lv50-58 Pokemon uses a diverse type spread — his ace changes per game. In Crystal his team is Pidgeot Lv50, Alakazam Lv54, Rhydon Lv56, Gyarados Lv58, Arcanine Lv58, Exeggutor Lv58. The Earth Badge he gives is the last Kanto badge, and combined with all Johto badges (8) gives the full 16 badges for Mt. Silver access. Blue is notably 'hard to find' — his gym is open but he is inside and his gym trainers are sparse. Viridian Forest in Crystal is not a dungeon: the trees were cleared. Route 2 just passes through openly with no forest encounters. Trainer House links to Mystery Gift system — when a real Mystery Gift partner was used the Cal NPC would update. For our implementation Cal defaults to the three presets. Route 22 is short and leads to the Victory Road gate that requires all 16 badges. Route 23 is the canyon corridor between the gate and Indigo Plateau.

unstructured_notes: QA focus: (1) Simulate Brock battle — his Rhyhorn has Horn Drill (OHKO) which at Lv41 vs high-level player should still check level formula correctly (fails if target is higher level). (2) Verify Moon Stone is obtainable from Mt. Moon B1F and that it is a non-respawning item. (3) Blue gym NPC dialogue should say he's the 'strongest trainer in Kanto' and that earning the Earth Badge proves a trainer's worth. (4) Headless simulation: enter Cerulean Cave with 15 badges — confirm denial message. Enter with 16 — confirm access. (5) Trainer House: simulate the Cal battle at all three difficulty tiers, confirm starter choice on Cal's side matches the player's current starter in the party. (6) Verify no Caterpie, Metapod, Weedle, Kakuna, Pikachu, or Oddish encounters on Route 2 (these were Viridian Forest exclusives in Gen 1, not present in Crystal's open route).

source_data.kanto_locations.md:
Pallet Town: Professor Oak's Lab, Player's House (Gen 1 origin), Blue's House. No Pokemon Center or Mart. Wild (Surf): Tentacool Lv20-24 (90%), Tentacruel Lv20-24 (10%). Oak gives Running Shoes in some versions — in Crystal he just offers lore dialogue post-game. Key NPC: Blue's sister Daisy gives grooming to raise happiness.

Route 21: Surf required. Connects Pallet (north) to Cinnabar (south). Wild (Surf): Tentacool, Tentacruel. Wild (Fishing): Krabby, Kingler, Staryu (Super Rod).

Cinnabar Island: Volcanic eruption destroyed most of the island. Only Blaine's gym remains (relocated to a small cave/tent at the east of the ruins). No Pokemon Mansion. No Pokemon Center. Wild (Surf around ruins): Magmar (morn/day), Tentacool. Gym: Blaine (Fire) — Magcargo Lv38, Magmar Lv38, Rapidash Lv38, Magmar Lv38... [verify from pokecrystal]. Badge: Volcano Badge.

Seafoam Islands: Routes 19-20 passes through. 5-floor dungeon (1F, B1F, B2F, B3F, B4F). Requires Strength + Surf + Waterfall. Wild: Seel, Dewgong, Horsea, Krabby, Jynx (rare). No Articuno in Crystal. Items: Calcium, Max Revive, various.

Blaine (from pokecrystal data, to be verified): typically Growlithe/Ponyta line + Magmar/Rapidash family at Lv36-38 range.

unstructured_notes: Cinnabar Island's destruction is the biggest lore event in the transition from Gen 1 to Gen 2 — a volcanic eruption burned the island. The Pokemon Mansion no longer exists; Blaine moved his gym to the seafoam area on the eastern part of what remains. This means the gym has no indoor trainers in the traditional sense — Blaine's 'gym' is a small cave. The key gameplay implication: no healing available near Cinnabar. Players must stock up or Fly back. Professor Oak in Pallet Town gives post-game dialogue about completing the Pokedex. Daisy (Blue's sister) grooms one of your Pokemon once per day to raise happiness — implement as a daily interaction flag. Seafoam Islands share tiles with Route 19-20 in the Surf path. The boulder puzzles from Gen 1 that redirected Seafoam's water flow are still present (using Strength). Articuno's room in Seafoam is empty in Crystal. The Seafoam Islands are a key location for fishing Horsea/Seadra (Super Rod). Route 21 is purely a Surf route — no land wild encounters, only water encounters.

unstructured_notes: Rock Tunnel has no trainers inside — only on the flanking routes. Kangaskhan in B1F is a notable rare find. Diglett's Cave is a critical shortcut that saves significant travel time between Pewter and Vermilion areas. Route 10 is split into north (Power Plant area) and south (Rock Tunnel exit); the split is important for Power Plant sidequest access. Steel Wing TM in Rock Tunnel 1F is valuable. unstructured_notes: Red's battle is the true final boss. The implementation must have: (1) access gated by EVENT_OPENED_MT_SILVER (all 16 badges + Oak visit), (2) music fade-out before encounter, (3) Red's '...' dialogue with no name, (4) post-battle party heal and second credit roll. Larvitar in the upper rooms is significant — it's one of the few places to catch Larvitar in the wild. Pupitar also appears. Mt. Silver rooms do not have a strict linear order — Room 2 branches to Item Rooms. Misdreavus only appears at night in the upper caves.

source_data.wild_encounters.md: Crystal version-exclusive notes: No Mankey/Primeape (Route 42 Gold only), no Growlithe (Routes 35-37 Gold only), no Spinarak (night Route 2 Crystal still has it), no Teddiursa/Ursaring in Kanto routes (Silver only). Electabuzz on Route 10 is Crystal-only among routes. Slugma on Routes 16-18 is Crystal-specific.
source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Crystal-Only Content
- New Pokemon availability changes (e.g., Phanpy/Teddiursa availability differs)
### Gold-Only Pokemon (not in Crystal wild)
- Mankey, Primeape (Route 42)
- Growlithe (Route 35, 36, 37)
- Spinarak, Ariados (Route 2 night in Crystal actually keeps Ariados)
- Teddiursa, Ursaring (Route 45, Mt. Silver — different per version)
### Silver-Only Pokemon
- Vulpix, Ninetales (Route 36, 37)
- Meowth, Persian (Route 39)
- Phanpy, Donphan (Route 45, Mt. Silver)
- Skarmory (Route 45)

unstructured_notes: Kanto wild encounters use the same morning/day/night system as Johto. Most Kanto routes have Gen 2 Pokemon added (Snubbull, Slugma, Houndour on night routes) that weren't in Gen 1. Crystal has unique wild availability vs Gold/Silver — use pokecrystal ASM as the definitive source. Key rare encounters: Pikachu on Route 2, Chansey on Routes 13-14, Mr. Mime on Route 21, Electabuzz on Route 10 (day). Cerulean Cave has Ditto and Electrode at decent rates. Silver Cave upper floors have Larvitar which is only available in Mt. Silver in Crystal.

unstructured_notes: Key QA: Red must NOT appear before all 16 badges are collected and Oak is visited. If game is in post-game state with only 15 badges, Red should be absent. Post-battle HealParty is a special routine — verify it heals all party members to full HP and restores PP before credits. Cerulean Cave is notable: Mewtwo does NOT appear (Gen 1 only) but Ditto, Electrode, and Misdreavus (night) do. Ensure no Mewtwo encounter data was accidentally included.

source_data.gym_leader_rematches.md:
In Pokemon Crystal, gym leaders do NOT have rematches.
Rematches are available from registered Phone Trainers.
Phone Trainers (Rematchable) include trainers from National Park, Olivine Lighthouse, Routes 26-39.

unstructured_notes: Important: Kanto gym leaders do NOT have rematch versions in Crystal — rematches are from phone trainers only. Lt. Surge's actual team uses Raichu (not just Electrode) as his primary, which is different from the location reference list — use the all_trainers.md data as authoritative. Blue's team at Lv56-58 is significantly stronger than all other gym leaders. Sabrina's teleporter puzzle uses 32 warp events internally. Janine's gym has a disguise gimmick where 4 trainers look like Janine before revealing themselves. Fuchsia Gym auto-defeats 4 trainers when leader beaten (Alice, Linda, Cindy, Barry).

source_data.game_progression.md:
141. Professor Elm gives S.S. Ticket
142. Board S.S. Aqua at Olivine City port -> arrives in Vermilion City
143. Alternatively: complete Copycat sidequest for Magnet Train Pass (Goldenrod <-> Saffron)

### S.S. Aqua — Travel to Kanto
- Route: Olivine Port -> Vermilion Port
- Events on ship: Various trainer battles, meet rival's Pokeball (in a cabin)

unstructured_notes: S.S. Aqua is the primary method of traveling from Johto to Kanto post-game. The ship has multiple floors/decks and various cabins. A notable story beat involves a locked cabin where Silver's rival events are referenced. The sailor NPC who gave HM04 Strength in Olivine Cafe is separate from the ship crew. Day-of-week schedule is important: Wednesday and Sunday from Olivine, Monday and Friday from Vermilion — missing these days means waiting. Magnet Train is the alternative fast travel (Saffron<->Goldenrod) but requires Copycat's Lost Item quest first. S.S. Ticket from Elm is required to board.

source_data.kanto_map_scripts.md:
### Saffron Magnet Train Station
Connects Saffron <-> Goldenrod. Requires Pass (from Copycat quest) and EVENT_RESTORED_POWER_TO_KANTO.

### Vermilion Gym
Gym Puzzle: 15 trash can bg_events (3x5 grid). In Gen 2 Crystal, traps are not active (Gym Guide confirms: 'the traps aren't active right now').

### Fuchsia Gym
Gym Gimmick: All 4 gym trainers disguised as Janine (using SPRITE_JANINE / variable sprites). Each spins when interacted with, then reveals true identity:
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_1 -> Lass Alice
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_2 -> Lass Linda
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_3 -> Picnicker Cindy
- SPRITE_FUCHSIA_GYM_4 -> Camper Barry
After each battle, sprite changes via variablesprite to show real identity.

unstructured_notes: Critical QA: Surge's trash can puzzle is INACTIVE in Crystal (unlike Gen 1) — the Gym Guide explicitly says so. Do not implement an active trash can mechanic. Blue's gym has no gym trainers at all — just Blue and the Gym Guide. Misty requires Power Plant quest completion before she appears in her gym. Brock's Gym has only 1 trainer (Camper Jerry). The Magnet Train requires two conditions: PASS item AND power restoration — both must be checked.

## 16. Goldenrod Radio Tower Crisis

Trigger: Encounter Silver in Underground during Radio Tower crisis

## 20. Victory Road
### Victory Road — Rival Battle 5
Trigger: Encounter Silver in Victory Road

Dialogue: Silver beginning to question his approach to Pokemon

### Mt. Moon Square — SCENE_MOUNTMOON_RIVAL_BATTLE
Rival appears, walks toward player
Battle: loadtrainer RIVAL2, RIVAL2_1_TOTODILE/CHIKORITA/CYNDAQUIL
After: EVENT_BEAT_RIVAL_IN_MT_MOON

unstructured_notes: The rival (Silver) has one of the most complex trainer data sets in the game: 3 variants per encounter (one per player starter), 8 encounter locations. His team always uses the type-advantage starter vs the player. Recurring team members: Gastly->Haunter, Zubat->Golbat, Magnemite->Magneton, and eventually Sneasel and Kadabra join. The Kanto Mt. Moon battle uses RIVAL2 trainer data (a separate set from RIVAL1) with a fully evolved team around Lv40. Silver's dialogue arc across the battles is important for characterization — early: arrogant thief, Victory Road: beginning to doubt himself.
