source_data.elite_four_strategies.md:
Pre-E4 Preparation: Recommended Level Range 42-50
Will: Difficulty 3/10 — Dark-type moves hit everything super-effectively
Koga: Difficulty 4/10 — Koga is annoying, not hard; bring Full Heals for status
Koga Forretress: Only weak to Fire (4x). Beware Explosion.
Koga Muk: Minimize makes it nearly unhittable. Use Swift.
Bruno: Difficulty 3/10 — Psychic or Flying sweeps most of team
Bruno Hitmontop: Beware Counter — use special attacks
Bruno Machamp at Lv46: Foresight removes Ghost immunity
Karen: Houndoom Lv47 is dangerous; Gengar with Destiny Bond is a trap
Lance: 3 Dragonite require Ice Beam/Blizzard. No badge boost for champion

1. Will (Psychic): Xatu Lv40, Jynx Lv41, Exeggutor Lv41, Slowbro Lv41, Xatu Lv42
2. Koga (Poison): Ariados Lv40, Venomoth Lv41, Forretress Lv43, Muk Lv42, Crobat Lv44
3. Bruno (Fighting): Hitmontop Lv42, Hitmonlee Lv42, Hitmonchan Lv42, Onix Lv43, Machamp Lv46
4. Karen (Dark): Umbreon Lv42, Vileplume Lv42, Gengar Lv45, Murkrow Lv44, Houndoom Lv47

unstructured_notes: The E4 uses Gen 2 rules: no bag items allowed in battle but trainer can use items they have set. Koga (not Agatha) is E4 #2 in Crystal because he was moved from Fuchsia Gym. Koga's Ariados uses Spider Web + Baton Pass to trap and pass the trap to a sweeper — this is a notable AI combo. Lance has three Dragonite, which was a Gen 2 controversy since the game itself says it's illegal at that level. Hall of Fame registration captures the full party data (level, species, moves) and stores it in SRAM. After credits, Elm gives S.S. Ticket on next visit.

unstructured_notes: Team Rocket has 4 named executives: Proton (Slowpoke Well + Radio Tower Underground), Petrel (Radio Tower), Ariana (Radio Tower Underground), Archer (Radio Tower Underground final boss). Archer's dialogue references Giovanni and Team Rocket's legacy. The Mahogany Base password 'HAIL GIOVANNI' is obtained by listening to a Murkrow NPC — this is an interactive eavesdropping mechanic. Lance heals the player's party mid-dungeon in the Mahogany Base, which is a unique mechanic. The Electrode switches must be defeated (battle) to disable the broadcast machine — they are BATTLETYPE_WILD encounters, not trainer battles. Clear Bell (Crystal exclusive) triggers Suicune's final encounter chain at Tin Tower.

source_data.special_encounters.md:
### Suicune (Crystal Storyline)
Location: Multiple encounters, final battle at Tin Tower
Level: 40
In Pokemon Crystal, Suicune has a unique storyline. You encounter it at:
1. Burned Tower (flees, alongside Raikou and Entei)
2. Cianwood City (cutscene, flees)
3. Route 42 (cutscene, flees)
4. Vermilion City (cutscene, flees)
5. Route 14 (cutscene, flees)
6. Tin Tower B1F (static battle, Lv40) — requires Clear Bell + all 3 Legendary Beasts seen
Moves: Rain Dance, Gust, Aurora Beam, Mist
Catch Rate: 3

### Raikou (Roaming)
Level: 40
Mechanics: Appears randomly in Johto grass areas. Flees immediately on encounter. HP and status persist between encounters.
- Changes route every time you change routes
- Cannot encounter it on water or in caves
- If KOed, it is gone forever (no respawn)
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Roar, Thunder Shock, Reflect, Crunch

### Entei (Roaming)
Level: 40
Mechanics: Same roaming mechanics as Raikou.
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Roar, Fire Spin, Stomp, Flamethrower

### Lugia
Location: Whirl Islands B2F
Level: 60
Requirements: Silver Wing
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Aeroblast, Safeguard, Gust, Recover

### Ho-Oh
Location: Tin Tower 7F
Level: 60
Requirements: Rainbow Wing + Clear Bell
Catch Rate: 3
Moves: Sacred Fire, Safeguard, Gust, Recover

## Roaming Mechanics
- Both released when you fall through the Burned Tower floor
- They roam Johto routes (grass encounters only)
- Each time you enter a new route, they move to a random adjacent route
- If you enter their current route, there's a chance to encounter them in grass
- They always attempt to flee on the first turn (Roar forces you out too)
- HP, status, and catch attempts persist across encounters
- If you KO either one, they disappear permanently
- Using Fly/Teleport to a route they're on works — they don't move when you Fly
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
### Burned Tower B1F — Release the Beasts
- Trigger: Fall through floor to B1F (coord_event), SCENE_BURNEDTOWERB1F_RELEASE_THE_BEASTS
- Cutscene: Raikou, Entei, Suicune awaken and flee one by one
  - Raikou runs left, Entei runs right, Suicune pauses to look at player then runs
- Flags set: EVENT_RELEASED_THE_BEASTS
- Enables: Raikou and Entei begin roaming Johto (special InitRoamMons)
- Enables: Suicune appears at Cianwood City (Crystal)
### Suicune Chase Sequence (Crystal-exclusive)
1. Burned Tower B1F (Ecruteak) — flees with beasts
2. Cianwood City (north rocks) — Eusine present, flees
3. Route 42 — flees
4. Vermilion City — flees
5. Route 14 — flees
6. Tin Tower (with Clear Bell) — final battle Lv40

unstructured_notes: Roaming Pokemon use a special data structure tracking their current map group/map number, remaining HP, status byte, and caught flag. When the player transitions between overworld maps that count as 'routes' (not cities/caves), the game calls UpdateRoamMons to randomly relocate each roaming beast to an adjacent valid route. The roaming encounter check happens during the normal grass encounter roll — if the player's current route matches the roamer's route, there's a secondary check to potentially trigger the roamer instead of a normal wild Pokemon. Suicune's cutscene encounters (not the final battle) are map script events, not battle triggers — they use sprite movement commands and flee without entering battle mode. The Eusine character appears at the Cianwood encounter but has his own trainer battle later in Dragon's Den.

unstructured_notes: Lapras is unique among these encounters as a weekly respawn (every Friday) rather than a truly one-time encounter. The game checks the day-of-week flag for Lapras's Union Cave tile. Red Gyarados's guaranteed shiny requires hardcoded DVs: pokecrystal sets DVs to 1/0/14/14/14/14 (Attack DV 1 produces female gender and shiny when Special DV is 10+). The Odd Egg at the Pokemon Communication Center (Crystal-only building replacing Goldenrod Pokemon Center 2F) has a 14% shiny chance encoded as a special hatching flag. Snorlax actually blocks the path to Diglett's Cave (Route 11) and is NOT on Routes 12/16 in Crystal — the Radio Tower in Lavender enables the PokeFlute frequency on the Pokegear radio, and the player must stand next to the Snorlax and open the radio to trigger the encounter.

source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
Each sighting sets a flag: EVENT_SAW_SUICUNE_AT_[LOCATION]

### Tin Tower Entrance — Kimono Girls (Crystal)
- Trigger: Clear Bell + all 8 badges
- What happens: Must battle 5 Kimono Girls again at Tin Tower entrance
- After winning: Suicune appears at Tin Tower base (Lv40, static encounter)

### Tin Tower Roof — Ho-Oh (Crystal)
- Trigger: Rainbow Wing (from Pewter City old man after all 16 badges)
- Battle: Ho-Oh Lv60
- Moves: Sacred Fire, Sunny Day, Fire Blast, Swift

unstructured_notes: Key QA concern: the Tin Tower Suicune encounter has two requirements — Clear Bell AND having 'seen' all three beasts (via Pokedex). The game checks a bitmask of Suicune encounter flags, not just the final one. Also note that Ho-Oh's moves in the story_events document list 'Sacred Fire, Sunny Day, Fire Blast, Swift' while the special_encounters document lists 'Sacred Fire, Safeguard, Gust, Recover' — the pokecrystal ASM must be checked as the authoritative source (the all_trainers.md or special_encounters.md moves table should reference the actual disassembly data). Always defer to the .asm file. The roaming-on-Fly rule is important for speedrunning strategies and must be faithfully implemented.

source_data.special_encounters.md:
## Gift Pokemon
### Togepi Egg
Location: Violet City Pokemon Center
Given by: Mystery Egg from Mr. Pokemon (Route 30), hatched by Professor Elm's aide
Level: 5 (when hatched)

### Eevee
Location: Goldenrod City (Bill's house, after meeting him in Ecruteak)
Level: 20
Note: Only one per save file. Choose evolution wisely.

### Shuckle (Mania)
Location: Cianwood City (Kirk/Mania's house)
Level: 15
Held Item: Berry
Note: He asks you to take care of it. He may ask for it back later — say no to keep it.

### Spearow (Kenya)
Location: Route 35 gate
Given by: Guard in the gate
Level: 10
Held Item: Mail (letter to deliver to Route 31)
OT: Webster
Note: Deliver the mail to the guy on Route 31, or keep Kenya.

### Tyrogue
Location: Mt. Mortar (deep interior)
Given by: Blackbelt Kiyo (after defeating him)
Level: 10

### Dratini
Location: Dragon's Den (after answering Elder's quiz correctly)
Level: 15
Special: If you answer ALL questions correctly, the Dratini knows ExtremeSpeed

## In-Game Trades
| You Give | You Receive | Nickname | Held Item | OT Name | Location |
|----------|------------|----------|-----------|---------|----------|
| Abra | Machop | MUSCLE | Gold Berry | MIKE | Goldenrod Dept Store 5F |
| Bellsprout | Onix | ROCKY | Bitter Berry | KYLE | Violet City (house near gym) |
| Krabby | Voltorb | VOLTY | PrzCureBerry | TIM | Olivine City |
| Dragonair | Dodrio | DORIS | Smoke Ball | EMY | Blackthorn City (female player only) |
| Haunter | Xatu | PAUL | MysteryBerry | CHRIS | Cianwood City |
| Chansey | Aerodactyl | AEROY | Gold Berry | KIM | Route 14 |
| Dugtrio | Magneton | MAGGIE | Metal Coat | FOREST | Power Plant |

Notes:
- Trade #4 (Dragonair -> Dodrio) requires a female player character
- Traded Pokemon gain boosted EXP (1.5x)
- All traded Pokemon have preset DVs and OT IDs

unstructured_notes: In-game trades use the NPC trade engine which requires a matching species in the player's party. The traded-away Pokemon is removed, and the received Pokemon is generated with preset DVs and OT data from npc_trades.asm. All received trade Pokemon have preset happiness (usually 70) and level matching what is defined in the trade data. The Haunter->Xatu trade does NOT trigger Haunter's trade evolution to Gengar because the received Pokemon is Xatu (the given Haunter becomes an NPC's Pokemon and is not returned). The Kenya sidequest involves a scripted mail item attached to the Spearow; removing the mail from the Spearow before delivering it breaks the quest but lets the player keep Kenya. The female-only Dragonair trade checks the player character gender flag stored in save data.

abstract_tasks: Implement hidden item data structures: per-map list of hidden item (x, y, item_id, event_flag) tuples loaded from pokecrystal hidden_items_complete.md
Implement Itemfinder mechanic: play direction-indicating beeps when player is adjacent to (within 1-2 tiles) a hidden item, beep intensity varies by distance
Implement hidden item pickup: pressing A on the correct tile with the correct event flag unset gives the item and sets the flag permanently (most hidden items are one-time)
Implement respawning hidden items: certain hidden items (notably Moon Stone at Mt. Moon on Monday nights) use time-based flags rather than permanent event flags
Verify all 84 hidden item coordinates match pokecrystal data exactly — coordinates are map-local (x, y) not world coordinates
Implement special hidden item interaction: Amulet Coin in Goldenrod Underground, Leftovers in Celadon Restaurant trash can, Soft Sand on Route 34 beach, each with correct event flag

unstructured_notes: The Itemfinder in pokecrystal is a key item that when activated scans nearby tiles for hidden items using a proximity check. The beep pattern changes based on closeness. Hidden items in pokecrystal are stored in the map's object data as ITEM objects with hidden=TRUE. The event flag for each hidden item is globally unique. Most hidden items are one-time: once picked up the event flag is set and the item does not reappear. Some items with daily/weekly respawn (like the Moon Stone at Mt. Moon Square on Monday nights, and berry trees) use the ENGINE_DAILY flags or RTC-based checks rather than permanent event flags. The Leftovers in the Celadon Restaurant trash can is one of the most valuable hidden items in the game due to its held item effect and should be verified against pokecrystal map data for exact coordinates.

unstructured_notes: Key QA scenario: the Haunter->Xatu trade should NOT trigger Gengar evolution even though Haunter normally evolves via trade. The NPC trade engine gives a pre-generated Pokemon (Xatu) rather than evolving the traded-in Pokemon, so no evolution check is needed for the received Pokemon. For hidden items, the QA should confirm that items behind locked event gates (e.g., Cleanse Tag at Tin Tower 5F which requires ascending the tower) are not accessible before the relevant event flags. Also confirm that the Odd Egg at the Pokemon Communication Center (Crystal exclusive) generates a random species from the 7 possible baby Pokemon and applies the 14% shiny chance — the species selection uses RNG at hatch time, not at gift time.

source_data.phone_engine.md:
## Phone Contact Struct (PHONE_CONTACT_SIZE bytes)
| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| PHONE_CONTACT_TRAINER_CLASS | Trainer class (0 for non-trainer) |
| PHONE_CONTACT_TRAINER_ID | Trainer ID within class |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_BANK | Bank for player-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_ADDR | Address for player-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT1_TIME | Time-of-day mask for player calls |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_BANK | Bank for NPC-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_ADDR | Address for NPC-initiated call script |
| PHONE_CONTACT_SCRIPT2_TIME | Time-of-day mask for incoming calls |
| PHONE_CONTACT_MAP_GROUP | Map group where contact resides |
| PHONE_CONTACT_MAP_NUMBER | Map number where contact resides |

## Call Timing
- Cycle 0: 20 minutes
- Cycle 1: 10 minutes
- Cycle 2: 5 minutes
- Cycle 3+: 3 minutes

unstructured_notes: Crystal's gym leaders explicitly do NOT offer rematches unlike HeartGold/SoulSilver. Their phone numbers are collectible and their calls only provide tips and rare Pokemon sighting hints. Each gym leader's number is obtainable at a specific location and time-of-day, and the schedule must be implemented faithfully. The phone system's outgoing call vs incoming call scripts are distinct: SCRIPT1 is player-initiated, SCRIPT2 is NPC-initiated. Swarm events triggered by phone calls set a temporary swarm flag that changes which Pokemon appear in the specified route/area's encounter table for the remainder of that in-game day.

unstructured_notes: The Goldenrod Department Store 5F has multiple sub-inventories that depend on player state — early game only has TM Thunderpunch/Fire Punch/Ice Punch (Goldenrod5F1), while later states add TM Headbutt (5F2) and TM Rock Smash (5F3/5F4). These inventory transitions are tied to EVENT flags from Ilex Forest (HM01 Cut = TM Headbutt becomes available) and Rock Smash events. The Mahogany fake shop selling Slowpoketail is intentional game design (satirizing Team Rocket's Slowpoke tail crime ring). The Bargain Shop's daily stock is the same items every day — the 'daily' refers to a full-price refresh, not different items. The Rooftop Sale is a timed event that occurs on certain weekends.

unstructured_notes: A critical QA point: in Crystal, gym leaders cannot be rematched via phone. This is a known difference from later remakes. If the implementation accidentally allows gym leader rematches, it would be a significant deviation from the original. The phone system should only offer rematches for the 23 route trainers. Also verify that swarm notifications actually modify the encounter table for the relevant route (e.g., Dunsparce appears in Dark Cave's encounter table when Hiker Anthony's swarm call is active). The Rooftop Sale event timing should be verified against pokecrystal's rooftop sale script — it's a limited-time event tied to specific in-game days.

goal: Implement item use mechanics — healing items (Potion/Super/Hyper/Max/Full Restore), status cure items, Revive, Sacred Ash
abstract_tasks: Implement RestoreHPEffect dispatch: Potion (+20), Super Potion (+50), Hyper Potion (+200), Max Potion (full HP), Fresh Water (+50), Soda Pop (+60), Lemonade (+80), Moomoo Milk (+100), RageCandyBar (+20), Berry (+10), Gold Berry (+30), Berry Juice (+20)
Implement HP item validation checks: target Pokemon must not be fainted (HP > 0) and must not be at full HP; display 'It won't have any effect' if either condition fails
Implement FullRestoreEffect: restore to full HP AND clear all non-volatile status (sleep/freeze/para/burn/poison), cannot be used on fainted Pokemon

Implement StatusHealingEffect dispatch: Antidote (poison only), Burn Heal (burn only), Ice Heal (freeze only), Awakening (sleep only), Parlyz Heal (paralysis only), Full Heal (any status); each must validate that the target has the matching status condition before use

Implement ReviveEffect: Revive restores fainted Pokemon to 50% max HP (rounded down), Max Revive restores to full HP, Revival Herb restores to full HP with -happiness penalty; target must be fainted (HP == 0)
Implement SacredAshEffect: revives ALL fainted party Pokemon to full HP in a single use; requires at least one fainted Pokemon in party; consumed on use
Implement bitter medicine happiness penalties: EnergyPowder -5 (friendship < 200) or -10 (friendship >= 200), Energy Root -10 or -15, Heal Powder -5, Revival Herb -10 or -15

source_data.item_use_mechanics.md:
## HP Restoration Items
| Item | HP Restored |
|------|-------------|
| Potion | 20 |
| Super Potion | 50 |
| Hyper Potion | 200 |
| Max Potion | Full HP |
| Full Restore | Full HP + cure all status |
| Fresh Water | 50 |
| Soda Pop | 60 |
| Lemonade | 80 |
| Moomoo Milk | 100 |
| RageCandyBar | 20 |
| Berry | 10 |
| Gold Berry | 30 |
| Berry Juice | 20 |
| Energy Root | 200 (happiness penalty) |
| Energypowder | 50 (happiness penalty) |

## Status Healing Items
| Item | Cures |
|------|-------|
| Antidote | Poison |
| Burn Heal | Burn |
| Ice Heal | Freeze |
| Awakening | Sleep |
| Parlyz Heal | Paralysis |
| Full Heal | All status conditions |
| Full Restore | All status + full HP |
| PsnCureBerry | Poison (auto-use in battle as held item) |
| PrzCureBerry | Paralysis (auto-use) |
| Burnt Berry | Freeze (auto-use) |
| Ice Berry | Burn (auto-use) -- NOTE: counterintuitive name |
| Mint Berry | Sleep (auto-use) |
| MiracleBerry | Any status (auto-use) |
| Bitter Berry | Confusion |

## Revive Items
| Item | Effect |
|------|--------|
| Revive | Restores a fainted Pokemon to 50% max HP |
| Max Revive | Restores a fainted Pokemon to 100% max HP |
| Revival Herb | Restores a fainted Pokemon to full HP (happiness penalty) |
| Sacred Ash | Revives ALL fainted party Pokemon to full HP |

## Item Use Validation
Items check various conditions before use:
- HP healing items: Pokemon must not be at full HP and must not be fainted
- Status heals: Pokemon must have the relevant status condition
- Revives: Pokemon must be fainted (HP = 0)

unstructured_notes: Item use in and out of battle uses the same effect functions but the context differs: in battle, the animation plays and the turn is consumed; in the overworld menu, the item is applied directly. HP healing items cap at max HP without error — applying a Potion to a Pokemon at max HP - 1 gives exactly 1 HP, not 20 (capped at max). Sacred Ash is extremely rare (only found at Tin Tower and from some trades) and its effect on ALL fainted Pokemon is unique. The Ice Berry (confusingly named) actually cures Burn — not Freeze. The Burnt Berry cures Freeze. These counterintuitive names must be implemented exactly as in the source data. Bitter medicine items apply happiness penalties immediately on use from the menu.

unstructured_notes: Guard Spec's 5-turn mist effect is stored in wMistCount for the player's side. Unlike Safeguard (which blocks status conditions), Guard Spec blocks STAT REDUCTION only — it does not block status moves that do not reduce stats. The interactions are: Guard Spec blocks opponent using Growl/Leer/etc., but does not block Toxic, Thunder Wave, or Confuse Ray. This is a key distinction from Safeguard. X Special only boosts Special Attack in Gen 2 (not Sp. Def as well, since they split). The player can use X items against their own Pokemon in any order during the battle turn, and the item use consumes their turn just like selecting a move. Items used by trainers (AI) come from their pre-defined item slots, not the player's bag.

## Scenario 14: Focus Energy + Critical Hit Interaction
Setup: Pokemon uses Focus Energy.
Outcome: In Gen 2, Focus Energy correctly adds +1 to the critical hit stage. With Focus Energy and a high-crit move (like Slash), the Pokemon reaches stage 3 (1/3 crit rate). Maximum stage 4+ gives 1/2 crit rate.
ASM: BattleCommand_Critical checks SUBSTATUS_FOCUS_ENERGY and adds 1 to the crit stage if set.

unstructured_notes: The QA should specifically test the counterintuitive berry-status pairings against the source ASM. The heal_status.asm file is the definitive reference: Ice Berry heals Burn, Burnt Berry heals Freeze. These names were likely swapped intentionally or accidentally in the original game and must be faithfully replicated. Guard Spec's 5-turn duration uses wMistCount — the QA should confirm the counter decrements at end-of-turn and the effect expires exactly after 5 turns (turn of use counts as turn 1, expires at the start of turn 6). Also confirm Guard Spec does NOT prevent Toxic or Thunder Wave (status conditions), only stat reduction moves like Growl, Screech, Charm, Cotton Spore, etc.

source_data.item_use_mechanics.md:
## Poke Ball Catch Formula

Step 1: Get base catch rate of species (wEnemyMonCatchRate = 0-255)
Step 2: Apply ball-specific multiplier from BallMultiplierFunctionTable
Step 3: HP factor: adjusted_rate = (catch_rate * (MaxHP_x2 - CurHP_x3)) / MaxHP_x2
Step 4: Status bonus — If Frozen or Asleep: add 10 (BUG: BRN/PSN/PAR add nothing due to AND with FRZ|SLP mask)
Step 5: Random byte 0-255; if random < adjusted_rate: caught!
Step 6: Master Ball always catches.

Ball modifiers: Master=always, Ultra=2x, Great=1.5x, PokeBall=1x, Level=4x/2x/1x by level ratio (off-by-one bug), Lure=3x if fishing, Moon=BUG never works, Friend=no bonus+happiness200, Fast=BUG only Grimer/Tangela/Mr.Mime, Heavy=additive +/-20..+40 by weight, Love=BUG 8x same gender, Park=standard

Known bugs:
1. BRN/PSN/PAR do not affect catch rate
2. HELD_CATCH_CHANCE has no effect
3. Overflow for Pokemon with max HP > 341
4. Catching Transformed Pokemon always catches Ditto
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Catch Rate Formula

CatchValue = (CatchRate * BallModifier * (MaxHP * 3 - CurrentHP * 2)) / (MaxHP * 3)
StatusBonus = +10 if Frozen/Asleep, +0 otherwise (BUG: BRN/PSN/PAR should add +5 but don't)
FinalCatchValue = CatchValue + StatusBonus
Random number 0-255 generated. If random < FinalCatchValue, caught.

Ball Multipliers:
Master Ball: Always catches
Ultra Ball: 2x
Great Ball: 1.5x
Poke Ball: 1x
Level Ball: 8x if player level/4 > enemy; 4x if /2 >; 2x if >
Lure Ball: 3x if fishing encounter
Moon Ball: BUG checks HELD_BURN_HEAL instead of HELD_MOON_STONE — never works
Love Ball: BUG 8x when SAME gender instead of opposite
Fast Ball: BUG only works for Grimer, Tangela, Mr. Mime
Heavy Ball: -20/<102.4kg, +0/<204.8kg, +20/<307.2kg, +30/<409.6kg, +40/>=409.6kg
Friend Ball: No bonus, sets happiness to 200

Source: engine/items/item_effects.asm lines 230-1060

## Apricorn-to-Ball Mappings
Red Apricorn: Level Ball — Better catch rate if your mon is higher level
Blue Apricorn: Lure Ball — Better catch rate on fished Pokemon
Yellow Apricorn: Moon Ball — Better catch rate on Moon Stone evolvers (BUG: never works)
Green Apricorn: Friend Ball — Caught Pokemon starts with high friendship
White Apricorn: Fast Ball — Better catch rate on fast/fleeing Pokemon (BUG)
Black Apricorn: Heavy Ball — Better catch rate on heavy Pokemon
Pink Apricorn: Love Ball — Better catch rate on opposite gender (BUG: same gender)

unstructured_notes: This sprint covers the catch formula and all ball mechanics. All bugs in the original Crystal code must be faithfully replicated. The Moon Ball bug (checking HELD_BURN_HEAL instead of HELD_MOON_STONE) means the Moon Ball NEVER provides its intended bonus — it behaves like a regular Poke Ball. The Love Ball bug means it boosts when SAME gender is matched — the opposite of its description. Fast Ball only works for Grimer, Tangela, and Mr. Mime due to wrong flee-rate comparison. The Friend Ball is unique: no catch rate bonus but sets the caught Pokemon's happiness to 200 (FRIEND_BALL_HAPPINESS constant). The Park Ball is used only in Bug Catching Contest with standard catch rates. Heavy Ball uses additive modifiers (not multiplicative) which can go negative for very light Pokemon. All bugs must be implemented to match pokecrystal behavior exactly.

unstructured_notes: Held items in Gen 2 have two trigger points: passive damage/stat bonuses that apply during damage calculation, and reactive items that trigger from held-item checks during end-of-turn processing or at status application time. The 10% type boost items are straightforward multipliers in the damage formula. Leftovers restores floor(max_HP/16) per turn at end of residual processing. Auto-berries are consumed on use. Quick Claw is notable: it has a 60% chance (not a full guarantee) of allowing the holder to move first in a turn, bypassing normal speed comparison (but not priority moves). BrightPowder lowers foe accuracy by 20 points in the accuracy check. King's Rock applies its flinch chance only to moves that do not already have flinch built in. Focus Band's 30% figure matches all_items.md but the item_details.md says 'may prevent fainting' — match the 10% figure from formulas if there's a discrepancy; always check pokecrystal source for exact probabilities. Lucky Egg stacks with Traded and Trainer multipliers (1.5x each) for up to 3.375x total.

source_data.evolution_mechanics.md:
## Evolution Methods in Gen 2

### EVOLVE_ITEM -- Evolutionary Stone
condition: wForceEvolution is set AND item matches
Stone evolutions triggered by using stone from bag. Do NOT trigger on level-up.

Stones:
Fire Stone: Vulpix->Ninetales, Growlithe->Arcanine, Eevee->Flareon
Water Stone: Poliwhirl->Poliwrath, Shellder->Cloyster, Staryu->Starmie, Eevee->Vaporeon
Thunder Stone: Pikachu->Raichu, Eevee->Jolteon
Leaf Stone: Gloom->Vileplume, Weepinbell->Victreebel, Exeggcute->Exeggutor
Moon Stone: Nidorina->Nidoqueen, Nidorino->Nidoking, Clefairy->Clefable, Jigglypuff->Wigglytuff
Sun Stone: Gloom->Bellossom, Sunkern->Sunflora

### EVOLVE_TRADE -- Trade Evolution
Basic trade: Kadabra->Alakazam, Machoke->Machamp, Graveler->Golem, Haunter->Gengar
Trade with item:
Poliwhirl + King's Rock -> Politoed
Slowpoke + King's Rock -> Slowking
Onix + Metal Coat -> Steelix
Scyther + Metal Coat -> Scizor
Seadra + Dragon Scale -> Kingdra
Porygon + Up-Grade -> Porygon2
Item is consumed during evolution.

### Everstone
Prevents level-up, happiness, stat evolutions. Does NOT prevent stone or trade evolutions.
Implementation: call IsMonHoldingEverstone; jp z, .dont_evolve

Branching evolution chains (relevant):
ODDISH->GLOOM: Level 21 -> Leaf Stone->VILEPLUME or Sun Stone->BELLOSSOM
POLIWAG->POLIWHIRL: Level 25 -> Water Stone->POLIWRATH or Trade+Kings Rock->POLITOED
SLOWPOKE: Level 37->SLOWBRO or Trade+Kings Rock->SLOWKING
EEVEE: Thunderstone->JOLTEON, Water Stone->VAPOREON, Fire Stone->FLAREON, Happiness day->ESPEON, Happiness night->UMBREON

SCYTHER: Trade+Metal Coat->SCIZOR
SEADRA: Trade+Dragon Scale->KINGDRA

unstructured_notes: Stone evolutions are the simplest evolution type to implement — they're triggered by an explicit player action (use item from bag), not by level-up logic. The wForceEvolution flag signals to EvolvePokemon that a stone was used. The stone is always consumed whether or not the Pokemon was eligible to evolve. IMPORTANT: Everstone does NOT block stone evolutions — this is a key Crystal mechanic. Trade evolution items require integration with the link trade system (Sprint 223), but the data structures (which species+item combinations trigger which evolutions) should be defined now. Sun Stone is the only Gen-2-exclusive stone; it evolves Gloom (into Bellossom, as an alternative to Leaf Stone's Vileplume) and Sunkern (into Sunflora). Both Gloom branches should share the same Gloom species in the evolution chain data, branching based on item used.

source_data.item_use_mechanics.md:
## TM/HM System (engine/items/tmhm.asm)
- TMs are consumed on use
- HMs are not consumed (infinite use)
- HM moves cannot be forgotten through normal means (ForgetMove checks IsHMMove)
- Compatibility check: each species has a TM/HM learnability bitfield

## Pack/Bag System
Pockets:
1. Items -- General items
2. Key Items -- Quest items
3. Balls -- All Poke Ball variants
4. TMs/HMs -- Technical/Hidden Machines
Maximum 20 different items per pocket (except TMs/HMs)
Maximum 99 of any single item

unstructured_notes: The TM/HM system requires two separate data structures: (1) the TM/HM item definitions (which move each TM/HM teaches), and (2) the per-species compatibility bitfields stored in each species' base stats entry. The TMs pocket stores all 57 TM/HM items without a cap of 20 (it holds all of them). Move Deleter is a Gen 2 Crystal exclusive NPC in Blackthorn City who can delete HM moves for free — this NPC is implemented in map scripts but the ForgetMove routine must have a 'bypass HM restriction' flag for when it's called from the Move Deleter. Game Corner TM tutors (Flamethrower, Thunderbolt, Ice Beam) in Goldenrod are NOT TMs but behave like free move tutors — they check compatibility differently. These are Crystal-exclusive and cost Game Corner coins (10,000 each).

source_data.pokemon_management.md:
## Bill's PC (Box System)
Overview: Accessed from any Pokemon Center PC. Functions: Deposit, Withdraw, Move, Change Box.
Box Structure: 14 boxes total (NUM_BOXES). Each box holds up to 20 Pokemon (MONS_PER_BOX). Current box: wCurBox. Box data stored in SRAM.

Deposit:
1. Party list displayed (names, levels, gender)
2. Cannot deposit if only 1 Pokemon in party
3. Cannot deposit eggs in certain situations
4. Mail: prompt to remove first
5. Move from party to current box

Withdraw:
1. Box contents displayed
2. Cannot withdraw if party is full (6 Pokemon)
3. Move from box to party

Move Pokemon Without Mail: move between boxes without manual deposit/withdraw. Saves during box switch.

Box Change: ChangeBoxSaveGame in save.asm. 'SAVING... DON'T TURN OFF THE POWER' displayed.

Implementation: BillsPC_GetSelectedPokemonSpecies, CopyBoxmonSpecies, PCMonInfo, wBillsPC_LoadedBox

## Mail System
9 mail types: Flower Mail, Surf Mail, LiteBlue Mail, Portrait Mail, Lovely Mail, Eon Mail, Morph Mail, BlueSky Mail, Music Mail, Mirage Mail
Attaching mail: requires mail item in bag
Removing mail: returns mail item to bag, clears held item
Sending to PC: mail must be removed first
Trading: mail transfers with Pokemon
source_data.memory_map.md:
### Box Data (SRAM)
Box data stored in SRAM (battery-backed). wCurBox tracks which box is currently displayed.

#### Active Battle State / Party
wBattleMon: Player's active Pokemon (battle_struct)
Party stored in WRAM (wPartyMon1..wPartyMon6)
PC boxes in SRAM

unstructured_notes: The box system is primarily a data management system — no battle logic required. The key constraint is that box switches trigger a save because the game only keeps ONE box loaded in WRAM at a time (the current box); others are in SRAM. Switching boxes copies the current box back to SRAM, saves the game, then loads the new box from SRAM to WRAM. This design means changing boxes is permanent (you can't 'undo' a box change). The mail system interacts with the PC: Pokemon holding mail show a special prompt, and the mail must be detached before the Pokemon can be deposited. Mail items go back to the player's bag when detached. PCMonInfo shows a summary screen for browsing Pokemon in boxes without withdrawing them. Implement wBillsPC_LoadedBox to track which box data is currently in WRAM.

source_data.pokemon_management.md:
## Party Management

Switch Party Mons (switchpartymons.asm):
Reorder Pokemon in party via menu. Swaps all data: species, struct, nickname, OT name, mail.

Party Menu (party_menu.asm):
Displays: species icon, nickname, level, HP bar, status.
Options per Pokemon: Stats, Switch, Item, Cancel.
In battle: additional Fight option.

Mon Menu: context menu when selecting a party Pokemon.
Field options: Stats, Switch, Item (Give/Take), Move (HM use), Cancel.

## Health System
HealParty: iterates through all party Pokemon, skips eggs, calls HealPartyMon.
HealPartyMon: clears status byte, copies MaxHP to CurrentHP, calls RestoreAllPP.
HP Bar: pixels = (currentHP * HP_BAR_LENGTH_PX) / maxHP
If maxHP > 255: both values divided by 4.
Minimum 1 pixel if HP > 0.

## Stats Screen (stats_screen.asm)
Page 1: Pokemon name, level, gender, HP (current/max), Attack, Defense, Speed, Sp.Atk, Sp.Def, status, item held, type(s).
Page 2: All 4 moves with current PP / max PP, move types.
Page 3: OT Name and ID, caught location and time.

## Move Learning (learn.asm)
LearnMove:
1. Check if any of 4 move slots empty
2. If empty: learn directly
3. If all 4 full: enter ForgetMove routine
ForgetMove:
1. 'Which move should be forgotten?' prompt
2. Display all 4 current moves
3. IsHMMove called -- HM moves cannot be forgotten
4. HM: 'HM moves cant be forgotten' message
5. Cancel: 'Stop learning [move]?' confirmation
6. Confirmed cancel: 'Did not learn [move]'

## Nickname System
correct_nick_errors.asm: scans for invalid characters, replaces with valid chars, ensures '@' terminator.

unstructured_notes: The party menu is one of the most-used UI elements in the game. HP bar color changes: green when HP > 50% of max, yellow when HP 20-50%, red when HP < 20% — these thresholds are important for visual feedback. The stats screen's 3 pages are navigated with left/right on the D-pad. Page 3 caught data was stored when the Pokemon was first obtained (Sprint 178 caught_data system). ForgetMove has a crucial interaction: if in battle, replacing a move updates the battle move array (wBattleMonMoves) and PP array — the in-battle case must be handled separately from the field case. The HM check is a simple lookup against an HM move list constant; the Move Deleter bypasses this check. Party switching (reordering) must swap the entire 48-byte Pokemon struct plus all associated strings (nickname 11 bytes, OT name 11 bytes).

source_data.pokemon_management.md:
Box Structure: 14 boxes, 20 Pokemon each. wCurBox tracks current. Data in SRAM.
Deposit: cannot deposit if only 1 Pokemon in party. Cannot deposit eggs in certain situations. Mail: prompt to remove.
Withdraw: cannot withdraw if party full (6 Pokemon).
Box Change: ChangeBoxSaveGame triggered. 'SAVING... DON'T TURN OFF THE POWER' displayed.

ForgetMove: IsHMMove check -- HM moves CANNOT be forgotten. 'HM moves can't be forgotten' message.

unstructured_notes: This QA covers data integrity for the most fundamental player-facing Pokemon storage system. Edge cases to explicitly test: (1) A party of 1 Pokemon attempting to deposit the last one. (2) A box that is completely full (20/20) — the deposit attempt should redirect to a different box or show 'Box is full'. (3) A Pokemon with a mail item — verify the mail goes back to the Items pocket (not lost). (4) HP bar rendering: a Pokemon at exactly 50% HP should show yellow (the threshold is >, not >=), and at exactly 20% HP should show red. (5) The ForgetMove routine when called from in-battle vs from field — ensure in-battle case updates wBattleMonMoves array. Also verify that correct_nick_errors.asm does not corrupt valid nicknames (test a Pokemon whose nickname contains all alphanumeric characters).

goal: Implement evolution system — all evolution methods (level, stone, trade, happiness day/night, stat-based), evolution animation, stat recalculation
abstract_tasks: Implement level-up evolution check (EVOLVE_LEVEL): wTempMonLevel >= required_level AND not holding Everstone

Implement happiness evolution check (EVOLVE_HAPPINESS): level-up occurred AND happiness >= 220; with three sub-variants: anytime (Golbat->Crobat, Chansey->Blissey, Pichu->Pikachu, Cleffa->Clefairy, Igglybuff->Jigglypuff, Togepi->Togetic), day-only (Eevee->Espeon, hours 4-17), night-only (Eevee->Umbreon, hours 18-3)
Implement stat-based evolution check (EVOLVE_STAT): Tyrogue at level 20 — Attack > Defense gives Hitmonlee, Attack < Defense gives Hitmonchan, Attack = Defense gives Hitmontop

Implement full EvolvePokemon routine: play evolution animation, change species, recalculate all stats with new base stats preserving DVs and StatExp, check for new level-up moves at current level and offer to learn them, update Pokedex for evolved species
Implement evolution cancellation: B button press during animation cancels evolution (kept current form, can evolve again at next trigger)
Implement Everstone check: call IsMonHoldingEverstone before level/happiness/stat evolutions; does NOT check for stone/trade evolutions

source_data.evolution_mechanics.md:
## Evolution Methods

1. EVOLVE_LEVEL: wTempMonLevel >= required_level. Checked every level-up. Everstone blocks.
2. EVOLVE_ITEM: wForceEvolution set AND item matches. Triggered by using stone from bag. Everstone does NOT block.
3. EVOLVE_TRADE: Trading is occurring. Everstone does NOT block.
4. EVOLVE_HAPPINESS: Level-up occurred AND happiness >= 220.
   Sub-variants:
   Day only (4:00AM-7:59PM): Eevee->Espeon
   Night only (8:00PM-3:59AM): Eevee->Umbreon
   Anytime: Golbat->Crobat, Chansey->Blissey, Pichu->Pikachu, Cleffa->Clefairy, Igglybuff->Jigglypuff, Togepi->Togetic
   Threshold: >= 220 out of 255 maximum.
5. EVOLVE_STAT: Tyrogue only at level 20.
   Atk > Def: Hitmonlee
   Atk < Def: Hitmonchan
   Atk = Def: Hitmontop

## Evolution Process
1. Evolution animation plays
2. Species changed to evolved form
3. Stats recalculated with new base stats (DVs and StatExp preserved)
4. Moves checked: if evolved species learns moves at current level, offer to teach
5. Pokedex updated (new species caught flag set)
6. Type updated to evolved form's types

## Cancelled Evolution
- B button during animation: evolution cancelled, Pokemon keeps current form
- Can trigger again at next applicable event
- NOT permanent prevention

## Everstone
- Prevents level-up, happiness, stat-based evolutions
- Does NOT prevent stone or trade evolutions
- Implementation: call IsMonHoldingEverstone; jp z, .dont_evolve
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Happiness System
Evolution threshold (Espeon, Umbreon, Blissey): >= 220
Frustration: power = (255 - happiness) * 10 / 25
Return: power = happiness * 10 / 25

## Stat Calculation
HP = ((Base + DV) * 2 + floor(sqrt(StatExp)) / 4) * Level / 100 + Level + 10
Other Stats = ((Base + DV) * 2 + floor(sqrt(StatExp)) / 4) * Level / 100 + 5
DVs and StatExp preserved on evolution; only base stats change.

unstructured_notes: The evolution system is a complex routine called from multiple places: after gaining a level, after using a stone, after a trade, and at specific event triggers. The EVOLVE_HAPPINESS variants require reading the current time of day (wTimeOfDay) and comparing against MORN/DAY (0,1) for Espeon and NITE (2) for Umbreon. The happiness threshold of 220 must be >= (not >). The evolution animation was already established in Phase 3 but the stat recalculation must be re-run after species change because the base stats table values change. Post-evolution move learning uses the same LearnMove/ForgetMove routines as level-up. The Pokedex update for the evolved species should mark it as 'caught' (not just 'seen'), matching what happens when you obtain one in the wild. Baby Pokemon (Pichu, Cleffa, etc.) all evolve via happiness anytime — none require time-of-day except Eevee.

goal: Implement breeding system — Day Care, egg group compatibility, egg production (step counter), DV inheritance, shiny breeding, egg moves
abstract_tasks: Implement Day Care deposit/withdraw: store up to 2 Pokemon, gain 1 EXP per step while stored, cost to retrieve = 100 + (100 * levels_gained), auto-learn level-up moves (delete oldest if full)

Implement egg group compatibility check: same egg group required AND opposite genders (or Ditto), Ditto can breed with any non-Undiscoverable Pokemon, genderless Pokemon can only breed with Ditto, baby/legendary Pokemon in Undiscoverable cannot breed

Implement Day Care Man dialogue system with 4 compatibility levels: incompatible ('prefer to play with other Pokemon', 0% egg chance), same OT different species (~20% per 256 steps), different OT different species (~50%), different OT same species (~70%)
Implement egg production step counter: every 256 steps check compatibility, generate random value against compatibility threshold, produce egg (wStepsToEgg system)

Implement DV inheritance for eggs: Defense DV inherited from opposite-gender parent (or Ditto), Special DV semi-inherited (50% as-is, 50% ±8 wrap), Attack DV random 0-15, Speed DV random 0-15, HP DV derived
Implement egg move inheritance: father's moves that match the offspring's egg move list are inherited; TM/HM moves known by father that offspring can learn via TM/HM are also inherited
Implement Odd Egg (Crystal-exclusive): Day Care Man gives one Odd Egg during story; hatches into one of 7 baby species with 14% overall shiny chance; all have Dizzy Punch as egg move

source_data.breeding_mechanics.md:
## Breeding Compatibility
15 Egg Groups. Ditto breeds with any non-Undiscoverable. Genderless: only Ditto. Baby/Legendary: Undiscoverable.

Compatibility levels (per 256 steps):
Incompatible: 0%
Diff species + diff OT: ~50.2%
Same species + diff OT: ~70.3%
Diff species + same OT: ~19.9%

## DV Inheritance
Defense DV: inherited from opposite-gender parent (or Ditto)
Special DV: 50% as-is from parent, 50% ±8 wrap
Attack DV: random 0-15
Speed DV: random 0-15
HP DV: derived = (Atk & 1)*8 + (Def & 1)*4 + (Spd & 1)*2 + (Spc & 1)

## Egg Moves
Father passes moves that: (1) offspring's species can learn as Egg Move AND (2) father currently knows the move.
TM/HM moves: if father knows TM/HM move that baby can learn via TM/HM, baby inherits it.

## Shiny Breeding
Shiny Ditto (Def=10, Spd=10, Spc=10, Atk=2/3/6/7/10/11/14/15) bred with compatible Pokemon:
Defense DV: 10 (guaranteed) — matches shiny requirement
Special DV: 50% chance of 10 — matches shiny requirement
Attack DV: 8/16 chance of shiny-compatible value
Speed DV: 1/16 chance of 10
Combined probability: ~1/64 (vs normal 1/8192)

## Egg Hatching
Each species has egg cycles. Each cycle = 256 steps. Hatch at level 5.
Common counts: Magikarp=5 cycles (1280 steps), most common=10 cycles (2560), starters=20 cycles (5120), Chansey/Eevee=25 cycles (6400)

## Odd Egg (Crystal Only)
7 species with ~14% overall shiny chance.
All have Dizzy Punch as egg move.
source_data.event_data_tables.md:
## Odd Egg (Crystal Only)
Hatches into one of 7 species. Each has normal version (all 0 DVs) and shiny version (DVs 2/10/10/10). All hatch at level 5 with Dizzy Punch.
Pichu: normal 8%, shiny 1%
Cleffa: normal 16%, shiny 3%
Igglybuff: normal 16%, shiny 3%
Smoochum: normal 14%, shiny 2%
Magby: normal 10%, shiny 2%
Elekid: normal 12%, shiny 2%
Tyrogue: normal 10%, shiny 1%
Overall shiny chance: 14%

unstructured_notes: The breeding system requires careful interaction with the step counter system (established in Sprint 35 / residual phase). The Day Care EXP gain (1 per step) uses flat experience — not subject to growth rate formula — so a Pokemon could be at a 'wrong' experience value that matches a different level. Level recalculation from EXP happens on retrieval. Auto-move-learning in Day Care deletes the OLDEST move (slot 1) if all 4 are full — no player input — this is different from the field behavior. The species of the egg is always the LOWEST evolution of the mother (or non-Ditto parent). Baby forms (Pichu, not Pikachu) are the lowest evolution and must be produced. The Odd Egg is a special scripted egg given by the Day Care Man once during the story (not a natural breeding product) and uses a different data table with pre-set DVs. Shiny breeding via Shiny Ditto is an emergent property of the DV inheritance system, not a special coded path.

Verify DV inheritance in breeding: breed shiny Ditto with Vulpix — Defense DV of offspring must equal Ditto's Defense DV (10), Special DV must be 10 or 2 (the two semi-inherited options), Attack/Speed must be independently random

source_data.event_data_tables.md:
## NPC Trades
Krabby -> Voltorb (VOLTY), OT: TIM, ID: 29189, Item: PRZCureBerry
Dragonair -> Dodrio (DORIS), OT: EMY, ID: 00283, Item: Smoke Ball, Female only required

unstructured_notes: Key QA scenarios for this sprint: (1) Tyrogue with equal Attack and Defense evolving into Hitmontop is the rarest case and historically most bug-prone. (2) The happiness threshold is >= 220, not > 220 — test with exactly 220. (3) Eevee evolution time-of-day: the time boundary for Espeon is 04:00-17:59 (Morning + Day), and Umbreon is 18:00-03:59 (Night). Test at the exact boundary hours (3:59 = night, 4:00 = morning). (4) For DV inheritance testing, run 200 breeding pairs and statistically verify Defense DV distribution (should always equal parent Ditto's Def DV), Special DV distribution (should be bimodal at parent's value and parent±8). (5) Verify the egg_moves.asm data structure correctly encodes which moves are Egg Moves for each species — this was supposed to be populated in Sprint 10 but verify it's correct for at least Skarmory (Drill Peck), Houndour (Fire Spin), and Pichu (ThunderShock at birth is a level-up move, not an egg move).

source_data.time_system.md:
## Time-of-Day Periods
Morning: 04:00-09:59 (MORN_HOUR=4, MORN_F)
Day: 10:00-17:59 (DAY_HOUR=10, DAY_F)
Night: 18:00-03:59 (NITE_HOUR=18, NITE_F)

TimesOfDay table checked sequentially:
1. hour < 4: Night
2. hour < 10: Morning
3. hour < 18: Day
4. hour < 24: Night
5. Fallback: Morning

## Day-of-Week Events
Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday: Bug-Catching Contest (9AM-6PM)
Friday: Lapras in Union Cave B2F (Lv20, respawns every Friday)
Friday: Lucky Number Show
Monday/Wednesday/Friday: Haircut older brother (Goldenrod Underground)
Saturday: Goldenrod Dept Store Rooftop Sale
Sunday: Trainer Rematches (many phone trainers)

## Time-Dependent Encounters
Morning-only: Ledyba, Pidgey (route-specific)
Night-only: Hoothoot, Spinarak, Gastly, Murkrow
Day-only: Natu, Sunkern
Morning/Day use one table, Night uses a different table.

## Daily Reset
CheckDailyResetTimer fires every 24 hours:
Clears wDailyFlags1, wDailyFlags2, wSwarmFlags
Clears wDailyRematchFlags (4 bytes)
Clears wDailyPhoneItemFlags (4 bytes)
Clears wDailyPhoneTimeOfDayFlags (4 bytes)
Decrements wKenjiBreakTimer (random 3-6 day cooldown)

## DST
Mom can toggle. SetClockForward: +1 hour to wStartHour (increment wStartDay if rollover). SetClockBack: -1 hour (decrement if underflow). Cannot toggle within 1 hour of midnight.
source_data.rtc_engine.md:
## RTC Hardware
Registers: RAMB_RTC_S (seconds), RAMB_RTC_M (minutes), RAMB_RTC_H (hours), RAMB_RTC_DL (day low), RAMB_RTC_DH (day high + flags).
RTC DH Flags: Bit0=day bit8, Bit6=halt, Bit7=overflow past 511 days.

GetTimeOfDay: Hours 4-9=MORN, 10-17=DAY, 18-3=NITE
Constants: MORN_HOUR=4, DAY_HOUR=10, NITE_HOUR=18, MAX_HOUR=24

## Clock Initialization (timeset.asm)
InitClock: Ask hour (0-23, default 10AM). D-pad up/down. Confirm Yes/No. Ask minutes. Confirm. Oak reacts.
SetDayOfWeek: 7 days (0=Sunday..6=Saturday). D-pad up/down. Confirm.

## Key RAM Variables
wCurDay: current day 0-6 (Sun-Sat)
hHours: current hour 0-23 (HRAM)
hMinutes: current minute 0-59 (HRAM)
wTimeOfDay: 0=morning, 1=day, 2=night
wStartDay/Hour/Minute/Second: offset from RTC to game time
wDST: DST flag (bit 7)
wDailyResetTimer: countdown for daily event reset

unstructured_notes: The time system is the core of Crystal's uniqueness over Gold/Silver. In a Rust engine, the RTC hardware must be replaced with a system clock abstraction that provides the same interface. The key is that wStartDay/Hour/Minute/Second are OFFSETS stored in save data that translate the current real time to game time — this allows the player to set their own clock offset without requiring a real hardware RTC. The time-of-day period lookup uses the sequential table check (not a simple range check), which means the fallback to Morning at the end is a real code path if hours is somehow invalid. Palette changes happen when the game checks time at map transitions or periodically — they are not animated transitions. Day-of-week is derived via wCurDay % 7, where wCurDay is the raw day counter that wraps every 140 days (20 weeks). The daily reset timer fires once per 24 hours to clear daily flags — this must happen reliably even if the player is mid-session.

Implement CheckDailyResetTimer that clears wDailyFlags1/2, wSwarmFlags, wDailyRematchFlags(4 bytes), wDailyPhoneItemFlags(4 bytes), wDailyPhoneTimeOfDayFlags(4 bytes) every 24 real hours

Implement day-of-week gated events: Bug Contest availability (Tue/Thu/Sat, 9AM–6PM), Lapras in Union Cave B2F (every Friday, Lv20), Lucky Number Show weekly Friday reset via RestartLuckyNumberCountdown

source_data.time_system.md:
## Day-of-Week System

Days are determined by: `GetWeekday: wCurDay % 7`

| Value | Day |
|-------|-----|
| 0 | Sunday |
| 1 | Monday |
| 2 | Tuesday |
| 3 | Wednesday |
| 4 | Thursday |
| 5 | Friday |
| 6 | Saturday |

Source: `home/pokedex_flags.asm` — GetWeekday

## Day-of-Week Events

### Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday: Bug-Catching Contest
- Location: National Park
- Time: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Morn/Day only)
- Rules: Given 20 Park Balls, keep one Pokemon from party, 20-minute time limit
- BUG_CONTEST_MINUTES = 20, BUG_CONTEST_SECONDS = 0
- Prizes: 1st: Sun Stone, 2nd: Everstone, 3rd: Gold Berry

### Friday: Lapras in Union Cave
- Location: Union Cave B2F (requires Surf)
- Level: 20
- Respawns: Every Friday if previously defeated/caught

### Friday: Lucky Number Show
- Location: Goldenrod Radio Tower
- Reset: Timer counts down days until next Friday using RestartLuckyNumberCountdown

### Monday/Wednesday/Friday: Haircut Brothers
- Location: Goldenrod City Underground
- Older Brother: Available Monday, lower happiness gains
- Younger Brother: Available certain days, higher max happiness gain

### Saturday: Goldenrod Dept Store Rooftop Sale
- Location: Goldenrod Department Store 6F

### Sunday: Trainer Rematches
- Many phone-registered trainers are available for rematches on Sundays

## Daily Reset Timer

The game maintains a daily reset timer (wDailyResetTimer) that clears daily flags when a new day starts.

### Flags Reset Daily
- wDailyFlags1: Fruit tree availability, Shuckle gift, fish swarm
- wDailyFlags2: Additional daily events
- wSwarmFlags: Swarm encounter flags
- wDailyRematchFlags: Trainer rematch availability (4 bytes)
- wDailyPhoneItemFlags: Phone item gift flags (4 bytes)
- wDailyPhoneTimeOfDayFlags: Phone time-of-day flags (4 bytes)

### Kenji Break Timer
- Kenji (Blackbelt, Route 45) has a special break timer
- Random 3-6 day cooldown between his availability
- Tracked separately in wKenjiBreakTimer
source_data.rtc_engine.md:
## Daily Reset Timer
CheckDailyResetTimer fires every 24 hours:
- Clears wDailyFlags1, wDailyFlags2, wSwarmFlags
- Clears wDailyRematchFlags (4 bytes)
- Clears wDailyPhoneItemFlags (4 bytes)
- Clears wDailyPhoneTimeOfDayFlags (4 bytes)
- Decrements wKenjiBreakTimer; if expired, resamples (random 3-6 days)
- Restarts 1-day countdown

## Lucky Number Show
Weekly event (every Friday):
- RestartLuckyNumberCountdown: Calculates days until next Friday
- _CheckLuckyNumberShowFlag: Returns carry when countdown expires

## Key RAM Variables
| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| wCurDay | Current day (0-6, Sunday-Saturday) |
| wDailyResetTimer | Countdown for daily event reset |
| wLuckyNumberDayTimer | Countdown for Lucky Number Show |
| wBugContestMinsRemaining | Bug Contest minutes left |
| wKenjiBreakTimer | Kenji's break between calls (3-6 days) |
source_data.event_engine.md:
## Daily Events
### Fruit Trees (fruit_trees.asm)
- Berry-producing trees throughout Johto/Kanto
- One berry per tree per day
- Berry type varies by tree location
### Haircut Brothers (haircut.asm)
- Goldenrod Tunnel, available once per day
- Older brother: available any day, lower happiness gain
- Younger brother: available specific days, higher happiness gain
- Random happiness increase amount
### Lucky Number (lucky_number.asm)
- Radio Tower lottery, once per week (reset on specific day)
- Compares drawn number against OT IDs of all party/box Pokemon
- Matching last digits = prizes (1 digit = Berry, 5 digits = Master Ball)

unstructured_notes: Day tracking is the twin system to time-of-day. The wCurDay is derived from the MBC3 RTC day counter via FixDays which normalizes to modulo 140 (20 weeks). GetWeekday then takes wCurDay % 7. Critical implementation detail: the FixDays modulo 140 means after ~20 weeks the RTC day wraps — this must be handled the same way as the original to preserve correct day-of-week across long play sessions. The Bug Contest is gated on both day-of-week AND time-of-day (morning/day only). Lapras respawn flag is stored in wDailyFlags1 and cleared every midnight reset, so the Friday Lapras comes back if it was already caught on a prior Friday. The Lucky Number Show uses wLuckyNumberDayTimer counting down to next Friday — this is separate from the general daily timer.

source_data.time_system.md:
## Time-of-Day Periods
| Period | Hours | Constant | Flag |
|--------|-------|----------|------|
| Morning | 04:00 - 09:59 | MORN_HOUR = 4 | MORN_F |
| Day | 10:00 - 17:59 | DAY_HOUR = 10 | DAY_F |
| Night | 18:00 - 03:59 | NITE_HOUR = 18 | NITE_F |

The TimesOfDay table is checked sequentially:
1. If hour < 4 (MORN_HOUR): Night
2. If hour < 10 (DAY_HOUR): Morning
3. If hour < 18 (NITE_HOUR): Day
4. If hour < 24 (MAX_HOUR): Night
5. Fallback: Morning

## Daylight Saving Time (DST)
Mom can toggle DST via phone call or in-person visit.
Protection: Cannot toggle DST if within 1 hour of midnight

## Daily Reset Timer
### Flags Reset Daily
- wDailyFlags1: Fruit tree availability, Shuckle gift, fish swarm
- wDailyFlags2: Additional daily events
- wSwarmFlags: Swarm encounter flags
- wDailyRematchFlags: Trainer rematch availability (4 bytes)
- wDailyPhoneItemFlags: Phone item gift flags (4 bytes)
- wDailyPhoneTimeOfDayFlags: Phone time-of-day flags (4 bytes)
source_data.rtc_engine.md:
## Time of Day System (GetTimeOfDay)
Maps current hour to time of day:
Hours 4-9: MORN (morning)
Hours 10-17: DAY
Hours 18-3: NITE (night)
Constants: MORN_HOUR=4, DAY_HOUR=10, NITE_HOUR=18, MAX_HOUR=24
A beta time-of-day table exists (unreferenced) using different thresholds.

## Day Fix System (rtc.asm)
### FixDays
Normalizes the RTC day counter (which can go up to 511) to game day format:
- Days are wrapped modulo 140 (20 weeks)
- Game tracks wCurDay modulo 7 for day-of-week

### ClockContinue
Called when loading a save:
1. Check RTC status flags
2. If RTC_RESET or RTC_DAYS_EXCEED_255: Clear daily timers, increment mobile adapter counters
3. If RTC_DAYS_EXCEED_139: Compare saved day with current, update if needed

source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Happiness System

### Happiness Thresholds
- Range: 0-255
- Evolution threshold (Espeon, Umbreon, Blissey): >= 220
- Frustration: power = (255 - happiness) * 10 / 25
- Return: power = happiness * 10 / 25

### Happiness Changes (per event)
Format: [happiness < 100, happiness 100-199, happiness >= 200]

| Event | Low | Mid | High |
|-------|-----|-----|------|
| Level up | +5 | +3 | +2 |
| Level up at caught location | +10 | +6 | +4 |
| Vitamin | +5 | +3 | +2 |
| X Item in battle | +1 | +1 | +0 |
| Beat Gym Leader | +3 | +2 | +1 |
| Learn a move (TM/tutor) | +1 | +1 | +0 |
| Lose to enemy | -1 | -1 | -1 |
| Faint to poison in overworld | -5 | -5 | -10 |
| Lose to much stronger enemy | -5 | -5 | -10 |
| Haircut (older brother, bad) | +1 | +1 | +1 |
| Haircut (older brother, good) | +3 | +3 | +1 |
| Haircut (older brother, great) | +5 | +5 | +2 |
| Haircut (younger brother, bad) | +1 | +1 | +1 |
| Haircut (younger brother, good) | +3 | +3 | +1 |
| Haircut (younger brother, great) | +10 | +10 | +4 |
| Energypowder/Heal Powder | -5 | -5 | -10 |
| Energy Root | -10 | -10 | -15 |
| Revival Herb | -15 | -15 | -20 |
| Daisy's Grooming | +3 | +3 | +1 |
| Walking (every other 256-step cycle) | +1 | +1 | +1* |

### Haircut Probabilities
- Older brother: 30% bad, 50% good, 20% great
- Younger brother: 60% bad, 30% good, 10% great

Source: `data/events/happiness_changes.asm`, `data/events/happiness_probabilities.asm`, `engine/events/happiness_egg.asm`

### Step Counter Mechanics
### Happiness Walking
- Every 256 steps, a counter toggles
- Every other toggle (512 steps effectively), each non-egg party member gains +1 happiness
- Capped at 255

source_data.evolution_mechanics.md:
### 4. EVOLVE_HAPPINESS -- Happiness Evolution
The Pokemon evolves when leveled up with high happiness (>= 220).
condition: wTempMonLevel increased (any level up) AND happiness >= 220

Happiness evolutions:
- Day only (4:00 AM - 7:59 PM): Eevee -> Espeon
- Night only (8:00 PM - 3:59 AM): Eevee -> Umbreon
- Anytime: Golbat -> Crobat, Chansey -> Blissey, Pichu -> Pikachu, Cleffa -> Clefairy, Igglybuff -> Jigglypuff, Togepi -> Togetic

The happiness threshold is 220 (out of 255 maximum).

unstructured_notes: Happiness is stored as an unsigned byte, clamped to 0–255 (cannot go negative, cannot exceed 255). The three-bracket system uses strict less-than comparisons: happiness < 100 (bracket 0), happiness < 200 (bracket 1), >= 200 (bracket 2). The 'level up at caught location' bonus (+10/+6/+4) requires reading the Pokemon's met-location and comparing to the current map. Walking happiness uses a toggle bit — every 256 steps the toggle flips; on the flip from 1->0, happiness applies. This means effectively every 512 steps. Friend Ball sets happiness to exactly 200 (FRIEND_BALL_HAPPINESS constant) at catch time. Trading resets happiness to the species base happiness value. Daisy's Grooming 99.6% success bug should be reproduced faithfully.

abstract_tasks: Implement Pokerus contraction: 3/65536 probability per step cycle (hRandomAdd == 0 AND hRandomSub < 3), requires STATUSFLAGS2_REACHED_GOLDENROD_F, no active Pokerus in party, target party member not previously cured (high nibble == 0)
Implement Pokerus byte format SSSS DDDD: high nibble = strain (1-15), low nibble = days remaining (1-4); generation: random byte, strain = (rand & 0x07)+1, duration = (strain & 0x03)+1

Implement Pokerus spreading: 1/3 chance per step cycle when infected mon exists, party size > 1, 50% chance direction forward/backward, stops at cured Pokemon or party end; new infection inherits strain, duration = (strain & 0x03)+1

Implement daily Pokerus tick via CheckPokerusTick: compares wTimerEventStartDay against current day, calls ApplyPokerusTick to decrement duration on each party member; when duration hits 0 strain byte preserved (cured state), cannot be re-infected
Implement Stat EV doubling effect: cured Pokemon (high nibble set, low nibble 0) retains 2x Stat Experience bonus permanently — this applies during EV gain events
Implement Shuckle Berry conversion as part of same step-cycle check: 6.25% chance (1/16), Shuckle in party holding Berry converts to Berry Juice, requires Goldenrod visited flag

source_data.event_engine.md:
## Pokerus

### Contraction (de novo infection)
Probability: 3/65536 per step cycle (~0.0046%)

Conditions for contraction:
1. Must have visited Goldenrod City at least once (STATUSFLAGS2_REACHED_GOLDENROD_F)
2. No party member currently has active Pokerus (low nibble != 0)
3. Random check: hRandomAdd must be 0 AND hRandomSub must be < 3
4. Random party member selected (0 to partyCount-1)
5. If selected mon already had Pokerus (high nibble != 0), abort

### Strain and Duration
The Pokerus byte format: `SSSS DDDD`
- High nibble (S): Strain (1-15)
- Low nibble (D): Days remaining (1-4)

Generation:
1. Generate random byte, reject 0
2. If high nibble is 0: use byte as-is for strain/duration
3. Otherwise:
   - Strain = (random & 0x07) + 1 (values 1-8)
   - Duration = (strain_value & 0x03) + 1 (values 1-4)
   - Final byte = (strain << 4) | duration

### Spreading
Conditions for spread:
1. 1/3 chance per step cycle (33% when an infected mon exists)
2. Party must have > 1 Pokemon
3. Direction: 50% chance to check forward, 50% backward in party
4. Stops at: cured Pokemon (low nibble = 0 with high nibble set), end of party

When infecting adjacent mon:
new_strain = infected_mon_strain (high nibble preserved)
new_duration = (strain & 0x03) + 1

### Curing
- Duration decremented daily at midnight
- When duration reaches 0: strain preserved, duration stays 0
- Cured Pokemon (high nibble set, low nibble 0) cannot be re-infected
- Cured Pokemon retains the 2x Stat Experience bonus permanently
source_data.time_system.md:
## Pokerus Tick
- Pokerus duration decrements by 1 for each real-world day that passes
- Checked via CheckPokerusTick: compares wTimerEventStartDay against current day
- If at least 1 day has passed, calls ApplyPokerusTick to process each party member
- Pokerus strain determines duration (1-4 days)

## Bug Contest Timer Details
(Shuckle Berry conversion in same event_engine.md step function)
- 6.25% chance per step cycle (1/16)
- If a Shuckle in party holds a Berry, converts it to Berry Juice
- Also requires Goldenrod visit flag

unstructured_notes: Pokerus is a rare passive infection that doubles all Stat Experience (EV equivalent) gained by the infected/cured Pokemon. The 3/65536 probability is implemented via two independent random bytes: hRandomAdd must equal 0 (1/256 chance) AND hRandomSub must be less than 3 (3/256 chance) — together 3/65536. The spread mechanic is directional: 50% chance to check the slot immediately before, 50% chance to check the slot immediately after. Spreading stops at already-cured Pokemon. The cured state (high nibble nonzero, low nibble zero) is a 'firewall' in the party — Pokerus cannot spread through a cured slot. Strain values 1-8 are generated but stored in nibble 1-15 range; the actual duration derived from strain is 1-4 days. Shuckle Berry->Berry Juice conversion is a separate check in the same step function, should be implemented together.

source_data.event_engine.md:
### Curing
- Duration decremented daily at midnight
- When duration reaches 0: strain preserved, duration stays 0
- Cured Pokemon (high nibble set, low nibble 0) cannot be re-infected
- Cured Pokemon retains the 2x Stat Experience bonus permanently

### Spreading
Conditions for spread:
1. 1/3 chance per step cycle (33% when an infected mon exists)
2. Party must have > 1 Pokemon
3. Direction: 50% chance to check forward, 50% backward in party
4. Stops at: cured Pokemon (low nibble = 0 with high nibble set), end of party

unstructured_notes: Key QA edge cases: happiness clamping at 0 and 255 (never underflow/overflow), bitter medicine on a Pokemon at happiness 0 should stay at 0 not wrap. Verify Return and Frustration power formulas: Return = happiness*10/25 (integer division, truncated), Frustration = (255-happiness)*10/25. At happiness=255 Return should be 102 (not 102.0). Eevee->Espeon requires morning or day time, not just any daytime hour — verify the exact pokecrystal time check (MORN_F or DAY_F flag). Pokerus: the 3/65536 rate must be tested carefully — recommend a Monte Carlo simulation over at least 1,000,000 step cycles.

source_data.game_progression.md:
### Starting Out
1. Game begins in player's bedroom (2F of Player's House)
2. Set time/day (affects encounters, events, NPC schedules)
3. Go downstairs — Mom gives Pokegear
4. Visit Professor Elm's Lab
5. Choose starter: Chikorita (Grass), Cyndaquil (Fire), or Totodile (Water), all Lv5
### Rival Battle 1
14. Silver ambushes you outside Cherrygrove (returning south)
15. Silver's team: Stolen starter Lv5 (type advantage over yours)
    - You chose Chikorita → He has Cyndaquil
    - You chose Cyndaquil → He has Totodile
    - You chose Totodile → He has Chikorita
16. Prize: $300
### Gym 3: Whitney (Normal)
55. Navigate flower maze
56. Whitney: Clefairy Lv18 (Encore, Metronome), Miltank Lv20 (Rollout, Milk Drink, Attract, Stomp)
57. Whitney cries after losing — must talk to Lass in gym to receive badge
58. Plain Badge — Strength usable outside battle; Speed boost
59. TM45 Attract
### Burned Tower
66. Explore Burned Tower — encounter Rival Battle 3 (Silver, 4 Pokemon)
67. Fall through floor to B1F
68. Legendary beasts Raikou, Entei, and Suicune flee — they begin roaming Johto
69. Ecruteak Gym is now accessible

unstructured_notes: The story event flag system is critical for correct NPC visibility and scene progression. Each scene has a numeric SCENE counter stored per map — setscene advances the local counter, setmapscene changes another map remotely. Rival's starter is always the type-advantage choice: player picks Chikorita -> rival has Cyndaquil, etc. Whitney badge requires talking to a specific Lass NPC inside the gym after the battle — if the player leaves without talking, they don't get the badge yet but the battle flag is set. The Burned Tower beast release scene must animate all three fleeing in sequence before Eusine appears — this is a multi-sprite cutscene. InitRoamMons must be called exactly once when EVENT_RELEASED_THE_BEASTS is set.

unstructured_notes: The Radio Tower arc is the most flag-dense section of the game. The Basement Key is acquired from the Underground warehouse, not the tower floors. Executive encounter order: Proton and Ariana are encountered in the Underground, Archer is the final boss who broadcasts the all-clear to Rockets after defeat. The Director is found on Radio Tower 5F, not 4F. The Red Gyarados is a BATTLETYPE_FORCESHINY encounter with hardcoded DVs making it shiny. Lance's mid-dungeon healing is a scripted call to heal player's party — must be triggered by stepping on specific tile event, not after a battle. Dragon's Den answers: if ALL 5 answers are the kind/love-focused options, Dratini gets ExtremeSpeed; otherwise ordinary Dratini.

### Oak's Lab
**Key Events:**

### Cerulean Gym
**Scene Scripts:**

  - Grunt runs down, bumps into player, says broken English, panics, flees
  - Sets EVENT_MET_ROCKET_GRUNT_AT_CERULEAN_GYM

**Hidden Items:**
- Machine Part at (3, 8) — quest-critical item for Power Plant restoration

**Vermilion City NPCs:**
- Snorlax — Level 50 wild battle (BATTLETYPE_FORCEITEM). Requires PokeFlute channel on radio (special SnorlaxAwake check). EVENT_FOUGHT_SNORLAX / EVENT_VERMILION_CITY_SNORLAX. Disappears after battle.

unstructured_notes: The Kanto post-game has many interlocking quests. Machine Part quest sequence: Grunt in Cerulean Gym -> Machine Part hidden in gym pool -> Power Plant Manager -> EXPN Card from Lavender -> PokeFlute channel unlocked -> Snorlax can be awoken. Misty cannot be battled until the Route 25 date scene fires AND the Grunt scene clears EVENT_ROUTE_25_MISTY_BOYFRIEND. The Magnet Train PASS is the other main transport option (besides S.S. Aqua) for Kanto/Johto travel. Oak's lab check for 16 badges must verify ENGINE_[BADGE]BADGE for all 16 badges. Snorlax disappears permanently after battle/defeat (one per save).

source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
## 18. Tin Tower — Legendary Encounter (Crystal)
### Tin Tower Entrance — Kimono Girls (Crystal)
- Trigger: Clear Bell + all 8 badges
- What happens: Must battle 5 Kimono Girls again at Tin Tower entrance
- After winning: Suicune appears at Tin Tower base (Lv40, static encounter)

## 19. Suicune Chase Sequence (Crystal-exclusive)
Suicune appears at fixed locations in sequence:
1. Burned Tower B1F (Ecruteak) — flees with beasts
2. Cianwood City (north rocks) — Eusine present, flees
3. Route 42 — flees
4. Vermilion City — flees
5. Route 14 — flees
6. Tin Tower (with Clear Bell) — final battle Lv40
Each sighting sets a flag: EVENT_SAW_SUICUNE_AT_[LOCATION]

unstructured_notes: The Suicune chain is strictly sequential — encounter N+1 only becomes available after encounter N's flag is set. The Tin Tower Suicune requires Clear Bell (from Radio Tower) AND completion of Kimono Girls rematch at Tin Tower entrance. Raikou and Entei use separate roam data structures from Suicune — they use the general roaming Pokemon system while Suicune has a scripted chain. Raikou and Entei Pokedex locations update when the player first sights them in any encounter (even brief sightings that result in flee). Ho-Oh is at Tin Tower roof (note: special_encounters says 7F but story_events says roof — use roof/top), Ho-Oh requires Rainbow Wing which in Crystal comes from Pewter old man after 16 badges. Note the inconsistency in Ho-Oh's moves between files: use Sacred Fire/Sunny Day/Fire Blast/Swift from story_events_by_map.md as it cites pokecrystal map scripts directly.

source_data.special_encounters.md:
## Roaming Mechanics (Raikou & Entei)
- Both are released when you fall through the Burned Tower floor
- They roam Johto routes (grass encounters only)
- Each time you enter a new route, they move to a random adjacent route
- If you enter their current route, there's a chance to encounter them in grass
- They always attempt to flee on the first turn (Roar forces you out too)
- HP, status, and catch attempts persist across encounters
- If you KO either one, they disappear permanently
- Using Fly/Teleport to a route they're on works — they don't move when you Fly
- The Pokedex map feature shows their current location after first sighting
source_data.story_events_by_map.md:
## 19. Suicune Chase Sequence (Crystal-exclusive)
Suicune appears at fixed locations in sequence:
1. Burned Tower B1F (Ecruteak) — flees with beasts
2. Cianwood City (north rocks) — Eusine present, flees
3. Route 42 — flees
4. Vermilion City — flees
5. Route 14 — flees
6. Tin Tower (with Clear Bell) — final battle Lv40
Each sighting sets a flag: EVENT_SAW_SUICUNE_AT_[LOCATION]

source_data.menu_systems.md:
## Start Menu (start_menu.asm)

### Menu Options
The start menu options vary based on game progress:

**Standard options:**
1. Pokedex (if received)
2. Pokemon (party menu)
3. Pack (bag/items)
4. [Player Name] (trainer card)
5. Save
6. Option
7. Exit

Additional options may appear based on flags (e.g., Pokegear after receiving it).

## Options Menu (options_menu.asm)

### Available Options (8 total)
| Option | Values | Default |
|--------|--------|--------|
| TEXT SPEED | Fast, Med, Slow | Med |
| BATTLE SCENE | On, Off | On |
| BATTLE STYLE | Shift, Set | Shift |
| SOUND | Mono, Stereo | Mono |
| PRINT | Lightest, Lighter, Normal, Darker, Darkest | Normal |
| MENU ACCOUNT | On, Off | Off |
| FRAME | Type 1-8 | Type 1 |
| CANCEL | (exits options) | -- |

### Text Speed
- Fast: TEXT_DELAY_FAST (1 frame per character)
- Medium: TEXT_DELAY_MED (3 frames per character)
- Slow: TEXT_DELAY_SLOW (5 frames per character)
- Stored in low nibble of wOptions

### Known Bug
- Options menu fails to clear joypad state on initialization, meaning a button press from the previous screen can inadvertently change a setting

## Save System (save.asm)
### Save Process (_SaveGameData)
1. Set wSaveFileExists = TRUE
2. Stage RTC time for save (StageRTCTimeForSave)
3. Backup Mystery Gift data (BackupMysteryGift)
4. Write primary save:
   - ValidateSave (write validation marker)
   - SaveOptions
   - SavePlayerData
   - SavePokemonData
   - SaveBox
   - SaveChecksum
5. Write backup save (identical structure)

## Main Menu (main_menu.asm)
### Menu Options
1. NEW GAME -- Start a new game
2. CONTINUE -- Load saved game (only if save exists)
3. OPTION -- Access options menu
4. MYSTERY GIFT -- Mystery Gift (if unlocked)

### Continue Screen
- Displays save file info: player name, badges, Pokedex count, play time

unstructured_notes: The start menu is event-driven: items appear/disappear based on game flags. In pokecrystal the menu is built dynamically by checking flag bytes at render time. Pokegear appears as its own menu item (not buried in items) once obtained. The MENU ACCOUNT option showing money in the start menu is a Crystal-specific feature. The Frame option changes wOptions frame bits which affect all text box borders — 8 frame styles are hardcoded in tile data. The joypad-not-cleared bug should be a direct faithful reproduction: on options menu open, the joypad input is not reset so if player pressed A to open options, the A press might immediately advance the first option.

goal: Implement bag/pack system — 4 pockets (Items, Balls, Key Items, TMs/HMs), item use from menu, registered item (Select button), toss/give
abstract_tasks: Implement 4-pocket bag structure: Items pocket (max 20 item types, 99 per stack), Key Items pocket (max 20, most are quantity 1), Balls pocket (all Poke Ball variants), TMs/HMs pocket (all 57 entries); items auto-sort into correct pockets on acquisition

Implement item use from bag menu: dispatch to ItemEffects jump table by item ID; validate use conditions before applying (HP not full for healing, correct status for status heal, battle required for X items, etc.); display failure messages for invalid use

Implement item registration via Select button: one item at a time registerable to quick-use slot; registered item displayed on HUD; pressing Select in overworld uses registered item directly; Key Items and some items can be registered

Implement toss/discard: confirmation prompt before tossing; Key Items cannot be tossed; HMs cannot be tossed; mail with message cannot be tossed while attached; deduct quantity and remove entry if quantity reaches 0

Implement give/take item to Pokemon: select item in bag, choose 'Give', select party Pokemon; item equips as held item; 'Take' removes held item back to bag; held items persist through battle and storage
Implement mart buy/sell/toss interface: buy shows price and quantity selector, checks bag capacity; sell at 50% of buy price (Key Items and HMs unsellable); quantities update in real time
Implement TM/HM system: TMs consumed on use, HMs infinite; TM compatibility check via species learnability bitfield before teaching; HM moves cannot be forgotten (ForgetMove checks IsHMMove)

source_data.item_use_mechanics.md:
## Pack/Bag System

### Pockets
The bag has multiple pockets (engine/items/pack.asm):
1. Items -- General items
2. Key Items -- Quest items
3. Balls -- All Poke Ball variants
4. TMs/HMs -- Technical/Hidden Machines

### Item Limits
- Maximum 20 different items per pocket (except TMs/HMs which hold all)
- Maximum 99 of any single item
- Items can be reordered within pockets via SELECT

### TM/HM System (engine/items/tmhm.asm)
- TMs are consumed on use
- HMs are not consumed (infinite use)
- HM moves cannot be forgotten through normal means (ForgetMove checks IsHMMove)
- Compatibility check: each species has a TM/HM learnability bitfield

## Mart System (engine/items/mart.asm, buy_sell_toss.asm)
### Buy
- Items purchased at marked-up prices
- Price displayed before purchase
- Quantity selection available
- Cannot exceed bag pocket capacity
### Sell
- Items sold at half purchase price
- Key items and HMs cannot be sold
- Mail cannot be sold while attached to Pokemon
### Toss
- Items can be discarded from bag
- Key items cannot be tossed
- Confirmation prompt before tossing

## Item Use Validation
Items check various conditions before use:
- HP healing items: Pokemon must not be at full HP and must not be fainted
- Status heals: Pokemon must have the relevant status condition
- Revives: Pokemon must be fainted (HP = 0)
- Battle items (X Items): Must be in battle
- Evolution stones: Pokemon must be compatible
- Vitamins: Stat experience must not already be maxed
- Poke Balls: Must be in wild battle (not trainer battle), party or box must have room
source_data.menu_systems.md:
## Scrolling Menu (scrolling_menu.asm)
### General Purpose Menu Engine
- Used by item lists, move lists, PC Pokemon lists
- Supports scrolling through lists longer than screen
- Handles cursor movement, scrollbar display
- Up/Down scrolls list
- A selects, B cancels

unstructured_notes: The bag pocket system in pokecrystal uses a contiguous list per pocket, not a hash map. Items are stored as (item_id, quantity) pairs. When quantity reaches 0 the entry is removed and subsequent entries shift down. The registered item is stored in wRegisteredItem RAM variable (separate from the bag). SELECT in the overworld triggers the registered item's field effect directly, bypassing the bag menu. TM/HM pocket holds all 57 TMs+HMs as entries (not like other pockets with a cap of 20) — their quantities work differently because TMs decrease by 1 on use while HMs never decrease. HM deletion restriction must be enforced in the move-forgetting menu, not just the bag.

(2) Reach 99 of one item, verify 100th cannot be added. (3) Verify the save process writes both primary and backup saves (check that both checksums are valid after save). source_data.pokegear.md:
## Cards
### 1. Clock Card (POKEGEARCARD_CLOCK)
Available: From game start
Time periods:
- Morning: 4:00 AM - 9:59 AM
- Day: 10:00 AM - 5:59 PM
- Night: 6:00 PM - 3:59 AM

### 2. Map Card (POKEGEARCARD_MAP)
Available: Given by the Guide Gent in Cherrygrove City (first visit)
Features:
- Cursor to any landmark to see its name
- Player icon shows current position (animated walking sprite)
- When on the S.S. Aqua, a special Fast Ship icon replaces the player sprite
- Shows Fly destinations (landmarks only, not routes)
- Two regions: JOHTO_REGION and KANTO_REGION, switchable with Select

### 3. Phone Card (POKEGEARCARD_PHONE)
UI: Scrollable list of up to 10 contacts. Cursor navigates with D-pad, A to call, B to close.

### 4. Radio Card (POKEGEARCARD_RADIO)
Available: Obtained from the Goldenrod Radio Tower after answering 5 quiz questions correctly
Upgrades: EXPN Card from Lavender Radio Tower manager.

## State Machine
13 states: CLOCKINIT/CLOCKJOYPAD, MAPCHECKREGION/JOHTOMAPINIT/JOHTOMAPJOYPAD, KANTOMAPINIT/KANTOMAPJOYPAD, PHONEINIT/PHONEJOYPAD/MAKEPHONECALL/FINISHPHONECALL, RADIOINIT/RADIOJOYPAD

## Fly System
### Johto Fly Points
New Bark Town, Cherrygrove City, Violet City, Azalea Town, Goldenrod City, Ecruteak City, Olivine City, Cianwood City, Mahogany Town, Lake of Rage, Blackthorn City, Silver Cave
### Kanto Fly Points
Pallet Town, Viridian City, Pewter City, Cerulean City, Vermilion City, Celadon City, Lavender Town, Saffron City, Fuchsia City, Cinnabar Island, Indigo Plateau
You can only Fly to cities/landmarks you have visited.

unstructured_notes: The Pokegear is given by Mom at game start. Clock and Phone cards are immediately available; Map card requires Guide Gent event in Cherrygrove. Radio card requires successfully answering 5 quiz questions at Goldenrod Radio Tower 1F. The state machine transitions drive the UI -- each card has its own INIT and JOYPAD state pair. Left/Right on D-pad cycles through unlocked cards only. The indicator arrow at top shows current card position. Map card shows player as an animated walking sprite; S.S. Aqua replaces this with a ship icon. Fly destination validity is per-visited-landmark, not all landmarks. Music must be restored correctly on exit depending on whether a radio station was playing.
